En: Some sort of industrial ruin? I don't recognize the name of the company. R&D used to mean Research and Development... It looks long-abandoned.
Zh: “某种工业废墟？我不认得这个公司的名字。R&D以前代表研究和开发…看起来已经废弃很久了。”

En: She? The man hears you. "I don't know who you mean, but... the building seems long-abandoned to me."
Zh: “她？”男人听到了你的话。“我不明白你什么意思，但是…在我看来，这栋大厦已经废弃很久了。”

En: You try to peer in but there are no windows on this side. Only the disinterested whistle of the wind.
Zh: 你想看清里面的情况，不过这边没有窗户。只有风儿冷漠的呼啸声。

En: Is *trying to talk to the city* something you've done before? Is it in your secret repertoire -- a trick for when you're out of ideas?
Zh: 跟*城市对话*这件事，你之前也做过吗？这是你的秘密保留项目吗——当你想不出主意的时候就用这种诡计？

En: A prayer of sorts? To Revachol.
Zh: 某种祈祷文？献给瑞瓦肖的。

En: She could be *anywhere*, how do I find her?
Zh: 她可能在*任何地方*，我该怎么找到她？

En: An adventure awaits! An adventure on the windswept urban coast.
Zh: 一场冒险等待着你！地点就在被风吹过的城市海岸。

En: Buckle up and raise your collar -- this search is going to be wet and cold.
Zh: 穿厚实点，武装起来——这次的搜查一定是阴冷而潮湿的。

En: Anyway -- you do it sector by sector. Go over the whole peninsula, ask the locals, enter the places which you *can* enter first.
Zh: 不管怎么说——你打算一步步来。走遍整个半岛，询问当地人，先去那些你*可以*进去的地方看看。

En: Or spiritual? She could be looking for refuge...
Zh: 也许是很谨慎？她可能在寻找庇护…

En: And then there's the church. You look at the bell tower, rising into the sky -- you look East and remember you already searched it. And found no trace of Ruby.
Zh: 然后是教堂。你看着直插云霄的钟楼——你看着东边，想起自己好像已经搜查过了，但是没有找到露比的踪迹。

En: And then there's the church. You look at the bell tower rising into the clouds -- a prominent place to hide, but maybe she's reckless?
Zh: 然后是教堂。你看着高耸入云的钟楼——藏在这里好像有点太显眼了，不过也许她很鲁莽呢？

En: Indeed. About 70 metres west from here.
Zh: 确实。从这里往西大概70米的地方。

En: How hard can it be? You were told she's north of the village and this place is a peninsula. West of here there are the cryptozoologists -- helping them might give you a reason to map out the place.
Zh: 能有多难？有人告诉过你，她在村子的北边，而且这地方是个半岛。西边是神秘动物学家，帮助他们也许可以给你一个摸清地形的理由。

En: How hard can it be? You were told she's north of the village and this place is a peninsula. You've already mapped it out while checking the traps. So you *know* the place.
Zh: 能有多难？有人告诉过你，她在村子的北边，而且这地方是个半岛。你在检查陷阱的时候已经摸清了这里。所以你已经*知道*这个地方了。

En: How hard can it be? You were told she's north of the village and this place is a peninsula. Look around. West of here there are people, talking. Who could it be, you cup your ear and think?
Zh: 能有多难？有人告诉过你，她在村子的北边，而且这地方是个半岛。看看周围。西边有人，正在说话。会是谁呢，你竖起耳朵想到？

En: And if that fails? If I don't find her? (Conclude.)
Zh: 如果那也失败了呢？如果我找不到她呢？（总结。）

En: First you need to tune in to the coast, get to know its residents. Then you can search where you can't enter: bunkers, storm drainage, this building here. You can always come back and talk to the wind again -- look where it already got you.
Zh: 首先，你需要倾听整个海岸，了解这里的居民。然后搜寻那些你进不去的地方：碉堡，雨水管，还有这里的这栋建筑。你随时可以回来，继续跟风对话——看看它会把你带向何处。

En: The writing rises in front of you, its ancient carton wet from the coastal air.
Zh: 文字在你面前升起，古老的纸箱被海岸的空气弄湿了。

En: Probably a good idea. Blowing on your fingers doesn't make them much warmer though...
Zh: 也许是个好主意。对手指哈气也没有变得很暖和…

En: Well... The man behind you hesitates for a moment. "I was *trying* to explain to Mikael what it is the officer keeps returning here for -- then I realized I don't know."
Zh: “呃…”你身后的男人犹豫了一会儿。“我在*试着*向米克尔解释为什么警官一直回到这里——然后我发现自己也不知道。”

En: No one. You're just talking to yourself again -- the letters slowly peeling off the mural seem to form a malevolent smile.
Zh: 没有人。你又在自言自语了——字母慢慢从壁画上剥落，好像组成了一张恶毒的笑脸。

En: In case she's underground? Don't mind that -- a bad attempt at humour.
Zh: “万一她真的在地下呢？别介意——想开个玩笑，不过失败了。”

En: He stops to consider your words. "*Ask Revachol* sounds like an expression. For when you have no idea. We should continue our search. Perhaps even get a little desperate..."
Zh: 他停下来思考着你的话。“*问瑞瓦肖*听起来很像在表达——你不知道。我们应该继续搜寻。甚至可以孤注一掷些…”

En: Of course. We should continue our search.
Zh: “当然。我们应该继续搜寻。”

En: I am trying to ask Revachol.
Zh: “我在试着询问瑞瓦肖”

En: I'm just thinking. This is me thinking. Nothing strange here.
Zh: “我只是在想事情，我想事情的时候就是这样。没什么奇怪的。”

En: Ask the wind or 'Aska i Vinden' is the name of a Vaasan lullaby, he remarks. "Maybe that helps?"
Zh: “问问风或者是‘风中轻语’是一首瓦萨人的摇篮曲，”他评论到。“也许这样能帮到你？”

En: Blood spatter reading, rooster sacrifice -- still quite common in Revachol, he remarks. "Hope it helps."
Zh: “阅读血迹，献祭公鸡——现在在瑞瓦肖还是很常见，”他评论到。“希望能帮你。”

En: I am performing *black magic*.
Zh: “我在施展*黑魔法*。”

En: I am trying to ask the wind.
Zh: “我在试着问风。”

En: 'Ask Revachol' is the name of a war time radio show the communists used to address civil issues, he remarks. "Maybe that helps?"
Zh: “‘询问瑞瓦肖’是战时的一个节目，是康米党用来解决民生问题的，”他评论到。“也许这样能帮到你？”

En: Of course. Didn't mean to imply otherwise.
Zh: “当然了。我没有在暗示什么。”

En: It doesn't.
Zh: 它没有。

En: Even though you're *sure* you succeeded, all is quiet. There is no cold hand brushing against your forehead, no rustle in the reeds -- the wind has died down. The ruin in front of you is silent as a tomb.
Zh: 虽然你*确定*自己成功了，一切还是很安静。没有冰冷的手拂过你的前额，芦苇丛也不再沙沙作响——风停了。你只能听见远处海浪拍打岸边的声音。站在你面前的废墟就像坟墓一样静谧。

En: Listen to the wind again.
Zh: 再次倾听风的声音。

En: Found the empty trap.
Zh: 找到空陷阱。

En: Found the jacket.
Zh: 找到夹克。

En: Heard Lena's true story.
Zh: 听莉娜的真实故事。

En: Went on a date.
Zh: 去约会。

En: Reconstructed execution.
Zh: 重新执行。

En: Made that awful call.
Zh: 做出那个可怕的决定。

En: Confronted the Pigs.
Zh: 跟猪猪女侠对峙。

En: Established the night club.
Zh: 建立夜店。

En: Discovered the anomaly.
Zh: 发现异常。

En: Death notification.
Zh: 死亡通知。

En: The once-bright mural towers above you, saying "FELD ELECTRICAL R&D: Tomorrow is just a whisper away."
Zh: 昔日光鲜耀眼的壁画高耸在你面前，上面写着“菲尔德电气R&D：明日近在眼前。”

En: The once-bright mural towers above you, saying "FELD ELECTRICAL R&D."
Zh: 昔日光辉耀眼的光景高耸在你面前，上面写着“菲尔德电气R&D”。

En: You felt it, before, when it rained in Martinaise and you *felt* the cold of the rain. The feeling persists. It has been in you ever since.
Zh: 你感觉到过，之前，在马丁内斯下雨的时候，你*感觉*到雨水的冰冷。这种感觉还持续着。从那之后就一直存在。

En: Why single out this one building?
Zh: 为什么单挑这一栋建筑呢？

En: The ruin in front of you is silent as a tomb.
Zh: 你面前的废墟安静的就像一座坟墓。

En: [Turn away.]
Zh: [转过身。]

En: A slogan used to intertwine with the loops a long time ago. Now only a shadow of peeled letters remains. It says: 'Tomorrow is just a whisper away'.
Zh: 很久以前，一个标语曾经跟线圈纠缠在一起。现在只剩下几个脱落字母的阴影。上面写着：‘明日近在眼前’。

En: Step closer.
Zh: 靠近一些。

En: Tomorrow is just a whisper away...
Zh: “明日近在眼前…”

En: Looks like tomorrow never came. The lieutenant raises the collar of his bomber jacket.
Zh: “看来明日没有到来。”警督竖起短夹克的衣领。

En: Ah, yes, their famous motto! You hear the man exclaim behind you. "A fine example of technological optimism, from..." He says something, but the wind blows and you can't hear him.
Zh: “啊，是的，他们最著名的箴言！”男人在你身后大声呼喊。“一个技术乐观主义的好例子，来自于…”他说了些什么，不过起了一阵风，你没能听见。

En: The wind whispers and whispers.
Zh: 风儿在窃窃私语。

En: Anyway -- we do it the old fashioned way: sector by sector. Go over the whole peninsula, ask the locals, enter the places where we *can* enter first. Like we did in the village.
Zh: “总之——我们就按老方法来吧：一个区一个区的来。走遍整个半岛，询问当地人，先去那些我们*可以*进去的地方看看。就像在村子里做的一样。”

En: I am trying to ask the wind.
Zh: “我在试着问风。”

En: (Turn to the lieutenant.) "She could be *anywhere*, how do we find her?"
Zh: （转向警督。）“她可能在*任何地方*，我们怎么找到她？”

En: And if that fails? If we don't find her? (Conclude.)
Zh: “如果失败了呢？如果我们找不到她呢？”（总结。）

En: What was I even attempting to do here?
Zh: 我到底打算在这里做什么啊？

En: How *do* we? I was really hoping she'd be in the village... The lieutenant sighs, then gets a hold of himself: "Okay."
Zh: “我们*怎么做*？我真的希望她就在村子里…”警督叹了口气，然后稳定了下情绪：“好吧。”

En: She's *probably* north of the village and this place is a peninsula. We've already talked to the cryptozoologists... He looks to his right. "Working with them might give us a good excuse to run around -- give us some *structure*..."
Zh: “她*可能*在村子北边，这地方是个半岛。我们已经跟神秘动物学家聊过了…”他看着自己的右边。“跟他们一起工作也许为我们提供了一个到处搜查的好理由——给了我们一些*结构*…”

En: She's *probably* north of the village and this place is a peninsula. He looks North. "We already scanned most of the outdoor areas on our wild *cryptid hunt*, so we have an understanding of the geography, at least."\n
Zh: “她*可能*在村子北边，这地方是个半岛。”他看向北边。“在进行野外*神秘动物狩猎*的时候，我们已经搜查了大部分户外区域，所以至少对这里的地理还是有了一定的了解。”

En: She's *probably* north of the village and this place is a peninsula. I think there's people west of here, we could ask them... He looks to his right and cups his ear.
Zh: “她*可能*在村子北边，这地方是个半岛。我想西边应该住着人，我们应该去问问…”他看着自己的右边，竖起耳朵。

En: Because I *hope* you didn't mean to climb that ladder... He points to the ladder next to the sign and says: "It's *not* safe."
Zh: “因为我*希望*你不是真打算爬上那个梯子…”他指着标志旁边的梯子，说到：“那样太*不安全*了。”

En: Or spiritual? She could be looking for refuge...
Zh: 或者是精神上的？她可能在寻找庇护…

En: Then -- if we're desperate -- we can look where we *can't* enter. Bunkers, storm drainage, this place. I'm sure it won't come to that. He looks behind him, at the dark red box crumbling across the chasm.
Zh: “然后——如果我们真的不顾一切——那可以去我们*不能*进入的地方看看。碉堡，雨水管道，这个地方。我肯定应该不会走到那一步。”他回头张望，那个黑红色的盒子在深渊旁摇摇欲坠。

En: Listen to the wind -- but for *what*? Ruby whistling? The crackle of her bonfire? Some errant whisper, or a dog barking...
Zh: 倾听风的声音——但是听*什么*呢？露比的口哨声？篝火的噼啪声？一些飘忽的低语，或者是一声狗的吠叫…

En: What am I *doing* here?
Zh: 我在这里*做什么*？

En: Shake off the feeling.
Zh: 摆脱这种感觉。

En: *WHAT ARE YOU DOING HERE?* A terrible cold comes over you as you think the words. They echo in your skull, alien suddenly.
Zh: *你在这里做什么？*一想到这些词语，你就被一股恶寒团团围住。它们在你的骨头里发出回声，突然间有点诡异的感觉。

En: For what? A song carried on the breeze? Or children laughing, far away? You walk and walk these city streets and deserted coast, and all it ever tells you -- is cold.
Zh: 为了什么？一首在微风中飘荡的歌？还是远处孩子的笑声？你走在城市街道和荒芜海岸上，它唯一能告诉你的——只有寒冷。

En: *THERE IS NOTHING ELSE.* The thought makes your bones rattle, like the skeleton of some deserted building ready to collapse.
Zh: *没有别的了。*这个想法让你的骨头开始打颤，像那些废弃的大楼骨架一样，随时准备崩溃坍塌。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: There's a ladder next to the sign. (Point to it.) "Perhaps we can climb it? Enter through the roof?"
Zh: “标志旁边有个梯子。”（用手指过去。）“也许我们可以爬上去？从屋顶进去？”

En: He stops to consider your words. "*Ask the wind* sounds like an expression. For when you have no idea. We should continue our search. Perhaps even get a little desperate..."
Zh: 他停下来考虑了一下你的话。“*问问风*听起来很像在表达——你不知道。我们应该继续搜寻。甚至可以孤注一掷些…”

En: There has to be a way to use brute force. Climbing sounds unsafe. Brute force is safe. Look around and find something to break if the ladder fails.
Zh: 肯定有使用蛮力的办法。攀爬听起来不太安全。不过蛮力很安全。在周围到处看看，如果梯子失败的话，找些其他的突破点。

En: Perhaps *you* can climb them. *We're* not climbing anything. I'm 43 years old -- and I plan to live to see 70.
Zh: “也许*你*可以爬上去。*我们*什么都不爬。我已经43岁了——还打算活到70呢。”

En: We've looked everywhere else.
Zh: “别的地方我们都看过了。”

En: Where does it go?
Zh: 它是通向哪里的？

En: He shrugs. "So how do we get in there? The doors were on the collapsed side of this building... they're gone, basically."
Zh: 他耸耸肩。“所以我们怎么进去？门在大厦坍塌的那边…基本上可以说，已经没了。”

En: The wind told me.
Zh: “风告诉我的。”

En: Right, he shrugs. "How do we get in there? The doors were on the collapsed side of this building... they're gone, basically."
Zh: “没错，”他耸耸肩。“我们怎么进去？门在大厦坍塌的那边…基本上可以说，已经没了。”

En: And then there's the church. He looks at the bell tower. "We've already searched that and can rule it out. I know it doesn't feel like progress, but exclusion is a step too."
Zh: “然后是教堂。”他看着钟楼。“我们已经搜查过，可以排除了。我知道看起来好像没什么进展，不过排除也是向前迈了一步。”

En: And then there's the church. He looks at the bell tower. "If I were a murder suspect I would not hide in the most prominent building here, but who knows? Maybe she's reckless..."
Zh: “然后是教堂。”他看着钟楼。“如果我是凶杀案嫌疑人，肯定不会藏在这里最显眼的建筑里，但是谁知道呢？也许她很鲁莽…”

En: Suddenly, all is quiet. There is no cold hand brushing against your forehead, no rustle in the reeds. The wind has died down, or gone behind a corner? You only hear distant waves washing the coast.
Zh: 突然间，一切变得安静起来。没有冰冷的手拂过你的前额，芦苇丛也不再沙沙作响。风正在停歇，或者躲到角落去了？你只能听见远处海浪拍打岸边的声音。

En: Trying to talk to the wind, the city? Whatever you thought would happen did not, and now you're just standing there, in the dark. With your hands fallen to your side.
Zh: 试着跟风说话，或者是城市？你以为会发生的事都没有发生，现在你只是站在原地，身处黑暗之中。双手垂落身体两侧。

En: Trying to talk to the wind? The city? Whatever you thought would happen did not, and now you're just standing there, in the pale of the morning. With your hands fallen to your side.
Zh: 试着跟风说话？还是城市？你以为会发生的事都不会发生，现在你不过是呆呆站在清晨苍白的光芒中。双手垂放在身体两侧。

En: Trying to talk to the wind? The city? Whatever you thought would happen did not, and now you're just standing there with your hands fallen to your side.
Zh: 试着跟风说话？还是城市？你以为会发生的事都不会发生，现在你只是双手垂落身体两侧，呆呆站立着。

En: Trying to talk to the wind? The city? Whatever you thought would happen did not, and now you're just standing there, in the dusk hour. With your hands fallen to your side.
Zh: 试着跟风说话，或者是城市？你以为会发生的事都没有发生，现在是黄昏时分，你只是站在原地。双手垂落身体两侧。

En: Trying to talk to the wind, or the city? Whatever you thought would happen did not, and now you're just standing there with your hands fallen to your side.
Zh: 试着跟风说话，或者是城市？你以为会发生的事都没有发生，现在你只是站在原地，双手垂落身体两侧。

En: Indeed. You hear what sounds like two men arguing where the lieutenant is pointing.
Zh: 确实。从警督手指的方向，你听到似乎是两个男人争执的声音。

En: Listen to the wind.
Zh: 倾听风的声音。

En: Found the empty trap.
Zh: 发现空陷阱。

En: Found the jacket.
Zh: 找到夹克。

En: Heard Lena's true story.
Zh: 听莉娜的真实故事。

En: Went on a date.
Zh: 去约会。

En: Reconstructed execution.
Zh: 重新执行。

En: Made that awful call.
Zh: 做出那个可怕的决定。

En: Confronted the Pigs.
Zh: 跟猪猪女侠对峙。

En: Established the night club.
Zh: 建立夜店。

En: Discovered the anomaly.
Zh: 发现异常。

En: Death notification.
Zh: 死亡通知。

En: No. There is something else. In the wind.
Zh: 不。还有些别的什么。就在风里。

En: As if said by someone else. Someone outside you. Around you. Not you.
Zh: 就好像是其他人说的。在你之外的某个人。在你周围的某个人。但不是你。

En: The lieutenant looks at you shiver, worried suddenly. "We should go. It gets cold if you stand in one place too long."
Zh: 警督看着颤抖的你，突然担心起来。“我们该走了。在一个地方站得太久会变冷的。”

En: Just blow on your hands for warmth.
Zh: 朝你的双手哈气取暖。

En: Who said that?
Zh: “谁说的？”

En: What? I didn't say anything. He looks at you. "What are we doing here? We keep returning and you act strange."
Zh: “什么？我什么也没说。”他看着你。“我们在这做什么呢？我们一直回到这里来，你的表现也很奇怪。”

En: I am performing *black magic*.
Zh: “我在施展*黑魔法*。”

En: I'm just thinking. This is me thinking. Nothing strange here.
Zh: “我只是在想事情，我想事情的时候就是这样。没什么奇怪的。”

En: Of course. Black magic. The most potent type of magic. If we're that desperate -- may I suggest getting shovels and digging? A quiver of a smile.
Zh: “当然，黑魔法。最强大的一类魔法。如果我们真有那么绝望——我建议不如弄点铲子开挖吧？”他微微一笑。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Yeah, I don't know...
Zh: “呃，我不知道…”

En: I am trying to ask Revachol.
Zh: “我在试着问瑞瓦肖”

En: He looks around. "Like -- maybe we can get shovels and dig?" A quiver of a smile. "Don't mind that -- a bad attempt at humour."
Zh: 他环顾四周。“比如——也许我们可以弄把铲子，然后挖掘看看？”他微微一笑。“别介意——想开个玩笑，不过失败了。”

En: Be honest. You're praying. It's that bad.
Zh: 诚实一点。你正在祈祷。其实也没那么糟。

En: For what? God? God is very, very silent. Too silent. Even the wind seems tired...
Zh: 为了什么？神明？神明非常、非常安静。太过安静。就连风好像都疲倦了…

En: Ever since I woke up -- maybe even before -- I've been getting these strange *cold spells*.
Zh: “自从我醒过来——也许之前也有过——我就好像被人下了奇怪的*寒冷咒语*。”

En: Good, good, yes. Cold spells... He seems incredulous. "So basically your *hangover* is telling you she's down there?"
Zh: “很好，很好，没错。寒冷咒语…”他看起来有些怀疑。“所以基本上是你的*宿醉*告诉你说她在下面？”

En: Yes. I can literally *talk* to my hangover. That's how bad it has gotten.
Zh: “是的。我可以跟我的宿醉*说话*，字面意义上的。情况真的就有这么糟糕。”

En: Say: "I think she's down *there*. Below this building."
Zh: 这么说吧：“我觉得她就在*下面*。在这栋大厦下面。”

En: In through the collapsed roof, flowing down a concrete staircase -- to the basement, sweeping away footprints in the dust on the stairs. And then the beach below the boardwalk, its winding tunnels... a whisper away.
Zh: 穿过坍塌的屋顶，流向一段混凝土楼梯——前往地下室，扫清灰扑扑楼梯上遗留的脚印。接着是木板道下面的海滩，进入风洞…最后变成一阵耳语。

En: Okay -- why?
Zh: “好吧——为什么？”

En: Finally, my time to shine!
Zh: 终于，我发光的时刻到了！

En: Suddenly there's a sigh, carried on the molecules around you, moving, flowing from high pressure to low pressure. Like that of a woman emptying her lungs. She wraps the collapsing stone box in front of you in her breath, flowing through it...
Zh: 突然间传来一声叹息，卷起你周身的微粒，移动着，从高压流向低压。就像一个女人在清空自己的肺部。她一口气卷起你面前坍塌的石头盒子，从中流过…

En: Call it a gut feeling.
Zh: “我把这个叫做直觉。”

En: In there? She could. Or... The lieutenant points behind you. "She could be in the *identical* ruin over there, or in that boat shack. In that church tower, maybe..."
Zh: “那里面吗？有可能。或者…”警督指向你的身后。“她可能藏在那边几乎一模一样的废墟里，或者是那个船屋。又或许，在那座塔型教堂里…”

En: Great idea!
Zh: 好主意！

En: Could Ruby be in there?
Zh: “露比可能在里面吗？”

En: Could she be in there?
Zh: “她有可能在里面吗？”

En: Indeed. Somehow you knew it was here. An urban ruin gutted by looters that once used to consume money and dispense warmth and light.
Zh: 确实。不知道为什么，你知道就在那里。一片被掠夺者破坏的城市废墟，那里曾经被用来消费金钱，散发温暖和光芒。

En: What is this building?
Zh: “这是什么建筑？”

En: Maybe we could, you know... He points to the man who points at the building, telling his son something. "Ask the man who's pointing at the building?"
Zh: “也许我们可以，你知道的…”顺着他手指的方向，你看到一个男人正指着那栋建筑对自己的儿子说话。“问问那个指着这栋建筑的男人？”

En: Why do I feel like I know this place?
Zh: 为什么我感觉自己知道这个地方？

En: What is happening...
Zh: 出什么事了…

En: SHE'S DOWN THERE.
Zh: 她就在下面。

En: Right. How do we get in, though? The doors were on the collapsed side of this building... they're gone, basically.
Zh: “好吧。那我们怎么进去？门在大厦坍塌的那边…基本上可以说，已经没了。”

En: There's something in the wind. Sometimes the only way to go forward is to fail first.
Zh: 风中好像有着什么。有时候，前进的唯一办法就是先失败。

En: Walk the coast, the old boardwalk, the reeds... You can always come back here and talk to the wind again. Look where it already got you.
Zh: 走过海岸，旧木板道，芦苇丛……你随时可以回来，继续跟风对话。看看它会把你带向何处。

En: Somewhere in Martinaise, Lieutenant Kitsuragi is consulting his notebook... A sudden draught turns a page between his hands. You're going to need him if you want to listen to the wind.
Zh: 马丁内斯的某个地方，曷城警督正在翻阅自己的笔记本…一阵突然的气流翻动了他手中的一页。如果需要倾听风的声音，你需要他在身边。

En: Lieutenant Kitsuragi stands in front of a mirror, fixing his orange bomber jacket. You should be asking him that question.
Zh: 曷城警督站在一面镜子面前，修理着自己的橙色飞行员夹克。你应该问问他这个问题。

En: The long abandoned industrial building towers above you in silence.
Zh: 早已废弃的工业建筑沉默无语地耸立在你上空。

En: A breeze, like a quiet sigh, moves over you. The cool air breaks across your body. It brings the salt of the sea into your lungs.
Zh: 一阵微风从你身上吹拂而过，宛如一声安静的叹息。凉爽的空气越过你的身体。它把大海的盐分带入你的肺部。

En: GO TO THE CHILDREN OF THE BIG SEA.
Zh: 去找大海之子。

En: What the hell was that?
Zh: 那是什么鬼？

En: Yes. I understand.
Zh: 好，我明白了。

En: Yes, Comrade Zephyr. I shall find them. Girl Child Revolution and Girl Child Communism!
Zh: 是的，海风同志。我会找到他们的。纯真革命和纯真康米主义！

En: The big sea... The Big C? That can only mean one thing: Communism.
Zh: 大海……大C？那只能说明一件事：康米主义。

En: The wind rushes away, leaving you where you were -- on the rotting boards of the Feld Building.
Zh: 风匆匆离去，把你留在原地——菲尔德大厦的腐烂木板道上。

En: BOY CHILD FREEDOM. BOY CHILD JUSTICE.
Zh: 孩子的自由，孩子的正义。

En: Officer? The lieutenant watches you attentively. "Are you okay? It looked like we lost you for a second."
Zh: “警官？”警督认真地看着你。“你还好吗？看起来你好像突然走神了一会儿。”

En: Did I have a seizure, Kim?
Zh: “我是不是癫痫发作了，金？”

En: I think I need to talk to some kids in the village.
Zh: “我想我需要和村里的一些孩子谈谈。”

En: The wind told me to talk to children. Let's roll.
Zh: “风告诉我，要和孩子们说话。我们走吧。”

En: I need to go to the children, Kim. Girl Child Communism. Boy Child Freedom.
Zh: “我需要去找孩子们，金。纯真的康米主义。纯真的自由。”

En: Doesn't look like it to me. He pauses to study you for a moment. "Probably not, anyway."
Zh: “我看不像。”他停下来仔细看了你一会儿。“也可能不是。”

En: When you're ready. The lieutenant nods.
Zh: 警督点点头。“等你准备好。”

En: Right. Well, I'm sure that after... The lieutenant's words come to a slow halt. He pinches the bridge of his nose.
Zh: “好吧。那，我相信以后……”警督的话音慢慢停下。他捏了捏鼻梁。

En: This is part of the investigation now.
Zh: “现在这是调查的一部分了。”

En: Okay... The lieutenant looks down the coast, toward the shacks. "When you're ready, officer."
Zh: “好吧……”警督看了看海岸线，朝小木屋的方向看去。“等你准备好，警官。”

En: Communism is a murder investigation, Kim. It's about understanding whose bootheel killed the human spirit.
Zh: “康米主义是一宗谋杀调查，金。要查出来谁的靴尖杀死了人类的灵魂。”

En: Pull on your knowledge to understand what caused the twins to hear radio in their heads.
Zh: 利用你的知识来搞懂是什么原因让双胞胎在脑海中听到了无线电。

En: Searched Ruby's lorry.
Zh: 搜查露比的货车。

En: Lingered on the north coast.
Zh: 在北海岸逛逛。

En: Heard stories from Idiot Doom Spiral.
Zh: 听白痴厄运漩涡的故事。

En: Searched the coast for traps.
Zh: 在海岸上寻找陷阱。

En: Seen inside the Church.
Zh: 查看教堂内部。

En: Icosahedral Dice Set "Sirens".
Zh: 二十面体骰子“塞壬”。

En: You know a lot about radio. Enough to know: You have no idea how or why those boys heard Speedfreaks FM in their heads.
Zh: 你对无线电很了解。足够知道：你不知道这些孩子为什么会在脑海里听到狂飙怪人.FM。

En: Let's see, let's see... Just flicking through the index of your knowledge...
Zh: 让我看看，让我看看……在你的知识索引里翻了翻……

En: Still nothing. Your head is full of obscure facts, none of which are helpful here. Move on before you make another scene.
Zh: 还是一无所获。你的脑袋里装满了晦涩难懂的事实，但在这里没有一件有用的。别再闹了，赶紧走吧。

En: Cool. No rush.
Zh: 好吧。不急着。

En: Come on, hurry up, we're on a schedule here!
Zh: 来吧，快点，时间不等人！

En: Searching with all possible speed!
Zh: 在全速搜索！

En: We have, uh... Radio brands, radio crimes, radio comedies, radio dramas, radio physics, radio psychics, radio scandals, radio shows, and way more in-between, but, uh....
Zh: 我们有，呃……无线电品牌，无线电犯罪，无线电喜剧，无线电剧，无线电物理学，无线电心理学，无线电丑闻，无线电节目，还有更多介于两者之间的东西，但是，呃……

En: But?
Zh: 但是？

En: Nothing that would be useful here. Sorry.
Zh: 没有什么有用的。对不起。

En: I know about ULAN frequencies. Does that help?
Zh: 我知道ULAN的频率。这有什么用吗？

En: You know little about ULAN frequencies other than that they exist. Maybe? It's impossible to say.
Zh: 你对ULAN频率知之甚少，只知道它们存在。也许吧？很难说。

En: Radio is killing the fucking cop star. Come on!
Zh: “无线电正在谋杀该死的警察之星。来吧！”

En: Is this all I am? A fount of useless radio trivia?
Zh: “我就是这种人？一个无用无线电豆知识源泉？”

En: Then I've got nothing. I'm a shit detective. I should be in the sewer.
Zh: “那我就什么都没有了。我是个狗屎警探。我应该爬回下水道里。”

En: What is he talking about? The young boy looks to his father, frowning.
Zh: “他在说什么？”小男孩看向父亲，皱着眉头。

En: I'm not sure, Mikael. He looks at you, as if making an appraisal.
Zh: “我不确定，米克尔。”他看着你，仿佛在做评估。

En: The lieutenant clears his throat. "Let's move on. I don't think we need to stand around anymore."
Zh: 警督清了清嗓子。“我们走吧。我觉得没必要在这傻站着。”

En: Apologies! You just need time to remember. There has to be something there. Go do other things around the coast. That might help.
Zh: 抱歉！你只是需要时间来回忆。一定有什么用得上的。去做海边做点别的事情吧。这可能会有帮助。

En: Knowledge like that isn't just obscure; it's unknowable. Dangerous theoretical facts like that are probably protected under the Coalition Government's Articles of Dominion, Title XIV, Article 7c. While an expert might be able to suss it out on their own, a layman like you has no hope.
Zh: 这样的知识不仅晦涩难懂，而且是不可知的。像这种危险的理论事实，很可能受到联盟的《统治条款》第十四章第7C款的保护。专家也许能自己弄清楚，但像你这样的门外汉就别想了。

En: You don't know how it happened. But the stores of your knowledge are vast and there *is* something crucial in them.
Zh: 你不知道事情是怎么发生的。但你的知识储备非常渊博，其中*肯定*有关键的东西。

En: What's that?
Zh: 什么东西？

En: You know where Ruby *isn't.*
Zh: 你知道露比*不在*那里。

En: She isn't in the fishing village. She's not with her bullet under the floorboards in the shack. So where could she be?
Zh: 她不在渔村，也没和子弹一起藏在小木屋地板下，那她会在哪里呢？

En: Land's End? You took in the sights there with Lilienne. Ruby wasn't up the disused radio tower, among the reeds, or beneath the boardwalk.
Zh: 陆地尽头？你和莉莉恩一起去看了那里的风景。露比不在废弃的无线电塔上，不在芦苇丛中，也不在木板道下。

En: The alcoholics never mentioned seeing Ruby either. Idiot Doom Spiral gave an exhaustive account of recent events, but never mentioned seeing Ruby.
Zh: 那群酒鬼也没有说见过露比。白痴厄运漩涡详尽地描述了最近发生的事件，但从未提到见过露比本人。

En: You've searched the whole coast for Morell's traps. Just as you didn't find the Insulindian phasmid, you did not find her.
Zh: 未了寻找莫雷尔的陷阱，你已经找遍了整个海岸。就像你没有找到伊苏林迪竹节虫一样，你也没有找到露比。

En: Nor is Ruby in the church. The crab-man, Tiago, is -- but not Ruby. Some criminals seek sanctuary from sacred buildings, though the sanctuary laws were abolished decades ago. But Ruby wasn't such a one.
Zh: 露比也不在教堂里。蟹男蒂亚戈在——但他不是露比。尽管庇护法在几十年前就被废除了，一些罪犯仍然在神圣的建筑中寻求庇护。但露比不是这样的人。

En: You've even entered the Church, met Tiago, and Soona. Some criminals seek sanctuary from sacred buildings, though the sanctuary laws were abolished decades ago. But Ruby wasn't such a one.
Zh: 你甚至已经进入了教堂，见到了蒂亚戈，还有苏娜。尽管庇护法在几十年前就被废除了，一些罪犯仍然在神圣的建筑中寻求庇护。但露比不是这样的人。

En: You've even entered the Church, helped the ravers set up a night club, and overheard... well, we don't need to think about that. Point is: Some criminals seek sanctuary from sacred buildings, though the sanctuary laws were abolished decades ago. But Ruby wasn't such a one.
Zh: 你甚至进入了教会，帮助派对狂们建立了一个夜总会，并且偷听到了……好吧，我们不需要考虑这些。重点是：尽管庇护法在几十年前就被废除了，一些罪犯仍然在神圣的建筑中寻求庇护。但露比不是这样的人。

En: Nor is Ruby in the Church. Some criminals still seek sanctuary from sacred buildings, even though sanctuary laws were abolished decades ago. But Ruby wasn't such a one.
Zh: 露比也不在教堂里。尽管庇护法在几十年前就被废除了，一些罪犯仍然在神圣的建筑中寻求庇护。但露比不是这样的人。

En: Does that mean she's *here*?
Zh: 这是不是说她就在*这里*？

En: If she's here on the coast, she'll be *here.* And besides, something weird happened to the twins around here. It'd be nice to know what caused that. Another fact for your collection!
Zh: 如果她在海岸，她就会在*这里*。而且，这附近的双胞胎也发生了一些奇怪的事。又是一个被你纳入囊中的事实！

En: Suddenly there's a sigh, carried on the molecules around you, moving, flowing from high pressure to low pressure. Like that of a woman emptying her lungs. She wraps the collapsing stone box in front of you in her breath, flowing through it...
Zh: 突然间，你周围的分子发出一声叹息，移动着，从高压流向低压。就像一个女人排空肺部的声音。她用气息包裹着你面前正在坍塌的石盒子，流淌在其中……

En: I see. Political theory as detective work. Right. The lieutenant looks down the coast, toward the shacks. "After you, officer."
Zh: “我明白了，政治理论指导警务工作。好吧。” 警督看了看海边，朝小木屋的方向走去。“你先请，警官。”

En: Judging eyes, like he's a fucking brain doctor. What the hell does he know?
Zh: 审视的眼神，他就像个他妈的脑科医生。他到底知道些什么？

En: You mean we should do that *right now*, don't you? the lieutenant asks, his usually even tone wavering slightly. "Can that be done *after* the murder investigation?"
Zh: “你的意思是我们应该*现在*就要去做，是吗？警督问道，他惯常平淡的语气略有动摇。”可以在谋杀案调查*之后*再做吗？“

En: The wind didn't tell me to wait.
Zh: “风没有让我等。”

En: True. And Ruby does have radio experience, as evidenced by her lorry... But how do we even get in there? The doors were on the collapsed side of this building... they're gone, basically.
Zh: “没错。而且露比确实有使用无线电的经验，从她的货车上就可以看出……但我们怎么才能进去呢？门在这栋楼倒塌的那一边……基本进不去了。”

En: True...  But how do we even get in there? The doors were on the collapsed side of this building... they're gone, basically.
Zh: “没错……但我们怎么才能进去呢？门在这栋楼倒塌的那一边……基本进不去了。”

En: The twins did hear the radio from here, so *something* is happening...
Zh: “双胞胎确实听到了这里传来广播声，所以*有事情*发生了……”

En: And you do know one thing about radios: ULAN. Ruby dabbles in advanced radio theories. ULAN, the Speedfreaks mystery -- it might just be related.
Zh: 而你确实知道一件关于无线电的事: ULAN。露比对先进的无线电理论颇有研究。ULAN，狂飙怪人.FM之谜——可能有关联。

En: ... Well, in fairness, you haven't seen much more of the coast. But you do know one last thing: ULAN. Ruby dabbles in advanced radio theories. ULAN, the Speedfreaks mystery -- it might just be related.
Zh: ……好吧，平心而论，你还没有遍查海岸一带。但你知道最后一件事：ULAN。露比对先进的无线电理论颇有研究。ULAN，狂飙怪人.FM之谜——可能有关联。

En: And you were probably right: The ULAN frequency is likely connected to all this. Ruby dabbles in advanced radio theories. The Speedfreaks FM case is a radio mystery. That may not be coincidence.
Zh: 而且你可能是对的。ULAN的频率很可能与这一切有关。露比对先进的无线电理论颇有研究。狂飙怪人.FM的事情就是一个无线电之谜。这可能不是巧合。

En: And, while you might not have seen much of the coast, you were probably right: The ULAN frequency is likely connected to all this. Ruby dabbles in advanced radio theories. The Speedfreaks FM case is a radio mystery. That may not be coincidence.
Zh: 虽然你可能没遍查海岸一带，但你可能是对的。ULAN的频率很可能与这一切有关。露比对先进的无线电理论颇有研究。狂飙怪人.FM的事情就是一个无线电之谜。这可能不是巧合。

En: That isn't a fact *yet*. But it's a compelling suspicion.
Zh: 这*还*不是板上钉钉的事实。但这是一个令人信服的怀疑。

En: So I was right! ULAN -- she *is* here!
Zh: 所以我是对的！ULAN——她*就在*这里!

En: We should look into that before we move on.
Zh: “在我们继续前进之前，我们应该先调查一下。”

En: The lieutenant glances down the coast, toward the fishing village. "There's also still the question of how the twins tuned into Speedfreaks FM..."
Zh: 警督看了看海边，朝渔村走去。“还有一个问题，双胞胎是如何调入狂飙怪人.FM的……”

En: Can we look into that later?
Zh: “可以晚点再查吗？”

En: A shuffle of nylon as the lieutenant shrugs. "I don't know... It's your call, detective. If you drop the radio lead now, just be prepared to live without having done it."
Zh: 警督耸耸肩，身上的尼龙夹克也在抖动。“我也不知道……。这是你的决定，警探。如果你现在放弃无线电这条线索，就准备好一辈子后悔着过活吧。”

En: A shuffle of nylon as the lieutenant shrugs. "It's your call, detective. If you drop the radio lead now, just be prepared to live without having done it."
Zh: 警督耸耸肩，身上的尼龙夹克也在抖动。“这是你的决定，警探。如果你现在放弃无线电这条线索，就准备好一辈子后悔着过活吧。”

En: You just want to dial your favourite radio station, don't you?
Zh: “你只是想调到你最喜欢的电台，是不是？”

En: A shuffle of nylon as the lieutenant shrugs. "I'm only observing that there's another lead you wanted to follow. And that if you drop the radio lead now, just be prepared to live without having done it."
Zh: 警督耸耸肩，身上的尼龙夹克也在抖动。“我只是观察到，你还有一条线索想要追查。如果你现在放弃无线电这条线索，就准备好一辈子后悔着过活吧。”

En: You see a once-bright mural towering above you. The signage has peeled off over the years, but you can still make out: 'FELD ELECTRICAL R&D'.
Zh: 你看到昔日光辉耀眼的光景高耸在你面前。多年之后，标志已经脱落，但你仍然可以辨认出几个大字：“菲尔德电气R&D。”

En: Above the mural -- a collapsed roof, broken windows set in walls that are cracking and will soon also fall, and the coastal breeze rustling and sighing in the remains of the edifice.
Zh: 壁画上方是坍塌的屋顶，墙上破碎的窗户摇摇欲坠，好像随时都会掉下来。海风在大厦的残骸里窸窣作响，哀声叹息。

En: Feld *Electrical*. How ironic -- all these dark rooms...
Zh: 菲尔德*电气*。真讽刺——看看这些黑漆漆的房间…

En: Feld Electrical -- you only know them as a small company that makes ink cartridges. Looks like they used to be... big.
Zh: 菲尔德电气——你只知道那是一家生产墨盒的小公司。看起来它也曾经…壮大过。

En: Lieutenant Kitsuragi stands in front of a mirror, fixing his black bomber jacket. You should be asking him that question.
Looks like tomorrow never came. The lieutenant raises the collar of his jacket.
Looks like tomorrow never came. The lieutenant raises the collar of his bomber jacket.
Even if it isn't -- no one told you.
Zh: 就算没有——也没人告诉你。

En: It is a thing for *me*. I can't *remember* it's not a thing.
Zh: “那是*我*的事情。我不*记得*没有这种事。”

En: That's not how physics work, detective. And even if it were -- wouldn't you need some kind of apparatus? A teleportation field or...
Zh: “物理学不是这么运作的，警探。就算有——难道你不需要某种装置吗？一个传送场，或者是…”

En: What are they talking about? the kid behind you whispers, asking his father.
Zh: “他们在说什么呢？”你身后的孩子低声问着自己的父亲。

En: Teleportation, Mikael. It's... generally thought impossible.
Zh: “心灵传送，米克尔。这个…一般被认为是不可能的事。”

En: Yes -- climbing it would be too *expensive*. The animations your body needs to make, interacting with the ladder, are beyond your capabilities at this moment in your career.
Zh: 没错——爬上去的代价实在是太*昂贵*了。需要制作你的身体动画，与梯子进行交互，已经超出了此刻你职业生涯的能力范围。

En: He *did*, he totally said something about the apparatus, god damn it...
Zh: 他*说过*的，他绝对说过什么装置的事，该死…

En: You shouldn't attempt to climb this ladder without the lieutenant here. What if you fall and need to be rushed to the hospital?
Zh: 警督不在的时候，你不应该尝试爬上屋顶。如果你摔下来，需要有人送你去医院呢？

En: Okay, well. That's impressive, but...
Zh: “好吧。确实挺让人钦佩的，不过…”

En: Careful...
Zh: “小心…”

En: It's not happening. You simply do not have the mind-over-matter athletic capacity to pull it off at this time.
Zh: 什么也没发生。这一次，你没有心胜于物的运动能力去成功完成任务。

En: Enough of that now, officer... The lieutenant pats you on the back. "There has got to be a better, more *age-appropriate* way in."
Zh: “目前来说已经够了，警官…”警督拍拍你的后背。“一定会有更好，更*适龄*的方法可以进去。”

En: You know, for the record, you didn't teleport there. You just climbed the ladder with your eyes closed...
Zh: “你知道的，明确的说，你不能传送进去。你只是闭着眼睛顺着梯子爬上去了…”

En: Hear that? You're old. That's why you can't do it. Old and feeble. A let-down. A failure.
Zh: 听见了吗？你老了。所以你做不到。又老又虚弱。真是让人失望。失败者。

En: No -- not like a regular person. Like a phase spider.
Zh: 不——不像普通人。像一只相位蜘蛛。

En: The rusty ladder leads to the rooftop.
Zh: 锈蚀的梯子通向屋顶。

En: In addition, the first rung is going to be *tough* to reach. It's, what, three metres above ground, and you're -- 180...?
Zh: 另外，第一级横档应该*很难*碰到。看起来，离地面大概有个3米吧，而你的身高——大概180厘米…？

En: One-eighty seems about right.
Zh: 180厘米好像差不多。

En: One-ninety. I'm a giant.
Zh: 190厘米。我是个巨人。

En: Okay. But, still -- the roof is collapsing and the wind gets pretty brutal up there. Dismounting from the ladder is going to be *hard*. Perhaps if you were to... *not* climb the ladder? Instead...
Zh: 好吧。不过还是那个问题——屋顶已经坍塌，上面的风非常大。从梯子上爬下去一定很*困难*。也许你可以…*不*爬梯子呢？可以…

En: Not to mention that the roof is collapsing and the wind gets pretty brutal up there. Dismounting from the ladder is going to be *hard*. Perhaps if you were to... *not* climb the ladder? Instead...
Zh: 更别提屋顶已经坍塌，上面的风非常大。爬梯子肯定很*困难*。也许你可以…*不*爬梯子呢？换个方法…

En: What if you were to do something more *subtle*?
Zh: 如果你可以做一些更*精妙*的事情呢？

En: What if you were to *re-conceptualize* climbing the ladder?
Zh: 如果你可以*重新定义*一下爬梯子这件事呢？

En: ASTRAL PROJECTION! Be *open-minded* about this.
Zh: 星体投射！对这个*开明*一点。

En: Yeah... Because falling from that height seems, well -- SPLAT! So don't do that. Just, you know...
Zh: 是啊…因为从这个高度摔下去好像，好吧——就是啪嗒一声！所以，你知道的…

En: On second thought, maybe teleportation isn't a thing.
Zh: 转念一想，也许心灵传送并不存在。

En: Why?
Zh: 为什么？

En: Because you're just standing there squeezing your buttocks and nothing is happening.
Zh: 因为你只是站在原地拼命提肛，但是什么也没发生。

En: Kim, do you know where I could *get* that teleportation apparatus?
Zh: “金，你知道我能去哪*弄到*传送装置吗？”

En: Kim, it isn't happening! I'm not teleporting.
Zh: “金，什么事也没发生！我没有传送过去。”

En: He looks at you, eyes wide with feigned surprise. "You're not? Please don't try to *climb* the building, we'll get in another way."
Zh: 他看着你，睁大双眼，装出很惊讶的样子。“没有吗？那就别试着*爬上*那栋大厦，我们找个其他办法进去。”

En: Don't say the stupid thing.
Zh: 别说蠢话了。

En: The lieutenant looks at you with that 'I-know-you're-thinking-the-stupid-thing' look -- as you grow tired of clenching your buttocks and give up.
Zh: 警督看着你，带着那副“我知道你又在想蠢事了”的表情——慢慢的，你厌倦了提肛的动作，放弃了。

En: Teleportation apparatus? I don't remember mentioning anything about teleportation apparatus. Please don't try to *climb* the building, we'll get in another way.
Zh: “传送装置？我不记得自己有提过传送装置的事。别尝试*爬上*那栋大厦，我们找个其他办法进去。”

En: Don't gloat. Just stand there like a Samaran master.
Zh: 别沾沾自喜了，只要像个萨马拉大师一样站在那里就好。

En: A rusty ladder leads to the rooftop. Some of the rungs are missing.
Zh: 一个锈蚀的梯子，一路通向屋顶。有些横档不见了。

En: Yeah, that doesn't look good at all.
Zh: “是啊，看起来一点也不好。”

En: You shouldn't attempt to get to the roof without the lieutenant here. What if you fall and need to be rushed to the hospital?
Zh: 警督不在的时候，你不应该尝试爬上屋顶。如果你摔下来，需要有人送你去医院呢？

En: It won't hurt to try.
Zh: “试一下也不会怎么样。”

En: You're not ready to do this yet. [Leave.]
Zh: 你还没有做好准备。[离开。]

En: Oh yes. It could hurt. A lot.
Zh: “是啊。可能会疼。非常疼。”

En: He is restraining himself from using a parental tone with you right now.
Zh: 此刻他正在克制自己，尽量不用父母的口吻跟你说话。

En: Yes. Teleportation is completely not a thing. Perhaps if you were to, instead, climb the ladder with your eyes closed? This way it's less scary.
Zh: 没错。心灵传送根本不存在。作为替代，也许你可以闭着眼睛爬梯子呢？这样就没那么吓人了。

En: Grab the first rung.
Zh: 抓住第一个横档。

En: It doesn't crumble in your hand, as one might expect. In fact, it doesn't even bend.
Zh: 跟期望中的不同，它没有在你手中破裂。事实上，它甚至都没有弯曲。

En: Officer, be careful! Officer!
Zh: “警官，小心啊！警官！”

En: The ladder gleams arrogantly with what remains of its metallic lustre as you hit the barrel and tumble onto the ground.
Zh: 你撞到木桶，摔到在地，而梯子带着自己仅存的一点金属光泽，傲慢地闪烁着。

En: Brace yourself!
Zh: 振作起来！

En: The ladder gleams arrogantly with what remains of its metallic lustre as you hit the barrel and tumble onto the ground.
Zh: 你撞到木桶，摔到在地，而梯子带着自己仅存的一点金属光泽，傲慢地闪烁着。

En: Assess the situation.
Zh: 评估现状。

En: The distances between the remaining rungs are rather *wide*. You'd have to use the mounting brackets. However, they seem corroded and the peeling rust is razor-sharp.
Zh: 剩下来的横档之间的距离非常*宽*，你必须使用托架才能过去。不过，它们看起来锈得厉害，剥落的铁锈像剃刀一样锋利。

En: Your fingers slip from the rung...
Zh: 你的手指从横档上滑落…

En: Grab the second rung.
Zh: 抓住第二个横档。

En: What if I don't climb? What if I just... *teleport*?
Zh: “如果我不爬呢？如果我可以直接…*传送*呢？”

En: Teleportation is not a thing. The lieutenant stares at you, stone-faced.
Zh: 警督面无表情地盯着你。“心灵传送根本不存在。”

En: C'mon, Kim. Where's your adventurous spirit?
Zh: “拜托，金。你的冒险精神哪去了？”

En: Teleportation *is* a thing. Just needs a bit of concentration...
Zh: “心灵传送*确实*存在。只是需要一点点集中力…”

En: This really has nothing to do with... adventure. We're dealing with basic physics here.
Zh: “这真的跟冒险…没什么关系。眼下只是基础的物理问题。”

En: Okay, let's say teleportation is a thing. Wouldn't you need some kind of... scientific apparatus to create a teleportation field? You can't just do it without apparatus.
Zh: “好吧，假设心灵传送存在。那你不应该需要某种…科学装置来创造出一个传送场吗？没有装置的话，你是做不到的。”

En: Teleport to the roof.
Zh: 传送到屋顶。

En: Inspired by Lo Manthang.
Zh: 受罗马丹的启发。

En: Saw Tiago climbing.
Zh: 看见蒂亚戈爬过

En: Remote Viewer bonus!
Zh: 遥视者加成！

En: Do it for Coach Physical!
Zh: 为了强身健体教练，拼了！

En: The Magnetic Dice Set.
Zh: 磁性骰子套组。

En: All you need to do is close your eyes and concentrate. Darkness *enfolds* you. You can *feel* the distance between the bench and the first rung of the ladder. All you need to is...
Zh: 你只需要闭上双眼，集中精神。黑暗*拥抱*着你。你可以*感觉*到长凳和梯子第一级横档的距离。你只需要…

En: Do it.
Zh: 去做吧。

En: *ZOOT*! *ZAP*! *POW*! *CRINKLE*! It's like magic, you feel yourself disappear, your atoms fading out of existence...
Zh: *嘣*！*咝*！*啪*！*沙沙*！就像魔法一样，你觉得自己消失了，你的原子存在消失了…

En: Oh my god! I can't believe it!
Zh: “天啦！简直难以置信！”

En: *BAM*! You find yourself on the roof... having mastered the art of physical displacement.
Zh: *嗒哒*!你发现自己站在屋顶…已经掌握了物理位移这门技术。

En: I did it, Kim! I teleported!
Zh: “我做到了，金！我传送过来了！”

En: I just *saw* you climb the ladder, the lieutenant shouts from below. "You just climbed it, like a regular person."
Zh: “我刚才*看着*你从梯子爬上去的，”警督在下面叫喊。“你爬上去的，像普通人一样。”

En: The wind at the top of the building starts howling loudly, blowing away the lieutenant's voice... faintly you hear...
Zh: 大厦顶部的风开始大声呼啸，吹走了警督的声音…你只能模糊地听到几个音节…

En: Never mind... find a way to let me in when you get inside! Don't go adventuring without backup, especially if we think the suspect may be hiding here.
Zh: “没关系…你进去之后，想个办法把我弄进去！没有增援的时候，千万不要冒险行事，特别是我们认为嫌疑人可能藏在这里的时候。”

En: Run. To the church. Home. Anywhere but here. Anything but calling her again.
Zh: 快跑。去教堂。回家。去哪里都可以，只要不在这里。做什么都可以，只要不再打给她。

En: Twenty-two million people live in Mirova, the capital city of Graad. It is the capital of the world after Revachol was destroyed.
Zh: 米诺瓦生活着2,200万人，它是格拉德的首都。在瑞瓦肖毁掉之后，它成了这个世界的都城。

En: That's right. Walk away.
Zh: 没错。走开吧。

En: It's over.
Zh: 都结束了。

En: She sighs. "Look, I don't understand what you're saying, or why you're calling me. You seem drunk."
Zh: 她叹了口气。“听着，我不明白你在说什么，也不知道你为什么要打给我。你好像喝醉了。”

En: Smash the headset into the cradle.
Zh: 把听筒重重地砸向支架。

En: No. I can't. I need the pain.
Zh: 不。我不能。我需要痛苦。

En: The cord dangles in the wind like a sickening worm.
Zh: 电话线在风中摇晃，就像一条令人作呕的蠕虫。

En: Static sings inside the phone glued to your ear. Ancient, sticky...
Zh: 电话传来的电流声黏在你的耳朵里。古老，粘腻…

En: Pick up the handset.
Zh: 拿起听筒。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: You hear the tone -- the machine is operable.
Zh: 你听到了声音——这台机器运转正常。

En: In the distorted distance you hear someone turning next to her. Bedsprings rattle.
Zh: 在扭曲的距离里，你听到有人在她身边翻了个身。床上的弹簧在嘎吱作响。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: It didn't give you *any* fun for that money. Nothing. Your hand is turning blue now... blood drips to the ground.
Zh: 那笔钱没有给你带来*任何*欢乐。什么也没有。现在你的手正在变蓝…鲜血滴落在地面上。

En: Calling...
Zh: 呼叫中…

En: End of tone. Someone picks up.
Zh: 呼叫音终止。有人接起了电话。

En: Pierre? Is that you, Pierre? The voice is female and sounds about a hundred years old.
Zh: “皮埃尔？是你吗，皮埃尔？”声音是一位女性，听起来好像有100多岁了。

En: She does not answer. All you hear is hiss.
Zh: 她没有回答。你只听到了嘶嘶声。

En: I might be? What's Pierre like?
Zh: “也许我是？皮埃尔什么样？”

En: Yes, it's me -- Pierre.
Zh: “是的，是我——皮埃尔。”

En: No, this is not Pierre.
Zh: “不，我不是皮埃尔。”

En: So nice of you to find the time to call me! It gets so lonely. Even the animals have died.
Zh: “你总算是找出时间打给我了！实在是太孤独了。就连动物都死了。”

En: There was someone there before. I heard someone. Who's there with you?
Zh: “刚才还有其他人。我听到其他人的声音了。谁跟你在一起？”

En: Do you have any news about Pierre?
Zh: “你有皮埃尔的消息吗？”

En: Who is Pierre?
Zh: “皮埃尔是谁？”

En: He's my sister's grandson. He used to visit me as a lad... Fine young man...
Zh: “他是我姐姐的孙子。他小时候经常来看我…真是个好年轻人…”

En: You're not a revolutionary, Harry... You're drunk.
Zh: “你不是革命家，哈里…你醉了。”

En: Yes, Pierre is dead.
Zh: “是的，皮埃尔死了。”

En: No, I have nothing.
Zh: “不，我什么消息也没有。”

En: But who are you then? A salesman of some sort? Modern goods are rubbish. And I can't afford them anyhow. It's a shame what you did to our country... The woman moans and the phone lines howl in unison with her.
Zh: “那你是谁？推销员？现代商品都是垃圾，反正我也买不起。你做的事，对我们国家来说是一种羞辱…”女人抱怨着，电话线也跟着她一起嚎叫。

En: Dora. The name feels like a *gift*. A gift that was meant for you -- to make it possible to live.
Zh: 朵拉。这个名字像是一件*礼物*。一件为你准备的礼物——让生活能够继续。

En: I'm not a salesman, I was just calling a random number and you somehow ran with it.
Zh: “我不是推销员，我只是随便拨了个号，然后就打到你这里了。”

En: This is because we are bred to be leaders. I can hear the common or mixed background from your voice.
Zh: “那是因为我们是繁衍的领导者。从你的声音里，我可以听出普通或混杂的背景。”

En: Calling...
Zh: 呼叫中…

En: I just so wanted it to be Pierre... Have a pleasant evening, prankster.
Zh: “我太希望是皮埃尔了…祝你度过一个愉快的夜晚，爱搞恶作剧的人。”

En: Calling...
Zh: 呼叫中…

En: Disconnect tone.
Zh: 断开通话。

En: Is someone *there*?
Zh: “有人*在*吗？”

En: Yes, I'm a salesman. What exactly did we do to your country?
Zh: “是的，我是个推销员。我们到底对你的国家做了什么？”

En: You ruined it and you sold it to foreigners. You don't own anything if everything is for sale.
Zh: “你毁了它，把它卖给外国人了。如果什么都能卖的话，那你就一无所有了。”

En: Calling...
Zh: 呼叫中…

En: Give it a thought. Goodbye.
Zh: “你想想吧，再见。”

En: Calling...
Zh: 呼叫中…

En: I don't know who I am... But who are you?
Zh: “我不知道自己是谁…不过，你是谁？”

En: I was a baroness.
Zh: “我是一位女男爵。”

En: Dora. She's still confused. "Who is this? The connection is bad..."
Zh: “朵拉。”她还是有些困惑。“你是谁？信号不太好…”

En: Pause. There is silence on the other end.
Zh: 停顿。电话那头一片寂静。

En: You won't get me and you won't get my money!
Zh: “你不会骗到我的，你也休想得到我的钱！”

En: There's more?
Zh: 还不够吗？

En: Sure you do. No one has anything nowadays.
Zh: “当然没有了。这年头所有人都一无所有。”

En: Calling...
Zh: 呼叫中……

En: You're either a salesman or a statistician of some kind. I am not interested in cooperating.
Zh: “你要么是推销员，要么就是某种统计师。我没兴趣配合。”

En: Calling...
Zh: 呼叫中……

En: No, I'm a police officer.
Zh: “不，我是一名警官。”

En: And calling -- for who knows how long. But no one answers. You need to insert more money to call again.
Zh: 而且呼叫了——天知道有多久。但是没有人应答。要想再打一次的话，你需要再多投些钱。

En: To hell with the police! To hell with you...
Zh: “见鬼去吧，警察！见鬼去吧，你…”

En: What a pity... I'm afraid I won't be coming to the funeral. I.... there... chrysanthemum...
Zh: “真可惜…恐怕我是不能出席葬礼了。我…那个…菊花…”

En: Don't react.
Zh: 不要回应。

En: That's... not good.
Zh: “那还真是…不太好呢。”

En: It gets lonely even when you're with people.
Zh: “就算跟人在一起，也会感觉到孤独。”

En: You dial the number again -- as you've done many times. You remember it well now: 001 -- 41 -- 44 -- 47 -- 11 -- 17 -- 361 -- 451 -- 67 -- 451 -- 451.
What kind of pets did you have?
Zh: “你养的什么宠物？”

En: Don't react. Whatever you do, don't react to that last thing.
Zh: 不要回应。不管你做什么，不要对最后一件事做出回应。

En: Put 10 cents in and dial the long phone number one more time.
Zh: 投入10分硬币，再次拨动那一串很长的号码。

En: Her voice is drowned in white noise. Sounds like waves washing a beach, growing in volume until the call suddenly disconnects.
Zh: 她的声音淹没在白噪音中。听起来就像海浪拍打岸边的声音，越来越响，越来越响，直到突然挂断。

En: Dial a random number -- with your eyes closed.
Zh: 随便拨一个号码——同时闭上眼睛。

En: Caustic echo...
Zh: 腐蚀性的回音…

En: White mourning...
Zh: 白色哀悼…

En: Smells like betrayal...
Zh: 闻起来有背叛的味道…

En: Sorry cop.
Zh: 抱歉警察。

En: Hello.
Silence. The connection falters. You hear her last words repeated back to you in an echo:
Zh: 寂静。信号断断续续。她最后的话语不断在你耳边回响：

En: There's a sigh.
Zh: 一声叹息。

En: You can't hear her reply.
Zh: 你没有听到她的回复。

En: Are you sure you're Pierre? Your voice, it's different... I... there... chrysanthemum...
Zh: “你确定你是皮埃尔？你的声音，不太一样…我…那个…菊花…”

En: Yes. 451 -- 67 -- 451 -- you are going deeper now, into some unknown place. Far away from this island of matter and its telecommunication networks....
Zh: 是的。451——67——451——你正越陷越深，深入某种不知名的地方。远离这个物质的岛屿和它的通讯网络…

En: No.
Zh: “不是。”

En: It doesn't matter if you react or not. You still think you hear a *man's* voice in the background. It's covered in pain and white noise...
Zh: 你是否回应根本不重要。不过，你还是*觉得*自己在背景中听到一个男人的声音。它被痛苦和白噪音掩盖了…

En: A creature is a creature. I wish I was the wind.
Zh: “人是一种生物。我希望自己是风。”

En: This feels wrong. Should you be doing this?
Zh: 这样感觉不对。你应该这么做吗？

En: Still calling...
Zh: 还在呼叫……

En: Hang up. [Leave.]
Zh: 挂断。[离开。]

En: No answer. You're not sure but you think you hear a *man's* voice in the background. It's covered in pain and white noise...
Zh: 没有回应。虽然不能确定，不过你还是*觉得*自己在背景中听到一个男人的声音。它被痛苦和白噪音掩盖了…

En: Keep dialling...
Zh: 继续拨号…

En: This is too much... You need to recede...
Zh: 这样太过了…你需要退后…

En: Disconnect tone.
Zh: 断开通话。

En: You get a sinking feeling. It makes you look if Lieutenant Kitsuragi overheard you. To your relief, he did not.
Zh: 你产生一种沮丧的感觉。感觉曷城警督像是在偷听你一样。令你欣慰的是，他没有。

En: Phone hanging up.
Zh: 电话挂断了。

En: A metal payphone under a yellow plastic dome. You could use it to call someone, unless you're out of change...
Zh: 黄色的塑料圆顶下有一台金属付费电话。你可以用它打给别人，除非你身上没零钱…

En: Phone hanging up.
Zh: 电话挂断了。

En: I need to know who you are. WHO ARE YOU?!
Zh: “我需要知道你是谁。你到底是谁？！”

En: Please do. Just please do.
Zh: “请这样做吧。就这样做吧。”

En: Please. I am going to hang up now, okay?
Zh: “拜托。我现在要挂断了，好吗？”

En: 'Harry please?!' -- I am going to *end* this call, dammit.
Zh: “‘拜托，哈里？！’——我要*挂断*电话了，该死。”

En: Put 10 cents in and dial a random number: 005-24-03-155.
Zh: 投入10分硬币，随机拨一个号码：005-24-03-155。

En: I need answers to my *mystery*.
Zh: “我需要找到自身*秘密*的答案。”

En: Put 10 cents in and dial a random number: 005-51-17-912.
Zh: 投入10分硬币，随机拨一个号码：005-51-17-912。

En: You're naked, aren't you? (Beat the handset against the phone.)
Zh: “你没穿衣服，是吧？”（用听筒敲打电话。）

En: Put 10 cents in and dial a random number: 005-99-77-313.
Zh: 投入10分硬币，随机拨一个号码：005-99-77-313。

En: You're warm.
Zh: “你很温暖。”

En: Oh no... is that you? Her voice sounds like she's waking up now. Still plaintive, tired...
Zh: “哦不…是你吗？”从声音听起来，她现在醒过来了。虽然还是很悲伤，疲惫…

En: Underneath it she is naked and warm under a blanket.
Zh: 在毛毯下面，是一丝不挂又温暖的她。

En: Good bye.
Zh: “再见。”

En: Hello, Girard speaking!
Zh: “喂，我是吉拉德！”

En: Someone with a masculine voice picks up:
Zh: 一个充满男子气概的声音传了过来：

En: Still calling...
Zh: 还在呼叫……

En: Again? Seriously?
Zh: 还来？认真的吗？

En: Calling...
Zh: 呼叫中……

En: Hang up.
Zh: 挂断。

En: Still, let it ring.
Zh: 继续，让它继续响着。

En: 11 -- 17 -- 361 -- your fingers keep moving like a spider, every time the ring rotates back with a little ring of metal, like a bell tolling.
Zh: 11——17——361——你的手指像蜘蛛一样继续移动着，每次转盘带着一个小金属圆环转回来的时候，就会发出铃铛敲响的声音。

En: Hey, Girard, get your wife for me, will you?
Zh: “喂，吉拉德，麻烦帮我叫一下你老婆，好吗？”

En: I want to live -- with you... (Take a sip from the bottle -- your throat is parched all of a sudden.)
Zh: “我想跟你——生活在一起…”（从瓶子里抿上一口——突然间你的喉咙有些干燥。）

En: I want to live -- with you...
Zh: “我想跟你——生活在一起…”

En: Is Alec Tricity there? I need to speak with Alec Tricity, please.
Zh: “是亚力克·崔西吗？麻烦我要找亚力克·崔西。”

En: Girard... what a douche name... Change it -- change your name!
Zh: “吉拉德…这名字好蠢…换了它——你换个名字！”

En: I don't know why I said that.
Zh: “我不知道为什么要这么说。”

En: Sorry, wrong number.
Zh: “抱歉，打错电话了。”

En: Calling...
Zh: 呼叫中……

En: Hang up. [Leave.]
Zh: 挂断。[离开。]

En: Calling...
Zh: 呼叫中……

En: No, but I got a feeling Al Kickurass is gonna make an appearance if you ever call this number again. Have a good one, asshole!
Zh: “不，不过我有种感觉，如果再拨一次这个号码的话，我就会给你点颜色看看。好好享受吧，混账！”

En: Your voice makes me want to turn into dust.
Zh: “你的声音让我想化成灰。”

En: What are you, like five? A woman's voice shouts something in the background and when Girard speaks again his voice is hushed...
Zh: “你是怎么回事，今年5岁吗？”背景里传来一个女人吼叫的声音，当吉拉德再次开口时，他的声音变得更加低沉…

En: Thanks for calling, asshole!
Zh: “谢谢你打过来啊，混账！”

En: Just let it ring.
Zh: 让它就这样响着。

En: Wh-who is this? The man does not understand.
Zh: “你…你是谁？”那个男人没搞清楚状况。

En: Calling...
Zh: 呼叫中……

En: It's Harry, put her on, I don't like waiting.
Zh: “是我，哈里，叫她接电话，我不想等。”

En: It's Pierre, now put her on. I don't like waiting.
Zh: “是皮埃尔，快叫她接电话。我不喜欢等。”

En: Oh no, not this... what time is it?
Zh: “哦不，别又来这个…现在几点了？”

En: I'm sorry it was a stupid joke, I shouldn't have called, it's stupid -- I'm so sorry.
Zh: “抱歉，这只是个愚蠢的玩笑，我不应该打过来的，这样太蠢了——我真的很抱歉。”

En: Pick it up!
Zh: “快接啊！”

En: Don't worry about it, buddy -- happens to all of us. You have a nice day now!
Zh: “别担心，伙计——这种事并不少见。祝你度过愉快的一天！”

En: What? It takes a second for her to realize what you said.
Zh: “什么？”她很快就意识到你在说什么。

En: You shouldn't mess with people like this... His voice is very calm. "You go have a nice day now."
Zh: “你不应该像这样打扰别人…”他的声音非常镇静。“祝你度过愉快的一天！”

En: C'mon, you know why.
Zh: 拜托，你知道为什么。

En: Who the fuck do you think you are talking to me like that? The voice on the phone is suddenly very agitated.
Zh: “你他妈以为自己是谁，居然敢跟我这样说话？”电话里的声音突然间变得很暴躁。

En: Just let it ring.
Zh: 让它继续响着。

En: Your voice is so beautiful.
Zh: “你的声音很美。”

En: I'm the guy who's fucking your wife, Girard. Now put her on before I lose my patience.
Zh: “我是睡了你老婆的人，吉拉德。现在，在我失去耐性之前，赶紧叫她接电话。”

En: Why?
Zh: 为什么？

En: Guillaume. Guillaume le Million.
Zh: “纪尧姆。纪尧姆·列米利翁。”

En: I'm Mazov. Kraz Mazov.
Zh: “我是马佐夫。卡拉斯·马佐夫。”

En: I'm no one, I'm sorry, I shouldn't have done this...
Zh: “我谁也不是，很抱歉，我不应该这么做的…”

En: Calling...
Zh: 呼叫中……

En: Finish it.
Zh: 完成它。

En: Very funny, asshole.
Zh: “很有意思嘛，混账。”

En: It looks like she does not want to pick it up, Harry. Stop scaring her.
Zh: 看来她并不想接电话，哈里。别再吓她了。

En: Very funny, asshole. Why don't you grow up! Oh, and disco is dead!
Zh: “很有意思嘛，混账。你为什么不能成熟点！对了，还有，迪斯科已经死了！”

En: I can't *tell* you who I am. I have a secret plan to win mankind three thousand years of peace on this planet. I have to be ominously vague... (Proceed.)
Zh: “我不能*告诉*你我是谁。我有个秘密计划，要为这颗星球上的人类赢得3000年的和平。我必须含糊其辞…”（继续。）

En: Doesn't matter, put her on, I don't like waiting.
Zh: “不要紧，让她接电话，我不喜欢等。”

En: I am an ultra-high-net-worth individual. I understand how reality works. I have *seen* into it. My mind is clear. (Proceed.)
Zh: “我是一个超级富豪。我明白现实是怎么运作的。我已经*看透*了。我的头脑很清醒。”（继续。）

En: Just tell me what's going on here! Now you hear fear too. The voice gets further away from the microphone, then yells: "Bian, come here!"
Zh: “告诉我到底发生什么事了！”现在你听到了害怕的意味。声音远离了话筒，然后大叫到：“比安，快过来！”

En: I am the last faithful kingsman. I will free the city of Revachol. I will turn back the sea. I will reverse time. (Proceed.)
Zh: “我是最后一名忠诚的卫士。我将会解放瑞瓦肖城。我会让海水逆流。我要让时间倒流。”（继续。）

En: The sounds are muffled. You can make out two people: a man and a woman called *Bian*. They are talking to each other in agitated voices. There's a shuffle of feet, children maybe?
Zh: 声音很含糊。你可以辨认出两个人：一个男人，和一个叫*比安*的女人。两人正在激烈的争吵。还有一阵脚步声传来，也许是小孩？

En: Calling...
Zh: 呼叫中……

En: More shouting. You're pretty sure you hear a man saying *fuck* several times. Then the crackle in the phone line gets violently loud, like the surf breaking.
Zh: 更多尖叫声传来。你很肯定自己听到一个男人说了很多次*去你妈的*。接着，电话线传来的噼啪声越来越响，仿佛惊涛拍岸。

En: You hear a faint *thuck*. You could swear you're hearing furniture falling over.
Zh: 你听见一阵微弱的*嚓声*。你发誓自己听到家具倾倒的声音。

En: Calling...
Zh: 呼叫中……

En: Stop it! I was just kidding, I'm not sleeping with your wife!
Zh: “住手！我只是开玩笑，我没睡你老婆！”

En: Calling...
Zh: 呼叫中……

En: Wait Girard! Let's just keep talking, buddy!
Zh: “等等，吉拉德！我们接着聊啊，伙计！”

En: Get away from her, I'm a cop!
Zh: “离她远一点，我是警察！”

En: Calling...
Zh: 呼叫中……

En: A sigh. She heard you, but she does not hang up. And neither do you. You can't.
Zh: 一声叹息。她听到了你的声音，但是没有挂断。你也没有。你不能这么做。

En: Calling...
Zh: 呼叫中……

En: I'm not going to stop. I'm going to keep fucking your wife, Girard.
Zh: “我是不会停下来的。我还会继续睡你老婆，吉拉德。”

En: Harry, please... A sad voice answers, dressed in distortion.
Zh: “哈里，拜托…”一个悲伤又失真的声音回答着。

En: You can't make out a single word, just vague voices. One more, a child, has joined. Something breaks -- or falls over? Footsteps approaching!
Zh: 你无法辨认出任何词语，只有模糊的声音。还有一个，小孩的声音，也加入了进来。有什么东西碎裂的声音——还是倒塌的声音？有脚步声在接近！

En: Who is this?
Zh: “你是谁？”

En: Mhm, she hums, her voice warm from sleep.
Zh: “嗯，”她嘟囔着，像是刚刚睡醒。

En: What the hell are you doing? The lieutenant has been keeping a distance, but steps closer now.
Zh: “你到底在搞什么鬼？”一直站在远处的警督朝你走了过来。

En: Now walk away. Forget about this. Walk away and do police work. *BURY* this in yourself and move on.
Zh: 现在赶紧离开吧。忘了这一切。现在就离开，去完成警察的本职工作。把这一段*埋葬*在心底，继续前行。

En: What? I'm just having a bit of fun, what's the big deal?
Zh: “什么？我只是想找点乐子，有什么大不了的？”

En: The headset lands in the cradle with a clank.
Zh: 耳机砰的一声落在支架上。

En: You're right, Kim -- it went wrong. Horribly wrong. I shouldn't have done this... (Hang up.)
Zh: “你说得对，金——出问题了。出大问题了。我不该这么做的…”（挂断。）

En: Strange. This is not how you started before.
Zh: 奇怪。你之前不是这样开始的。

En: Let it call!
Zh: 让它继续响着！

En: Something hits the phone with a loud crack of plastic and microphone hiss -- after that you are left with the disconnect tone.
Zh: 某种东西砸中电话，伴随着塑料响亮的破裂声，还有话筒的嘶嘶声——在那之后，通话就断开了。

En: Hang up.
Zh: 挂断。

En: What in the name of god did you just do?
Zh: 你刚才到底做了些什么？

En: That's it. You're caught in a trap. It'll take quite a while for the shit-circus to end now.
Zh: 就这样。你落入圈套了。现在这场闹剧要等好一会儿才会落幕。

En: What the hell are you doing? The lieutenant has been keeping a distance, but steps closer now.
Zh: “你到底在搞什么鬼？”一直站在远处的警督朝你走了过来。

En: No. Let it call. I *must*.
Zh: 不管。让它继续响着。我*必须*这样做。

En: You're right, Kim -- this went wrong, I shouldn't have done this...
Zh: “你说得对，金——出问题了。我不该这么做的…”

En: I was just having a bit of fun, what's the big deal?
Zh: “我只是想找点乐子，有什么大不了的？”

En: 451 -- you have dialled god knows how many numbers. The headset has been waiting silently to relay a signal -- surely nothing can come of this, you think. But it does. A connection.
Zh: 451——天知道这个号码你已经拨过多少次。耳机静静地等待着传送信号——肯定不会有什么结果，你想到。但是确实有。电话接通了。

En: Before it's too late, please.
Zh: 拜托，再晚就来不及了。

En: I don't understand what I did wrong.
Zh: “我不明白自己到底做错了什么。”

En: I'm a revolutionary servant of humanity. I will free mankind and abolish the classes. I will raise the dead. (Proceed.)
Zh: “我是人类的革命公仆。我会解放人类，废除阶级制度。我会让死人复活。”

En: Big deal?! Not a muscle moves in the lieutenant's face.
Zh: “有什么大不了？！”警督脸上的肌肉纹丝不动。

En: You're right, you shouldn't have.
Zh: “你说得对，你不该这么做的。”

En: Let's just go, I've had enough of this phone booth.
Zh: “我们还是走吧，我已经受够这个电话亭了。”

En: You were making *prank calls*. Not a muscle moves in the lieutenant's face.
Zh: “你在打*恶作剧电话*。”警督脸上的肌肉纹丝不动。

En: Put it down.
Zh: 把它放下。

En: Stop calling me, man! Someone picks up. The voice on the other end is slightly hysterical.
Zh: “别再打过来了，伙计！”有人接起了电话。另一头的声音稍微有些歇斯底里。

En: Still calling...
Zh: 还在呼叫……

En: Calling...
Zh: 呼叫中……

En: Who is this?
Zh: “你是谁？”

En: Calling...
Zh: 呼叫中……

En: Calling...
Zh: 呼叫中……

En: Calling...
Zh: 呼叫中……

En: Calling...
Zh: 呼叫中……

En: Calling...
Zh: 呼叫中……

En: I want to die.
Zh: “我想死。”

En: *Still* calling...
Zh: *还在*呼叫中……

En: In the middle of it -- a familiar ring. Small. Distorted.
Zh: 在它中间——是一个熟悉的铃声。轻微。扭曲。

En: Uh... who is this?
Zh: “呃…你是谁？”

En: You dial the number again -- twenty six pulls of the rotary dial. The machine eats the coin and a terrifying ocean of distance rustles in your ear...
Zh: 你再一次拨动那个号码——第26次拨动电话转盘。机器吞下硬币，耳边传来一阵遥远而可怕的沙沙声…

En: Yes, but a slight change of plans -- I want this delivered to the Whirling-in-Rags in Martinaise.
Zh: “可以，但是计划稍微有些改变——我想把这个送到马丁内斯的褴褛飞旋。”

En: We could all be a bit kinder to each other, don't you think? Consider your debt paid, my friend.
Zh: “我们都可以对彼此友好一点，你难道不觉得吗？就当你的债务已经还清了吧，我的朋友。”

En: You seem to be in some sort of trouble. Maybe I can help you, I'm a police officer.
Zh: “你像是惹上什么麻烦了。也许我可以帮你，我是一名警官。”

En: You're not a kingsman, Harry... You're drunk.
Zh: “你不是卫士，哈里…你喝多了。”

En: My *debt?* I don't fucking owe you, it's... The young man realises something. "Who is... ah never mind, I don't have time for this..."
Zh: “我的*债务*？我他妈一个子儿都不欠你的，那是…”年轻人突然意识到了什么。“你是…啊，无所谓了，我没时间陪你玩这个…”

En: Hello. She sounds sleepy.
Zh: “喂。”她的声音昏昏欲睡。

En: The phone gets hanged up fast as lightning, all you hear is a little shuffle of nylon as the hand moves on the other end.
Zh: 电话闪电般地挂断了，你只能听到一点点尼龙摩擦的声音——那是手在电话另一端移动的声音。

En: Tethys, I uh... The young man realises something. "Hey, you're not Tethys! Screw you and don't ever call here again, you're fucking with some *serious* people!"
Zh: “特提斯，我…”年轻人意识到了什么。“等等，你不是特提斯！去你大爷的，如果你再敢打来，我告诉你，我他妈也不是*好惹*的！”

En: There's a moment of silence before the voice speaks again, more calmly: "You got the wrong number, don't call here again."
Zh: 一阵沉默后，声音再度响起，这次更加镇静：“你打错了，别再打过来了。”

En: You're not a high-net-worth individual, Harry... You're drunk.
Zh: “你不是超级富翁，哈里…你醉了。”

En: Amid the howling in the wire you make out a woman's voice with a thick Samaran accent: "Girard... Girard, this is a joke. Someone is making a joke..."
Zh: 在电话线的呼啸声中，你辨认出一个女人的声音，带着浓厚的萨马拉人口音：“吉拉德…吉拉德，这是个玩笑。有人在开玩笑…”

En: You're not ominous, Harry... You're drunk.
Zh: “你不是先知，哈里…你喝醉了。”

En: You just do. And you know it is going to hurt.
Zh: 你刚刚做了。而且你知道这样会很痛。

En: The handset starts slipping from your sweaty palm... your breathing is heavy.
Zh: 听筒从你汗湿的掌心滑落…你的呼吸变得沉重起来。

En: Are we about *done* with this now? The lieutenant has taken a few steps back.
Zh: “我们现在可以*结束*了吗？”警督后退了几步。

En: You close your eyes and put your index finger on the rotary dial, then pull down on the number, then move one up and repeat the motion, twice...
Zh: 你闭上眼睛，把食指放在电话转盘上，拨下一个号码，然后向上移动一格，重复动作，两次…

En: You close your eyes and feel your finger move on the rotary dial. You start with 005, as before...
Zh: 你闭上眼睛，感觉手指在转盘上滑动的感觉。像以前一样，你打算从005开始…

En: Still calling...
Zh: 还在呼叫……

En: Followed by seven digits at random. The last one makes the silence turn into a ring. It's calling...
Zh: 接着是7个随机的数字。当你拨动最后一个数字，电话那头的寂静变成一阵铃声。正在呼叫中…

En: And calling -- for two minutes now. You're out of luck. No one's home there either. But the payphone still has your ten cents in there...
Zh: 呼叫——大概已经持续了2分钟。你运气不好。还是没人在家。不过那10分钱还在付费电话里…

En: Yeah, *bratan*. We've had all the fun there was to have here. And it wasn't much.
Zh: 是啊，*兄弟*。我们在这里玩的很开心。也没有太过。

En: Time keeps passing. In the distance, seagulls squawk -- no one is home at this number. But the coin... it's still in there... you find yourself thinking:
Zh: 时间继续流逝。远方，海鸥在大声尖叫着——这个号码无人应答。但是硬币…它还在里面…你发现自己在想：

En: What were you trying to achieve by closing your eyes? Did you want your muscle memory to take over and call somewhere? Where?
Zh: 你闭上眼睛有什么目的吗？难道想让肌肉记忆全权接管，打去某个地方？又是哪里呢？

En: 41 -- 44 -- 47 --  it's too late now, the movement is almost mechanical.
Zh: 41——44——47——现在已经太迟了，这个动作几乎是机械的。

En: Home?
Zh: 家？

En: Pitiful and terrifying. You feel your pulse rise with each ring...
Zh: 可怜又可怕。你感觉自己的脉搏在随着每一声铃声加速…

En: His own *heart*.
Zh: 他自己的*心里*。

En: It's *really* time to stop this now.
Zh: 现在*真的*应该停下来了。

En: Wait -- what did I just do?
Zh: 等等——我刚才做了什么？

En: Calling...
Zh: 呼叫中……

En: An ultra-long-distance call. Your ear fills with a crackle, the wash of a strange ocean full of white noise. A little bird starts ringing in there, not like the local calling tone before. No, a small ring in a cage of distortion, far away, a distant network of phones...
Zh: 一个超级长途电话。你的耳朵里充斥着噼啪声，还有白噪音的海洋在不断冲刷着。一只小鸟开始在那里歌唱，不像之前听到的本地呼叫音。不，那是扭曲牢笼中一阵微弱的铃声，来自远方，一个遥远的电话网络……

En: Keep dialling...
Zh: 继续拨号…

En: 41 -- 44 -- 47 -- the rotary dial feels cold from the sea air.
Zh: 41——44——47——海风把拨号转盘吹得冰凉。

En: Again you close your eyes -- and again your fingers move on the rotary dial. Ten metallic rings as the dial returns to zero after each number.
Zh: 你再一次闭上双眼——手指再度在拨号转盘上移动。每拨动一个号码后，10个金属圆环就会重新归零。

En: 005 is Revachol ZoC -- 001 is Graad, on the Graadian isola, where the telephone was invented. The next two digits you dial are the area code for the city of Mirova...
Zh: 005是瑞瓦肖的区号——001是格拉德洲的区号——那里就是电话诞生的地方。接下来拨动的两个号码是米诺瓦城的区号…

En: You dialled 001. This is not the area code of Revachol. It is another destination -- on another isola. Some far-off nation state.
Zh: 你拨下001。这不是瑞瓦肖的区号。那是另一个终点——身在另一个洲。某个遥远的国度。

En: Stop!
Zh: 住手！

En: Long windows... how do I know that?
Zh: 长型的窗户…我是怎么知道的？

En: You only have two, maybe three things left to say before the change runs out.
Zh: 在零钱用完之前，你只剩下两次，也许是三次机会。

En: Ringing by the bedside of a dark but capacious apartment with long windows... you know this to be true.
Zh: 电话在一间黑暗宽阔的公寓床边响起，那里的窗户是长型的…你知道这是真的。

En: It didn't give you *any* fun for that money. Your hand is turning blue now... blood drips to the ground.
Zh: 那点钱是不会给你带来*任何*乐趣的。现在你的手正在变蓝…鲜血滴落在地面上。

En: Officer -- that's enough. Let's return to work...
Zh: “警官——够了。我们回去工作吧…”

En: Your hand is swelling up.
Zh: 你的手肿了。

En: Fuck it, it doesn't hurt.
Zh: 该死，它一点都不疼。

En: It didn't give you *any* fun for that money.
Zh: 那点钱是不会给你带来*任何*乐趣的。

En: Calling...
Zh: 呼叫中……

En: Who cares. Run. Go. Don't think about this. Do police work, it's all there is.
Zh: 谁在乎呢。走吧。跑吧。别想了。做回警察的本职工作，仅此而已。

En: That's enough now, officer... The lieutenant stands at a distance.
Zh: “够了，警官…”警督站在远处。

En: Khm... A single *khm* lets you know the lieutenant is ready to move now.
Zh: “咳…”只需一声*咳*，就是让你知道警督已经准备走了。

En: The headset lands in the cradle with a clank. There it sits -- still warm from your hand. You have no idea what just happened.
Zh: 耳机砰的一声落在支架上。它静静坐在那里——上面还残留着你手心的温度。你不知道刚才发生了什么。

En: Put 10 cents in and dial the long phone number again.
Zh: 投入10分硬币，再次拨动那一串很长的号码。

En: No-no... She's waking up now. "It's *you*, isn't it? It's you..."
Zh: “不不…”她现在清醒了。“又是*你*吗？真是你…”

En: You pick up the handset. There's a tone -- the machine is operable.
Zh: 你拿起听筒。里面传来一阵声音——这台机器运转正常。

En: Hang up.
Zh: 挂断。

En: There's still some unused change in there -- the machine waits for a number to be dialled.
Zh: 里面还有一些没用的零钱——机器正在等待拨通号码。

En: A metal payphone under a yellow plastic dome. There's a bitter, caustic taste on the tip of your tongue as you look at the handset.
Zh: 黄色的塑料圆顶下有一台金属付费电话。当你看着听筒的时候，舌尖传来一阵苦涩的味道。

En: Calling still...
Zh: 还在呼叫……

En: You can't. Some strange force is keeping the headset glued to your hand, your ear listening to the ring in the speaker...
Zh: 你做不到。某种奇怪的力量把听筒黏在了你的掌心，强迫你的耳朵倾听扬声器里传出的铃音…

En: Life is garbage.
Zh: 生活就是垃圾。

En: The cold metal is hard and hurts your knuckles. You look at them turning purple.
Zh: 冷金属很坚硬，你的关节有点疼。你看着它们慢慢变紫。

En: The cold metal is hard and your knuckles bleed a little...
Zh: 冷金属很坚硬，你的指关节出了点血…

En: Takes your mind away from that voice, for one second at least...
Zh: 把你的注意力从那个声音上转移开，至少一秒…

En: Phone hanging up.
Zh: 电话挂断了。

En: Disconnect tone.
Zh: 断开通话。

En: Punch it again, the pain is good.
Zh: 再次砸上去，痛苦的感觉很美妙。

En: The cold metal is hard and your knuckles bleed.
Zh: 冷金属很坚硬，你的指关节出血了。

En: Hello, Girard. Technically speaking, you're electricity.
Zh: 喂，吉拉德。从技术的层面上讲，你是电流。

En: That's right. You *can*. Whatever you're doing here, you can just stop it and go on with your life.
Zh: 没错。你*可以*。不管你在做什么，你都可以停下来，继续自己的生活。

En: You want more money now, huh?! YOU WANT FUCKING MONEY?!!!? (Punch the phone again.)
Zh: “你想要多一点钱，是吗？！你他妈想要钱是不是！！！？”（再次击打电话。）

En: No, what *you* are is a *surprise*, get his wife on the phone...
Zh: 不，*你*是一个*惊喜*，让他的妻子来接电话…

En: I don't know who I am. I am an amnesiac. (Proceed.)
Zh: “我不知道我是谁。我是一个失忆症患者。”（继续。）

En: You want my MONEY?! (Punch the phone.)
Zh: “你想要我的钱？！”（击打电话。）

En: What *you* are is a *surprise*, get his wife on the phone...
Zh: *您*是一个*惊喜*，让那个妻子来接电话…

En: Say you're not Pierre... that seems like a better idea. (You don't know why.)
Zh: 说你不是皮埃尔…这个主意好像更好。（你也不知道为什么。）

En: The lieutenant is too far away to hear your yelp. The sea wind blows...
Zh: 警督离得太远，听不见你的叫喊声。海风在呼啸…

En: You're not an amnesiac, Harry... You're drunk.
Zh: “你不是失忆症患者，哈里…你喝醉了。”

En: Kim...
The lieutenant shrugs apathetically, then walks a little distance away to concentrate on his notes. Looks like he'd rather not get into it again.
Zh: 警督冷漠地耸耸肩，然后走远了些，专注做他的笔记去了。看来这次他不想参合进来。

En: Calling in the night....
Zh: 半夜打电话…

En: This has lowered his opinion of you -- but only a little.
Zh: 这让他降低了对你的评价——不过只有一点点。

En: He accepts your apology.
Zh: 他接受了你的道歉。

En: Then the ocean breaks. Out of the depths, a woman's voice emerges. Small. The dearest thing you've ever heard.
Zh: 然后大海分裂开来。从那深处，一个女人的声音浮现出来。很轻，但却是你听过的最亲切的声音。

En: Calling...
Zh: 呼叫中……

En: Calling...
Zh: 呼叫中……

En: I'll get you your money, alright? I just need 'til tonight. Let me work.
Zh: “我会把钱给你的，好吗？只要等到今晚就好。让我先做事。”

En: Calling...
Zh: 呼叫中……

En: The saddest sound in the world.
Zh: 这个世界上最悲伤的声音。

En: He does not seem to be overly thankful of your kindness as he hangs up.
Zh: 他挂断电话时，似乎对你的好意并不是特别感激。

En: Disconnect tone.
Zh: 断开通话。

En: Calling...
Zh: 呼叫中……

En: The area code for Revachol Zone of Control.
Zh: 瑞瓦肖控制区的区号。

En: Put 10 cents in and dial a random number: 005-11-11-313.
Zh: 投入10分硬币，随机拨一个号码：005-11-11-313。

En: Calling...
Zh: 呼叫中……

En: Hang it up.
Zh: 挂断。

En: I'm tired... A man answers, fast this time. His voice is hoarse from cigarettes. You hear typing in the background.
Zh: “我累了…”一个男声响起，这次很快。他的声音因为吸烟而变得沙哑。你听到背景里传来打字的声音。

En: Sounds like he hasn't talked to anyone in quite a while.
Zh: 听起来他有一会儿不会跟人说话了。

En: Calling still...
Zh: 还在呼叫……

En: What are you tired of?
Zh: “你累什么？”

En: I'm tired too.
Zh: “我也累。”

En: Is there anything I can do to help you? I'm with the police.
Zh: “有什么能帮你的吗？我是警察。”

En: Let it call more.
Zh: 让它在多响几声。

En: Writing. I hate writing so much... But I have to get back to it.
Zh: “写作。我真的好恨写作…但是我必须重新捡起来。”

En: You do not hear the customary disconnect tone, just silence in the handset -- the machine is still waiting for you to dial a number.
Zh: 你没有听到通常的断线音，只有一片沉默——机器还在等待你拨动一个号码。

En: The man disappears with a sigh.
Zh: 男人的声音随着一声叹息消失了。

En: If I could go just one month without writing. No, two months... I could regenerate my brain. Fucking liberalism...
Zh: “我可以一个月不写作。不，是两个月…我可以让我的大脑重生。该死的自由主义…”

En: There is nothing.
Zh: “什么也没有。”

En: Lucky you. The call went too fast for the payphone to register. You can still make a new one without paying.
Zh: 你很幸运。电话挂得太快，付费电话还没来得及登记。你还可以免费再打一次电话。

En: Seems like it did not have time to swallow the coin. This sometimes happens.
Zh: 看来它还没来得及吞下那枚硬币。有时候会发生这种事。

En: Both pitiful and terrifying. You feel your pulse rising with each ring...
Zh: 可怜又可怕。你感觉自己的脉搏在随着每一次响铃声加速…

En: Still...
Zh: 还是…

En: He doesn't have a home. Not anymore.
Zh: 他没有家。再也没有了。

En: He doesn't have a home. His home is under a boat.
Zh: 他没有家。他的家在船底下。

En: Is that you?
Zh: “是你吗？”

En: And fight.
Zh: 还有战斗。

En: Harry? How do you know my name?
Zh: “哈里？你怎么知道我的名字？”

En: I'm not drunk.
Zh: “我没喝醉。”

En: Okay I'm drunk, what does it matter? I'm still *me*!
Zh: “好吧，我醉了，那又怎么样？我还是*我*！”

En: I'm gonna get even drunker. (Take a big sip.)
Zh: “那我要喝的再醉一点。”（猛灌一口。）

En: I'm not drunk -- I'm *high*.
Zh: “我没喝醉——我*嗑嗨了*。”

En: Harry? Who's Harry?
Zh: “哈里？哈里是谁？”

En: Harry? Who's Harry -- are you sleeping with him? Is *Harry* there?!
Zh: “哈里？谁是哈里——你跟他睡了吗？*哈里*在那里吗？！”

En: Harry? Who's Harry -- are you sleeping with him? I'm also Harry!
Zh: “哈里？谁是哈里——你跟他睡了吗？我也是哈里！”

En: Because it's me... Look, I don't understand what you're saying or why you're calling me. You seem drunk.
Zh: “因为是我…听着，我不明白你在说什么，也不知道你为什么要打给我。你好像喝醉了。”

En: I don't understand what you're saying.
Zh: “我不明白你在说什么。”

En: I'm not drunk or high, I'm just... hurt... why does it hurt to talk to you?
Zh: “我没有喝醉，也没有嗑药，我只是…很痛…为什么跟你说话会感觉到痛苦？”

En: Oh god... There's silence, it's heavy as tin. The white noise howls.
Zh: “天啦…”一片寂静，沉重如锡。白噪音还在呼嚎。

En: Do you know what time it is? It's so late here... Sounds like she's looking for a clock on the night stand.
Zh: “你知道现在几点了吗？这边现在已经很晚了…”听起来她正在寻找床头柜上的时钟。

En: Hey.
Ooo... are you there?
Zh: “呃…你在吗？”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: It's four o'clock, Harry! I need to wake up in two hours.
Zh: “是4点，哈里！我两个小时之后就得起床了。”

En: Do you want to party?
Zh: “你想参加派对吗？”

En: I want to talk about me. Who am I? You sound like you know me.
Zh: “我想聊聊我的话题。我是谁？听起来你好像认识我。”

En: Where are you? You sound like you're in another world.
Zh: “你在哪？听起来你像是在另一个世界。”

En: You're in Mirova, right?
Zh: “你在米诺瓦，是吗？”

En: No, I want to go to sleep...
Zh: “不，我想睡觉…”

En: What do you want to talk about? That we haven't talked about already...
Zh: “你想说什么？我们还有什么没说过的吗…”

En: I'm in Mirova. Sleeping.
Zh: “我在米诺瓦。正在睡觉。”

En: Yes, I'm in Mirova. Sleeping.
Zh: “是的。我在米诺瓦。正在睡觉。”

En: Catastrophic damage.
Zh: 灾难性的伤害。

En: The Academy? That sounds better than my job. I'm happy.
Zh: “学院？听起来比我的工作好多了。我很高兴。”

En: Pfft, Academy... my job is *real*.
Zh: “切，学院…我的工作更*真实*一些。”

En: I don't know... I don't understand what's happening.
Zh: 我不知道…我不明白出什么事了。

En: You need to stop, Harry. You'll die.
Zh: 你得停下来，哈里。你会死的。

En: You need to stop. Harry. You're killing yourself.
Zh: 你得停下来，哈里。你这是在自杀。

En: Where?
Zh: “哪里？”

En: No response, only a sigh. The connection crackles, like burning paper.
Zh: 没有回应，只有一声叹息。电话线里传来噼啪的声响，就像燃烧的纸张。

En: To work.
Zh: “去工作。”

En: The Academy. Where I work.
Zh: “学院。那是我工作的地方。”

En: Where are you going in two hours?
Zh: “两个小时后你要去哪里？”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: What are you doing to yourself right now?
Zh: 你在对自己做什么？

En: My job is sad and terrible. It has dead bodies in it.
Zh: “我的工作可悲又可怕。而且还会碰到尸体。”

En: I'm making a funny prank call.
Zh: 我在打搞笑的恶作剧电话。

En: Fun? You need to stop. Harry. You're killing yourself.
Zh: 搞笑？你得停下来。哈里。你这是在自杀。

En: Oh no... please stop. Please let's just hang up...
Zh: “哦不…别这样。请挂掉电话…”

En: My heart hurts. I'm gonna have another heart attack.
Zh: “我的心在燃烧。我的心脏病又要发作了。”

En: My heart hurts. I'm gonna have a heart attack.
Zh: “我的心在燃烧。我的心脏病要发作了。”

En: This is bad, you feel your right hand on the handset cramping up with pain...
Zh: 感觉很糟糕，你那握着听筒的右手痛的抽筋…

En: No. We're not gonna do this. Your hand is *not* going to cramp up, not now.
Zh: 不。我们不会这么做的。你的手*不会*抽筋的，现在不会。

En: (Hang up.)
Zh: （挂断。）

En: Your hand isn't moving. The headset hisses in your ear with *evil* sadness.
Zh: 你的手没有移动。听筒在你耳中发出嘶嘶的声响，带着一种*邪恶*的悲伤。

En: Disconnect tone -- the machine ran out of money.
Zh: 断线音——机器没钱了。

En: Is someone there with you?
Zh: “有人跟你在一起吗？”

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: I am the law. I'm a detective. I'm doing a case. There's a hanged man.
Zh: “我就是法律。我是一名警探。我正在查案。有一个男人被吊起来了。”

En: Right now. I'm gonna give you *one* chance.
Zh: 就是现在，我再给你*一次*机会。

En: Push the headset in the cradle with all the strength you have!
Zh: 用尽全身的力量，把听筒挂回支架上。

En: She does not answer anymore.
Zh: 她不再回答。

En: I'm gonna solve it.
Zh: “我会解决案子的。”

En: It doesn't matter. This case doesn't matter.
Zh: “没关系。这个案子不重要。”

En: None of it matters -- not anymore.
Zh: “这些都不重要——再也不重要了。”

En: Can you help me solve it? I need to solve it. They won't take me back if I don't.
Zh: “你能帮我破案吗？我必须把案子破了。如果破不了，他们是不会接纳我的。”

En: Harry...
Zh: “哈里…”

En: You should still take it. It's probably Doom Spiral's.
Zh: 你还是可以拿走它。它很可能是厄运漩涡的。

En: You should still take it.
Zh: 你应该把它拿走。

En: A poet could write a dozen verses and still not begin to capture the profound vexation in that sigh.
Zh: 一个诗人可以写下十几首诗篇，但还是无法捕捉到那声叹息里蕴藏的深厚烦恼。

En: Someone has left an unidentifiable article of clothing on this railing. It smells really bad.
Zh: 有人在栏杆上留下一件无法辨认的衣服。闻起来真的很糟糕。

En: Maybe later. [Leave.]
Zh: 之后再说吧。[离开。]

En: Take a closer look.
Zh: 靠近观察。

En: It's streaked with dried seagull shit and tangled with pieces of seaweed. A dangling arm suggests that there might be a jacket beneath the crust of filth.
Zh: 上面粘着海鸥粪便，还缠绕着一些海草。那只晃晃悠悠的袖子暗示着污秽的外壳下可能是一件夹克衫。

En: It seems likely that it was left in the surf until someone laid it out on this fence to dry out. Unfortunately that just seems to have stiffened it into a shapeless mass.
Zh: 看起来像是被留在了海浪里，直到有人把它捞出来，铺在石凳上晒干。可惜的是，这样似乎让它变成了一团僵硬又不成形的垃圾。

En: Please tell me you're not taking that with you.
Zh: “请告诉我，你不会把它带走。”

En: Why not?
Zh: “为什么不可以？”

En: It's a guano-encrusted jacket. And you're already carrying around enough as it is.
Zh: “那件夹克外面结了一层硬硬的海鸟粪。而且你已经带着不少乱七八糟的东西了。”

En: It might be a clue.
Zh: “这可能是条线索。”

En: I think this is the jacket the Idiot Doom Spiral Guy wanted me to find.
Zh: “我觉得这件夹克，就是那个白痴厄运漩涡想让我找的东西。”

En: The lieutenant looks at the stinking rag and sniffs. "I'm sure he'll be *thrilled* to have it returned."
Zh: 警督看着那块散发着恶臭的破布，稍微嗅了嗅。“我肯定他会很*高兴*找回这个东西的。”

En: A clue? You think our suspect is a seagull who's been defecating on unsuspecting jackets?
Zh: “一条线索？你觉得我们的嫌疑人是一只在毫无防范意识的夹克衫上面排便的海鸥吗？”

En: No.
Zh: “不是。”

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: It could have been multiple seagulls.
Zh: “也许是很多只海鸥。”

En: The lieutenant sighs.
Zh: 警督叹了口气。

En: Me neither.
Zh: “我也不觉得。”

En: The cloth, if you can still call it that, makes a soft crunching sound as you thrust your finger into it.
Zh: 当你用手指去戳这件衣服的时候——如果还能这么称呼它的话——它发出了一阵轻柔的嘎吱声。

En: Much as the thought of touching it causes you to cringe, this could be the jacket that the idiot in the colourful tracksuit was looking for...
Zh: 尽管触碰它的想法会让你想要退缩，这也很可能是那个穿着彩色运动服的白痴在找的东西…

En: This jacket reeks of loss and failure. It could be the one that the idiot in the colourful tracksuit was looking for...
Zh: 这件夹克散发着失败的恶臭。它很可能是那个穿着彩色运动服的白痴在找的东西…

En: Touch it.
Zh: 触碰它。

En: We should also give the Jamrock Library a call, see if we can learn anything about this Billie Méjean.
Zh: “我们还要给加姆洛克图书馆去个电话，看看能不能了解到这位比莉·梅让的信息。”

En: Are you serious? The lieutenant's face changes immediately, taking on a more sombre expression.
Zh: “你认真的吗？”警督的脸色立马变了，表情愈发阴沉。

En: If we take the case then we have to identify the man and take care of the corpse. I can take the body to the morgue on my own or we can ask for the help of the Hardie boys.
Zh: “如果我们接下案子，那就需要确认男人的身份，还要处理好尸体。我可以自己把尸体送到停尸房，或者我们也可以找哈迪兄弟寻求帮助。”

En: Let's leave this case for the station, I don't want to spend any time on it.
Zh: “我们还是把案子留给警局吧，我不想再在上面浪费时间了。”

En: Alright. The station is going to want details, however. Let's make sure we didn't miss anything before we head back to my Kineema to make the call.
Zh: “好吧。不过警局可能会需要我们提供细节。回我的锐影打电话之前，我们最好保证没有遗漏任何东西。”

En: How long has he been dead? Did you *identify* him, search through his pockets? Is he still there?
Zh: “他死去多久了？你有没有确认他的*身份*，口袋搜查过了吗？他还在那个地方吗？”

En: Whoah, easy now... I haven't done anything yet, I wanted to tell you first.
Zh: “哇哦，放松点…我什么都还没做呢，只是想先跟你说一声。”

En: I found a library card from his pockets, but I haven't examined it yet. The body is still there.
Zh: “我在他的口袋里发现了一张借书证，不过还没仔细检查。尸体还在那个地方。”

En: I found a library card from his pockets, issued to someone named Billie Méjean by the Jamrock Public Library. The body is still there.
Zh: “我在他的口袋里发现了一张加姆洛克公共图书馆的借书证，签发给一个名叫比莉·梅让的人。尸体还在那个地方。”

En: The lieutenant nods to himself, thinking. "From where I stand I can see two options. We either take the case or report back to the station and leave it for our colleagues to handle."
Zh: 警督点点头，若有所思。“在我看来有两种选择。要么我们接下这起案子，顺着线索确认尸体身份——或者，汇报给我的警局，把案子交给同事去处理。”

En: Great, we should call the library -- maybe it can provide us with a lead. That is, if we decide to take the case.
Zh: “很好，我们应该打给图书馆——也许那边能提供一些线索。如果我们决定接下案子的话，那就这么办了。”

En: Taking the case would mean that we need to identify the man and take care of the corpse. I can take the body to the morgue on my own or we could ask for the help of the Hardie boys.
Zh: “接下案子意味着我们需要确认男人的身份，还要处理尸体。我可以自己把尸体送到停尸房，或者我们也可以找哈迪兄弟寻求帮助。”

En: But if all of this seems too much of a hassle, then there's also the option of reporting back to the station and leaving the case for our colleagues to solve.
Zh: “不过这一切好像有点太麻烦了，还有一个选择就是汇报警局，把案子交给我们的同事去处理。”

En: Field autopsy isn't necessary if the cause of death doesn't appear to be criminal -- and this looks like a simple accident to me.
Zh: “如果死因不像是犯罪行为导致的话，那就没必要现场验尸——而且在我看来，这就是一起简单的事故。”

En: Hold on -- what about field autopsy?
Zh: “等等——那现场验尸呢？”

En: I'd say we should just write down 'head trauma' in the autopsy report and leave it at that. It would save us at least two hours of unnecessary work.
Zh: “要我说，我们应该在验尸报告里写下‘头部创伤’，然后就到此为止。这样至少可以节省两个小时不必要的工作。”

En: Yes, but... isn't that kind of sloppy?
Zh: “是啊，但是…这样是不是有点太草率了？”

En: You're right, I don't see anything criminal here. Let's leave it at that.
Zh: “你说得对，我没发现什么犯罪行为。这个话题就到此为止吧。”

En: Maybe, but we don't really have that much time or resources to spare. Still, if you want to go ahead -- be my guest.
Zh: “也许，不过我们真的没有那么多时间，也没有那么多资源可以浪费。不过，如果你想接的话——那就请便吧。”

En: Good call.
Zh: “说得好。”

En: Let's make sure we didn't miss anything -- especially any identifying information -- before we go.
Zh: “在我们离开之前——先确认没有遗漏任何东西——特别是任何身份信息。”

En: I want us to solve the case completely on our own.
Zh: “我想让我们俩全权负责这起案件。”

En: Alright. We're still going to have to call the station to let them know we're taking on an additional case, and so they can take away the body.
Zh: “好吧。不过我们还是要打给警局，让他们知道我们准备接下额外的案子，这样他们就能把尸体带走。”

En: The situation looks bad. Calm yourself. Steady your breathing.
Zh: 情况看起来很糟糕。冷静下来。稳住呼吸。

En: You'll have to go for the gun sooner or later... perhaps you can learn crucial facts before you do?
Zh: 你迟早都要把枪拿回来…也许在这之前，你可以先了解一些重要事实？

En: Sometimes when things get very bad it's good to remind yourself that you have a body. By scratching it.
Zh: 当情况变得非常糟糕的时候，提醒你自己还有一具身体是很不错的。只要抓一抓就可以。

En: Just lower the gun, officer.
Zh: “把枪放下，警官。”

En: This is dangerous. You're 70% certain you always leave your gun loaded.
Zh: 这很危险。你有70%的肯定，你的枪应该是上膛的。

En: Is one of those things a... police cap?
Zh: 那里面是不是有一顶…警察帽？

En: It's hard to say. It's been so long since you wore yours.
Zh: 很难说。你已经很久没戴了。

En: She didn't consume them. She didn't look high. She 'confiscated' them, a little like you are doing now.
Zh: 她没有用过。她看起来没有嗑嗨。她‘没收’了它们，跟你现在做的有点相似。

En: There were narcotics in there too. You're thinking of taking them? Do!
Zh: 那里面还有麻醉剂。你想拿走它们吗？那就去吧！

En: He's grateful you did this.
Zh: 他很感激你能这么做。

En: Listen to him. For once.
Zh: 听他的。哪怕就这一次。

En: Magnesium, drouamine, maybe even  speed... My god, she has speed on her? What else?
Zh: 镁片，多巴宁，也许还有思必得……我的天啦，她身上有思必得？还有别的吗？

En: That's it, you're under arrest!
Zh: “没错，你被捕了！”

En: You're making it worse, officer, the lieutenant says quietly. His eyes never leave the woman's face.
Zh: “你把情况搞得更糟糕了，警官，”警督小声说着，眼神一直停留在女人的脸上。

En: Recent. Self-inflicted.
Zh: 近期的。自残。

En: Hands on the...?
Zh: 手放在哪…？

En: Kim?
I hear you, ma'am, look... (Start slowly bending over.) "...hands going on the ground."
Zh: “我听见了，女士，你看…”（慢慢弯下腰。）“…我把手放在地上。”

En: I *just* want to help you, that's all.
Zh: “我*只想*帮你，仅此而已。”

En: We *just* want to help, that's all.
Zh: “我们*只想*帮你，仅此而已。”

En: SUSPECT AT LARGE ON FOOT, she yells in the megaphone. "PIGS IN PURSUIT, CUFF HIM, BOYS!"
Zh: “嫌疑人徒步在逃，”她对着一个扩音器大喊。“猪猪女侠正在追踪，把他拷起来，小伙子们！”

En: That's right, Pigs, give me the gun. It will be alright. It's mine.
Zh: “没错，猪猪女侠，把枪给我。没事的。它是我的。”

En: Yeah, back her up, right up against those boxes and do some sex-healing.
Zh: 是啊，撑住她，就靠在那些箱子上，然后进行性治疗…

En: STOP! For the love of god, don't.
Zh: 住手！看在老天爷的份上，不要。

En: Confirmed. Don't listen to your glands right now. Listen to me. The lady asked, if you're the backup?
Zh: 确认完毕。现在不要听信你的腺体。听我的。这位女士问你，你是不是增援人员？

En: Rat squad? The question is addressed to herself more than you. "But I thought... But Mr. Morrand..." The megaphone raises and she screams: "AGGRAVATED ASSAULT, MAN DOWN, SUSPECT ON FOOT!"
Zh: “老鼠小队？”这个问题更像是留给她自己的，而不是你。“但是我以为…但是莫兰德先生…”她举起扩音器，大喊到：“加重攻击罪，有人倒下，嫌疑人徒步逃脱！”

En: No, the crazed woman mumbles, shaking her head. "No, no, no... I though Mr. Morrand... Gareth..." Suddenly she raises the megaphone and screams: "AGGRAVATED ASSAULT, MAN DOWN, OFFICERS IN PURSUIT!"
Zh: “不，”那个疯女人摇摇头，嘴里念念有词。“不，不，不，我以为莫兰特先生…加雷斯…”她突然举起扩音器，大喊到：“加重攻击罪，有人倒下，警官开始追踪！”

En: There's a scenario unfolding in her head right now. It has nothing to do with what's happening here.
Zh: 现在，一个剧本正在她的脑海里慢慢展开，不过跟这里真正发生的事一点关系都没有。

En: LAW ENFORCEMENT COMPROMISED, she screams in the megaphone. Red and blue lights illuminate the spit flying everywhere. "IMPERSONATING A POLICE OFFICER!"
Zh: “执法人员暴露了，”她对着扩音器大喊。红蓝相间的灯光照射出四处飞溅的唾沫星子。“冒充警官！”
