En: Does she not believe you two are actually police officers?
Zh: 难道她不相信你们俩真是警官？

En: Does she not believe you're actually a police officer?
Zh: 难道她不相信你真是警官？

En: Wait, what if she's right and I actually *am* an imposter?
Zh: “等等，如果她是对的，我其实*真是*个冒名顶替的骗子呢？”

En: Hey, you're the one impersonating a police officer!
Zh: “喂，你才是冒充警官的人好吗！”

En: I really am a cop. Look... my badge. (Take out your badge and show it to her.)
Zh: “我是真警察。看……这是我的警徽。”（把你的警徽拿给她看。）

En: We really are cops. Look, my badge... (Take out your badge and show it to her.)
Zh: “我们是真警察。看，这是我的警徽…”（把你的警徽拿给她看。）

En: RESISTING ARREST! CONFINED QUARTERS! DROP YOUR HEAD! She bashes the megaphone against her head. Repeatedly. Then screams in the bloody mouthpiece: "COME IN DISPATCH! COME IN FUCK! CIVILIAN ENGAGEMENT!!!"
Zh: “拒捕！封锁现场！把头低下！”她用扩音器用力地砸向自己的头，一遍又一遍。接着，她对着被血染红的扩音器喊到：“紧急情况！操，快回话！平民卷入案件！！！”

En: LICENCE AND REGISTRATION!!! She repeatedly bashes the megaphone against her head, then screams in the bloody mouthpiece: "LICENCE AND REGISTRATION! COME IN DISPATCH! SECTOR, SECTOR, AZIMUTH!!!"
Zh: “证件和登记号！！！”她反复用扩音机砸向自己的头，然后对着被血染红的扩音器大喊：“证件和登记号！紧急情况呼叫！分部，分部，报告方位！！！”

En: Big red key? That's code for the battering ram. Cop talk. You know this.
Zh: 大红钥匙？那是攻城槌的代号。警察的行话。你知道这个。

En: We really are cops. Look, white rectangles!
Zh: “我们真的是警察。看，白色矩形！”

En: Now... the lieutenant takes a deep breath and steadies his voice, "... is not the time."
Zh: “现在…”警督深吸一口气，然后稳住自己的声音，“…时机不对。”

En: Eee... I'm in full RCM lieutenant's uniform and look at my partner. We're cops alright.
Zh: “呃…我穿着全套的RCM警督制服，而且看看我的搭档。我们真是警察。”

En: Eee... I'm in full RCM lieutenant's uniform. Trust me. I'm a cop.
Zh: “呃…我穿着全套的RCM警督制服。相信我，我是个警察。”

En: Look, ma'am... (Point to your clothes.) "White rectangles. I *am* a cop."
Zh: “看，女士…”（指着自己的衣服。）“白色矩形。我*是*个警察。”

En: She's losing it -- one twitch and there will be blood.
Zh: 她要失去控制了——再来一下肯定会见血。

En: In this moment of hesitation she almost doesn't seem disturbed, but like someone suddenly waking from a deep sleep.
Zh: 在这犹豫的片刻，她似乎没有任何不安，更像是一个从深度睡眠中突然惊醒的人。

En: That's *the* gun, man! That's *your* gun. Please get it back!
Zh: 就是*那支枪*，伙计！那是*你的*枪。一定要把它拿回来！

En: It's okay, ma'am. Everything's gonna be alright.
Zh: “没事的，女士。一切都会好起来的。”

En: But... Her eyes fill with tears, then suddenly she slams the megaphone against her lips and teeth. Blood gushes down her chin, but she doesn't notice it.
Zh: “但是…”她的眼中饱含泪水，然后突然用扩音器猛砸自己的嘴唇和牙齿。鲜血沿着她的下巴滴落，但她根本没有注意到。

En: Too late, lieutenant.
Zh: 太迟了，警督。

En: Get the gun, lieutenant, it's okay. It's safe, he thinks. Just get it.
Zh: 拿走枪，警督，没事的。很安全，他想到。直接去拿吧。

En: Just trust me and go for the gun.
Zh: 相信我，去拿枪吧。

En: Okay, she's actually *more* agitated now. My bad.
Zh: 好吧，现在她确实变得*更*焦虑了。我的错。

En: Loneliness.
Zh: 孤独。

En: Yes, sure. But also -- in a less abstract way -- the neurodegenerative disease I mentioned earlier.
Zh: 是的，当然。不过——以一种不那么抽象的方式——我之前提过的神经变性疾病。

En: Officer... The lieutenant shakes his head and adds: "There is *no* way I'm leaving it like this."
Zh: “警官…”警督摇摇头，补充到：“我是*绝对不会*就这样放手不管的。”

En: You're right, of course. Let's do this!
Zh: “没错，你说得对。我们动手吧！”

En: Suit yourself, but *I* need to decompress first. (Turn to leave.)
Zh: “随便你，但是*我*要先减减压。”（转身离开。）

En: The lieutenant nods. His eyes stay on the swaying firearm.
Zh: 警督点点头，目光停留在晃荡的枪支上。

En: Again, a neurodegenerative disease.
Zh: 再说一次，是神经变性疾病。

En: It's not a code. Just disjointed words.
Zh: 这不是代码。只是些杂乱无章的词句。

En: Mine? No. It's ours, the RCM's.
Zh: “我的？不，是我们的，RCM的。”

En: So that's how Cunoesse must have felt, when you pointed the gun at her.
Zh: 所以当你拿枪指着坤妮斯的时候，她一定就是这种感受。

En: So that's how Cunoesse must have felt... right before you fired a shot at her.
Zh: 所以这就是坤妮斯的感觉…就在你向她开枪之前。

En: Put your hands where I can see them, the ageing woman under a mountain of police paraphernalia mumbles to herself, then notices you and reaches for the megaphone...
Zh: “把手放在我能看见的地方，”一个身上堆满警察装备的老妇人正在喃喃自语，接着，她注意到了你，把手伸向扩音器的方向。

En: Doesn't look good. Let's deal with this later. [Leave.]
Zh: “看起来不太妙啊。等会再过来吧。”[离开。]

En: Nope, not right now. [Leave.]
Zh: 不，现在不行。[离开。]

En: We can solve this peacefully, please lower the weapon.
Zh: “我们可以和平解决问题的，请放下武器。”

En: Ma'am, we're here to help. Tell us what's wrong.
Zh: “女士，我们是来帮忙的。告诉我们，出什么事了。”

En: Ma'am, I'm here to help. Tell me what's wrong.
Zh: “女士，我是来帮忙的。告诉我，出什么事了。”

En: COMPLY, OR I WILL LIGHT YOU THE FUCK UP! she continues to yell as she lumbers about.
Zh: “照我说的做，不然我他妈就把你点燃！”她一边缓慢地移动着，一边对你大声叫喊。

En: Madam! Please drop the firearm immediately!
Zh: “女士！请立即放下武器！”

En: We can't leave. A delirious civilian is pointing a firearm at us. *Your* firearm. You *need* to solve this.
Zh: “我们不能离开。一个神志不清的市民正拿武器对着我们。*你的*武器。你*必须*解决这个问题。”

En: The woman looks at you, but through you. Like you don't exist. Her eyes gleam feverishly and the rotating police-beacon lights reveal deep scratch marks on her cheeks.
Zh: 女人看着你，但却穿过了你。就好像你根本不存在。她的眼睛里闪烁着兴奋的光，警灯旋转的光映照出她脸上的划痕。

En: Raise your hands.
Zh: 举起你的手。

En: Easy, ma'am, take it easy...
Zh: “放松，女士，放松点…”

En: Failure to comply. Suspect is displaying aggression! OFFICER UNDER DURESS! OFFICER UNDER DURESS!
Zh: “未服从命令。嫌疑人表现出攻击行为！警官受到胁迫！警官受到胁迫！”

En: Time to get my gun!
Zh: 是时候找回我的枪了！

En: This is the Pigs.
Zh: 这就是猪猪女侠。

En: Rogue cop on the loose.
Zh: 逍遥法外的流氓警察。

En: A peaceful solution.
Zh: 和平的解决方案。

En: Suspect is agitated.
Zh: 嫌疑人情绪很激动。

En: Suspect is *really* agitated.
Zh: 嫌疑人的情绪*真的*很激动。

En: Flashed your piece.
Zh: 亮出你的东西。

En: Back-up? She lowers the megaphone half-way, but immediately raises it again and screams: "BACK-UP, BACK-UP! STATUS UNCONFIRMED!"
Zh: “增援？”她略微放低手中的扩音器，随后又立即举了起来，大声叫喊到：“增援，呼叫增援！身份未确认！”

En: You're not the police. *We* are the police.
Zh: “你不是警察。*我们*才是警察。”

En: You're not the police, *I* am the police. I am the Law!
Zh: “你不是警察。*我*才是警察。我就是法律！”

En: You're not the police, *we* are the police. We are the Law!
Zh: “你不是警察。*我们*才是警察。我们就是法律！”

En: You're not the police. *I* am the police.
Zh: “你不是警察。*我*才是警察。”

En: IT'S THE GODDAMN POLICE, SHIT-BAG, she yells into the megaphone. "HUG THE PAVEMENT, YOU'RE UNDER ARREST!"
Zh: “我他妈是警察，混球，”她对着扩音器大喊。“趴在路面上，你被捕了！”

En: Please identify yourself, ma'am?
Zh: “请证明自己的身份，女士？”

En: You shouldn't be here. Something's very wrong with her. She's completely out of control.
Zh: 你不应该在这里。她好像很有问题，已经完全失去控制了。

En: Scavenged battery-powered police lights protrude from her back. The flickering light-show reveals a gun in her shaking hand.
Zh: 电池供电的警灯在她身后十分突出。闪烁的灯光映照出她颤抖的双手，还有手中的那把枪。

En: Officer, Pigs! Double-yefreitor Harrier Du Bois, requesting your sidearm for inspection. (Hold out your badge.)
Zh: “警官！我是双重荣誉警督哈里尔·杜博阿，请求检查你的配枪。”（拿出你的警徽。）

En: Patrol officer Pigs, I need your gun for inspection.
Zh: “巡警，我需要检查你的枪。”

En: Ma'am, please... The lieutenant is trying to maintain eye-contact. "We want to help you, but you need to lower that weapon."
Zh: “女士，请冷静…”警督试着保持眼神接触。“我们想帮你，但你需要放下武器。”

En: See? He glances at you. "This is what happens, when we're not around."
Zh: “看见了吗？”他瞥了你一眼。“我们不在的时候，就发生了这种事。”

En: She thinks she's a police officer... Try treating her like a police officer. A *lower-ranking* police officer.
Zh: 她觉得自己是个警察…试着把她当成警官对待。一个*级别较低*的警官。

En: Patrol officer, I have to sign you up for a disciplinary hearing... (Slowly shake your head.)
Zh: “巡警，我需要你去参加纪律听证会…”（慢慢摇头。）

En: Show me your hands. This is the Pigs. Show me your hands *RIGHT NOW*, she screams in the megaphone. "SHOW ME YOUR HANDS RIGHT NOW!"
Zh: “举起手来。我是猪猪女侠。*马上*把手举起来，”她对着扩音器大喊。“现在把手举起来！”

En: Superseding event! Split-second decision! Get on the ground! GET ON THE GODDAMN GROUND!
Zh: “替代事件！瞬间决定！趴下！他妈的给我趴下！”

En: Disturbance reported, authorize deadly force. SECTOR, TAKE THE SHOT! Her head snaps at you. "BIG RED KEY, BIG RED KEY!"
Zh: “骚扰报告，授权使用致命武力。分部，开枪！”她的脑袋朝你猛撞。“大红钥匙，大红钥匙！”

En: OFFICER IN NEED OF ASSISTANCE! Her eyes dart between you and Kim. "SUSPECT AT LARGE, GET ON THE GROUND!"
Zh: “警官呼叫增援！”她的眼睛在你和金之间飞快扫过。“发现被通缉嫌疑人，趴下！”

En: Wait, what should we do, Kim?
Zh: “等等，我们应该怎么办，金？”

En: This is bad, the lieutenant whispers. "Getting your weapon back is the priority. We can't have an unhinged civilian running around with a gun."
Zh: “情况不妙，”警督低语到。“需要优先考虑的是怎么把你的武器拿回来。我们不能让一个神志不清的市民带着枪到处跑。”

En: I *am* the police! Don't move! DON'T MOVE! Hands on your head! SUSPECT IS ARMED AND DANGEROUS!
Zh: “我*是*警察！别动！不要动！双手抱头！嫌疑人有武器，十分危险！”

En: What's the situation?
Zh: “现在是什么情况？”

En: Let's just talk, alright?
Zh: “我们谈谈，好吗？”

En: Blow her away, lieutenant!
Zh: “杀了她，警督！”

En: You're making it worse, officer, the lieutenant says quietly. His eyes never leave the woman's face.
Zh: “你把情况搞得更糟糕了，警官，”警督小声说着，眼神一直停留在女人的脸上。

En: The lieutenant's eyes stay fixed on the woman and her gun -- he studies them closely, then mumbles: "Fascinating."
Zh: 警督的眼睛死死盯着女人和她手中的枪——他仔细地观察了一下，然后嘟囔到：“太好了”。

En: His shoulders relax and a look of realization appears in his eyes... Did he notice something?
Zh: 他的肩膀放松下来，眼睛里流露出几分顿悟的意味…他是注意到什么了吗？

En: THIS IS THE POLICE! She howls through her megaphone. "UNLAWFUL ENGAGEMENT. HANDS ON THE GROUND, SCUM-BAG!"
Zh: “我是警察！”她对着扩音器大声叫喊。“非法接触！趴下，你们这些人渣！”

En: Sir... She hesitates, looking around in confusion. The three barrelled pepperbox wavers in her hand.
Zh: “先生…”她有些犹豫，困惑地看着四周。随后放下了手中的三管胡椒盒手枪。

En: Wh... what? She lowers the megaphone and stares at the gun in her hand.
Zh: “什…什么？”她放下扩音器，盯着手中的枪。

En: Patrol officer, you're in gross violation of the RCM Code of Conduct.
Zh: “巡警，你严重违反了RCM的行为准则。”

En: Please, Pigs, just give me the gun.
Zh: “麻烦你，猪猪女侠，把枪给我。”

En: The woman stands, slumped. She looks catatonic under her mountain of RCM paraphernalia.
Zh: 女人颓然地站着。裹在一堆RCM装备下的她，看起来像个紧张性精神病患者。

En: Her hand is trembling from some sort of neurodegenerative disease.
Zh: 她的手因为某种神经变性疾病不停地颤抖。

En: The man appears to be pulling out an object from his pocket! Her eyes fill with terror. "FEAR UNDER THREAT! FEAR UNDER THREAT!"
Zh: “男人好像要从口袋里掏东西！”她的眼神里满是恐惧。“害怕受到威胁！害怕受到威胁！”

En: Her eyes bulge with terror. Veins protrude on her forehead.
Zh: 她吓得瞪大了双眼，前额青筋爆出。

En: I wasn't taking anything out of my pocket.
Zh: “我没有从口袋里掏东西。”

En: Ma'am, we just wanna talk to you.
Zh: “女士，我们只想跟你谈谈。”

En: Ma'am, I just wanna talk to you.
Zh: “女士，我只想跟你谈谈。”

En: GET ON THE GROUND! I WANT YOU ON THE PAVEMENT RIGHT NOW! THIS IS THE PIGS!
Zh: “趴下！现在马上给我趴在地上！我是猪猪女侠！”

En: Subject yourself to a FULL BODY CAVITY SEARCH! PROHIBITED MATERIALS CONCEALED IN NOSTRILS, EARS, MOUTH, NAVEL, URETHRA, FORESKIN, VAGINA, AND RECTUM!
Zh: “需要进行全面体腔检查！违禁物质可能隐藏在鼻孔、耳朵、嘴巴、肚脐、泌尿管、下体、还有直肠！”

En: I'm not moving.
Zh: “我没动。”

En: Ma'am, I need you to calm down. We just want to talk.\n
Zh: “女士，我需要你冷静。我们只想和你谈谈。”

En: Ma'am, I need you to calm down. I just want to talk.
Zh: “女士，我需要你冷静。我只想和你谈谈。”

En: SUSPECT IS ON THE LOOSE! SUSPECT AT LARGE! Officer administers street justice at her own peril!
Zh: “嫌疑人在逃！在逃嫌疑犯！警官维护街头正义，自负一切后果！”

En: LATERAL VASCULAR NECK RESTRAINT! CAROTID SLEEPER! CAROTID SLEEPER! Critically reducing blood from passing through the neck of the suspect!
Zh: “按住侧颈部血管！扼喉！扼喉！嫌疑人颈部流通血液严重减少！”

En: THAT ONE EXTRA LICK WITH A BATON OR EXTRA KNEE STRIKE ABOVE WHAT WAS ACTUALLY REQUIRED! SUSPECT IS UNDER CONTROL! SUSPECT IS UNDER CONTROL!
Zh: “使用警棍或者膝盖进行额外打击已经超出实际需求！嫌疑人已被控制！嫌疑人已被控制！”

En: In her mind you're under control now, the next moment you're not. It appears the things she says are very real to her, as long as she's saying them.
Zh: 在她的脑海里，现在你已经被控制住了，但是下一刻又没有。看来只要是她自己说出来的，那就是此刻正在发生的事情。

En: Be careful, detective. Don't do anything that might set her off.
Zh: “小心，警探。不要做出任何激怒她的事。”

En: In her mind the cavity search is happening, as long as she's saying it. It appears the things she says are very real to her.
Zh: 只要她这么说，那就代表她的脑海里正在进行体腔搜查。看来对于她来说，自己口中说出的话就是真实发生的事情。

En: I got a gun too! Looks like it's a gun-off! (Cock *both* barrels.)
Zh: “我也有枪！看来要进行射击对决了！”（竖起枪管。）

En: Careful, I'm packing fire too... (Flash your piece.)
Zh: “小心，我也在填充弹药了…”（亮出你的东西。）

En: The woman stands, slumped. She looks catatonic under her mountain of RCM paraphernalia. Nothing more to do here.
Zh: 女人颓然地站着。裹在一堆RCM装备下的她，看起来像个紧张性精神病患者。这里没什么可以做的了。

En: Shake her shoulder.
Zh: 摇晃她的肩膀。

En: Pick up the RCM cap.
Zh: 捡起RCM帽子。

En: You're not getting the porta-reel...
Zh: “录音机是不会给你的……”

En: It's just a standard flashlight, ma'am. Calm down.
Zh: “这是个普通的手电筒，女士。请冷静一点。”

En: The sabre is only decorative. It's not a threat to you.
Zh: “军刀只是个装饰，对你来说不构成威胁。”

En: I don't feel comfortable being separated from my multitool.
Zh: “跟我的多用工具分开感觉不太舒服。”

En: If you want the prybar you're gonna have to... pry it from me.
Zh: “如果你想要撬棍的话，那你必须…从我手里抢走。”

En: No! The tare is mine. I collected it.
Zh: “不！回收品是我的，都是我收集的。”

En: Ma'am... I got a gun.
Zh: “女士…我有枪。”

En: Easy, now. They're just chaincutters.
Zh: “放松一点。这只是切链钳而已。”

En: No! I need this ledger to take notes.
Zh: “不！我需要这本手册做笔记。”

En: As she waves her hands, you notice familiar-looking ampoules and packets sticking out the mountain of police gear on her back. Medicine? Or drugs?
Zh: 当她挥手时，你注意到很眼熟的安瓿和小包，在她背后山一样高的警用装备中格外显眼。药品？还是毒品？

En: Are you on drugs?
Zh: “你嗑药了吗？”

En: CONFISCATED CONTRABAND! The megaphone makes her voice almost painfully metallic. "RESTRICTED ACCESS, TWO KILOS MISSING, EYE-WITNESS REPORT COMPROMISED!"
Zh: “没收违禁品！”扩音器让她的声音充满痛苦的金属质感。“封闭通道，丢失2公斤，目击证人暴露！”

En: SUSPECT IS ARMED AND DANGEROUS! REQUESTING IMMEDIATE BACK-UP! She's now panting heavily. "RESISTING ARREST, BREAKER BREAKER, CUT HIM OFF!"
Zh: “嫌疑人携带武器，非常危险！请求立即增援！”她开始重重地喘气。“拒捕，请求打断，打断他的话！”

En: DON'T MOVE! She seems to grit her teeth. "HANDS ON YOUR HEAD, HUMAN SHIELD! HANDS ON YOUR FUCKING HEAD!"
Zh: “不要动！”她似乎咬紧了牙关。“双手抱头，呼叫人盾！赶紧他妈的把手抱在头上！”

En: Wait, officer! the lieutenant shouts. "We're trying to *de*-escalate the situation! Adding more guns just makes it worse."
Zh: “等等，警官！”警督大叫到。“我们*不能*让眼前的紧张事态加剧！增加枪支只会让情况变得更糟。”

En: There has to be a way to resolve this situation peacefully.
Zh: 一定能找到和平解决这种情况的办法。

En: Confiscate the narcotics. (Wink!)
Zh: 没收麻醉剂。（眨眼！）

En: Leave her be. [Leave.]
Zh: 不管她。[离开。]

En: You take the phial of pyrholidon and the bottle of speed. As evidence, obviously.
Zh: 你拿走一瓶吡嚯浣酮，还有一瓶思必得。显然是拿来当证物的。

En: She doesn't even flinch as you reach out and disentangle the familiar-looking lieutenant's cap from her mountain of RCM paraphernalia.
Zh: 当你伸出手，从她身上那堆RCM警用品上取下相当眼熟的警督帽时，她完全没有一丝畏惧。

En: The old woman doesn't react to your touch.
Zh: 老妇人对你的碰触没有反应。

En: Oh. Is that yours?
Zh: “哦，这是你的吗？”

En: I think so.
Zh: “我想是的。”

En: Maybe. Probably. You know me.
Zh: “也许。可能吧。你懂我的。”

En: The lieutenant nods.
Zh: 警督点点头。

En: It's your hat. The lieutenant nods.
Zh: 警督点点头。“这是你的帽子。”

En: The lieutenant coughs. "You're taking those, are you?"
Zh: 警督咳了一声。“你打算把那些东西拿走，是吧？”

En: I'm *confiscating* these. As evidence.
Zh: “我要*没收*这些东西，作为证据。”

En: I need to do this case. I need everything I can get.
Zh: “我需要这个案子。我需要我所能得到的一切。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: I guess I got my answer. The lieutenant gives you his usual firm stare.
Zh: “我猜我知道答案了。”警督像往常一样死死地盯着你。

En: Right. The lieutenant gives you his usual firm stare. Then sighs. "Okay."
Zh: “好吧。”警督像往常一样死死地盯着你。“好吧。”

En: Sure. The lieutenant gives you his usual firm stare. "I guess that's fine."
Zh: “当然。”警督像往常一样死死地盯着你。“我猜应该还好吧。”

En: Fine, you take them then.
Zh: “好吧，那就交给你了。”

En: Thank you. He pockets the drugs. "I'm doing this to help you. We need to focus on the case."
Zh: “谢谢。”他把毒品装进口袋。“我这样做是为了帮你。我们必须把精力都放在这个案子上。”

En: You might not want to take those. The lieutenant states firmly. "You seem to have a slight... problem..."
Zh: “你可能还是别拿走它们的好。”警督死死地盯着你。“你好像有一点点…小问题…”

En: Doesn't want to hurt her?! This is flight or fight -- do or die.
Zh: 不想伤害她？！要么逃跑，要么战斗——不是生，就是死。

En: What happened to make her like this?
Zh: 到底发生了什么，让她变成现在这个样子？

En: Enough! Fry me some bacon!
Zh: 够了！给我煎点培根！

En: There's no way you can take aim and fire before she gets a shot off.
Zh: 你不可能抢在她之前瞄准开枪的。

En: So this is what the weather's like on the other end of a cop's gun?
Zh: 所以站在警枪的另一端感觉怎么样？

En: I don't think she's on drugs, the lieutenant whispers. "Being *off* drugs might actually be the problem here..."
Zh: “我不觉得她嗑药了，”警督低语到。“*没*嗑药可能才是问题…”

En: Yes, I'm the back-up. (Resist a yawn.) "Here to back you up."
Zh: “没错，我就是增援。”（抑制住打哈欠的冲动。）“我是来支援你的。”

En: Baby, you know who I am -- everybody knows. (Wink.)
Zh: “亲，你知道我是谁——大家都知道。”（眨眨眼。）

En: *Back-up*?! There's nothing to back up, nowhere to back down -- the terrible finale is on us!
Zh: “*增援*？！有什么好增援的，已经无处可退了——可怕的结局就要降临在我们头上！”

En: Me, back-up? More like a fuck-up...
Zh: “我，像增援吗？我更像个混账吧…”

En: Lieutenant double-yefreitor Harrier Du Bois, 41st Precinct. This is my partner, Lieutenant Kitsuragi, from the 57th.
Zh: “双重荣誉警督哈里尔·杜博阿，隶属于41辖区。这是我的搭档，57区的曷城警督。”

En: Lieutenant double-yefreitor Harrier Du Bois, 41st Precinct.
Zh: “双重荣誉警督哈里尔·杜博阿，隶属于41辖区。”

En: I'm a detective with the 41st Precinct. This is my partner from the 57th.
Zh: “我是41区的一名警探。这位是我57区的搭档。”

En: Yes. I'm a detective with the 41st Precinct.
Zh: “是的。我是41区的一名警探。”

En: Nowhere... she repeats, sudden sadness in her eyes. There's a moment of clarity. "Mr. Morrand? No I..." She raises the megaphone and screams: "AGGRAVATED ASSAULT, MAN DOWN, SUSPECT ON FOOT!"
Zh: “无处可去…”她重复着，眼里突然充满悲伤。只有片刻的清醒。“莫兰德先生？不，我…”她举起扩音器，大喊到：“加重攻击罪，有人倒下，嫌疑人徒步逃脱！”

En: I do? The question is addressed to herself more than you. "Gareth? No I..." She raises the megaphone and screams: "AGGRAVATED ASSAULT, MAN DOWN, SUSPECT ON FOOT!"
Zh: “是吗？”这个问题更像是留给她自己的，而不是你。“加雷斯？不，我…”她举起扩音器，大喊到：“加重攻击罪，有人倒下，嫌疑人徒步逃脱！”

En: Fuck-up, she repeats, "fuck up, fuck up, you fucked up, you fucked up, Gareth!" Spit is flying everywhere as she screams in the megaphone: "AGGRAVATED ASSAULT, MAN DOWN, SUSPECT ON FOOT!"
Zh: “见鬼，”她重复到，“见鬼，该死，你搞砸了，你搞砸了，加雷斯！”她朝着扩音器大喊，唾沫星子飞溅地到处都是：“加重攻击罪，有人倒下，嫌疑人徒步逃脱！”

En: You are? The question is addressed to herself more than you. "Mr. Morrand? No I..." She raises the megaphone and screams: "AGGRAVATED ASSAULT, MAN DOWN, SUSPECT ON FOOT!"
Zh: “你是吗？”这个问题更像是留给她自己的，而不是你。“莫兰德先生？不，我…”她举起扩音器，大喊到：“加重攻击罪，有人倒下，嫌疑人徒步逃脱！”

En: I'm with the Remote Viewers Division.
Zh: “我是遥视者部队的。”

En: But back-up... She looks around, bewildered. "I thought Mr. Morrand..." She raises the megaphone and screams: "AGGRAVATED ASSAULT, MAN DOWN, SUSPECT ON FOOT!"
Zh: “但是增援…”她环顾四周，有些困惑。“我以为莫兰德先生…”她举起扩音器，大喊到：“加重攻击罪，有人倒下，嫌疑人徒步逃脱！”

En: I'm with the rat squad!
Zh: “我是老鼠小队的！”

En: What's the situation... the lieutenant hesitates addressing the woman, "... officer?"
Zh: “现在是什么情况…”警督犹豫地对她喊话到，“…警官？”

En: With a swift, poorly coordinated move the woman slams the megaphone against her lips and teeth. A trickle of blood runs down her chin. She doesn't notice it.
Zh: 随着一下迅速但非常不协调的动作，女人用扩音器狠狠砸向自己的嘴唇和牙齿。一丝血从她的下巴滴落。她甚至都没注意到。

En: OFFICER COMPROMISED, UNLAWFUL IMPERSONATION! PIGS *EN ROUTE*, ENGAGE AT WILL!!!
Zh: “警官陷入危险，非法假扮！猪猪女侠*正赶往现场*，各自为战！！！”

En: Easy... the lieutenant whispers. "Press her too hard and she'll..."
Zh: “放松…”警督低语到。“别给她太多压力，不然她会..”

En: A click. Nothing happens. She looks at the useless weapon. "This isn't police issue. Police weapons have bullets. This isn't real! What is this?"
Zh: 咔哒一声。什么也没发生。她看着没用的武器。“这不是警察签发的。警察的武器里有子弹。这不是真的！这是什么？”

En: Wait...
Zh: “等等…”

En: In there she is alone, trapped in a world of blue and red lights.
Zh: 她在那里是孤独的，困在一个蓝红灯光交织的世界。

En: Her children and their children, if there were any, stopped coming when it got really bad.
Zh: 她的孩子，还有孩子的孩子，如果还有的话，在情况变得糟糕的时候，就不再来了。

En: Understood.
Zh: “明白。”

En: I've had... disturbances similar to this before. Many calls, but... never this bad.
Zh: “我…之前遇见过类似的失调状况。喊过很多声，但是…从没这么糟糕过。”

En: She stands motionless. Just a heap of clothes and flashers now -- maybe if you search her once more?
Zh: 她站在那里，一动不动。现在只剩下一堆衣物和闪光——也许你可以再搜查她一遍？

En: The woman stands motionless. Just a heap of clothes and flashers now -- maybe if you search her?
Zh: 她站在那里，一动不动。现在只剩下一堆衣物和闪光——也许你可以搜她的身？

En: Time to get my gun!
Zh: 是时候找回我的枪了！

En: Rogue cop on the loose.
Zh: 逍遥法外的流氓警察。

En: A peaceful solution.
Zh: 一个和平的解决方案。

En: Suspect is agitated.
Zh: 嫌疑人情绪很激动。

En: Suspect is *really* agitated.
Zh: 嫌疑人的情绪*真的*很激动。

En: Flashed your piece.
Zh: 亮出你的东西。

En: Alright, here's the plan: You hurl the ledger in her face -- already darting right -- and *immediately* close the distance. Left hand grabs the barrel, right one breaks the wrist...
Zh: 好吧，计划是这样的：你把手册扔到她脸上——已经瞄准过了——然后*立即*缩短距离。左手抓住枪管，右手拧断手腕…

En: She's frantic, confused, teetering under all that gear... There's an opening, if you just move fast enough.
Zh: 她狂乱，迷惘，在那身盔甲下摇摇欲坠…如果你的动作足够快的话，应该能找到突破口。

En: Alright, here's the plan: You fling the chaincutters in her face -- already darting right -- and *immediately* close the distance. Left hand grabs the barrel, right one breaks the wrist...
Zh: 好吧，计划是这样的：你把切链钳扔到她脸上——已经瞄准过了——然后*立即*缩短距离。左手抓住枪管，右手拧断手腕…

En: Alright, this is how we do it: You hurl the tare bag in her face -- already darting right -- and *immediately* close the distance. Left hand grabs the barrel, right one breaks the wrist...
Zh: 好吧，我们这样做：你把回收袋砸到她脸上——已经瞄准过了——然后*立即*缩短距离。左手抓住枪管，右手拧断手腕…

En: Alright, this is how we do it: You fling the prybar at her-- already darting right -- and *immediately* close the distance. Left hand grabs the barrel, right one breaks the wrist...
Zh: 好吧，我们这样做：你把撬棍砸到她脸上——已经瞄准过了——然后*立即*缩短距离。左手抓住枪管，右手拧断手腕…

En: Here's the plan: Drop the multitool and immediately dart right -- the noise will startle her, allowing you to close the distance. Left hand grabs the barrel, right one breaks the wrist...
Zh: 计划是这样的：丢掉多用工具，然后立即瞄准——响动会吓她一跳，这样你就可以缩短距离。左手抓住枪管，右手拧断手腕…

En: Here's the plan: You jerk left, dash right, close the distance and cut her hand off. Ready?
Zh: 计划是这样的：你向左猛冲，然后再向右冲，缩短距离，然后砍掉她的手。准备好了吗？

En: Here's the plan: Shine the light straight in her eye and immediately dart right-- the light will blind her, allowing you to close the distance. Left hand grabs the barrel, right one breaks the wrist...
Zh: 计划是这样的：用光线直射她的眼睛，然后立即冲向右边——光线会让她暂时失明，这样就能缩短你们之间的距离。接着左手抓住枪管，右手拧断手腕…

En: Porta-reel is the key. Up the volume to the *MAX* and hit 'Play'. The sound will startle her, allowing you to close the distance. Left hand grabs the barrel, right one breaks the wrist...
Zh: 录音机是关键。把音量调到*最大*，然后按下‘播放’键。声音会吓她一跳，这样就能缩短你们之间的距离。接着左手抓住枪管，右手拧断手腕…

En: The gun, throw it in her face -- she won't see it coming. As she tries to catch or evade, you close the distance. Left hand grabs the barrel, right one breaks the wrist...
Zh: 那把枪，朝她脸上扔——她不会想到这一点的。当她试着接住枪或者躲避时，你就能缩短距离。接着左手抓住枪管，右手拧断手腕…

En: Alright, here's how we do it: As soon as that gun isn't pointed at you again, you dash right, then immediately close the distance. Left hand grabs the barrel, right one breaks the wrist...
Zh: 好吧，我们这样做：等枪没有对准你的时候，你冲向右边，立即缩短距离。接着左手抓住枪管，右手拧断手腕…

En: ...fingers lock behind her head, knee to the face, knee to the face, crush the ribcage, side-step and drop her with the hammer kick.
Zh: …用手指锁住她的后脑勺，用膝盖撞她的脸，再用膝盖撞她的脸，砸碎她的胸腔，横跨一步，然后用下劈腿把她打倒。

En: OH MY GOD, YES!!!!
Zh: 我的天啦，没错！！！

En: No. That won't be necessary. Look closer. The gun, all three barrels, red and blue light shining through -- it's not loaded.
Zh: 不。没这个必要。仔细看看。那支枪，虽然有三根枪管，但是透过红蓝相间的灯光可以看见——里面根本没有填装子弹。

En: A drop of sweat runs down your nose and in it you see the Martinaise inlet, wrapped in the night. In the abandoned fish market three figures stand in a spectacular light-show. A large, loud woman is pointing an empty firearm at the other two. She doesn't want to listen... or even to be heard.
Zh: 一滴雨坠落在你的鼻子上，透过它，你看见被夜色包裹的马丁内斯水湾。废弃的鱼市上，三个身影站在一场壮观的灯光秀中央。一个高个子、大嗓门的女人用一把空枪指着另外两个人。她不想听别人说话…甚至也不想被倾听。

En: No! Look how she's holding it. Like it's light in the nose. It must be... That means the gun is empty!
Zh: 不！看看她手里的东西。就像鼻子里有光一样。这就意味着…那把枪是空的！

En: No. An ominous feeling fills your gut and suddenly you *know* the gun is empty. Thinking about it makes you nauseous.
Zh: 不。一种不祥的预感在你的腹部翻滚，你突然*意识到*那支枪是空的。一想到它就让你感到恶心。

En: No. A strange feeling fills your gut and suddenly you *know* the gun is empty. Thinking about it makes you nauseous.
Zh: 不。一种不祥的预感在你的腹部翻滚，你突然*意识到*那支枪是空的。一想到它就让你感到恶心。

En: A drop of sweat runs down your nose and in it you see the Martinaise inlet, wrapped in the night. In the abandoned fish market two figures face each other in a spectacular light-show. One of them is forced to stare into the three empty barrels of a gun. Not an uncommon sight in the neighbourhood.
Zh: 一滴雨坠落在你的鼻子上，透过它，你看见被夜色包裹的马丁内斯水湾。废弃的鱼市上，两个身影面对面站在一场壮观的灯光秀中央。其中一个人被迫盯着一把手枪和它那三根空空如也的枪管。这种场面在这个街区可不常见。

En: No. Wait... Earlier the lieutenant noticed something. Something about the gun... Could it be empty without her knowing it?
Zh: 不。等等…早些时候警督就注意到什么了。跟枪有关的事…难道枪是空的，但她并不知情？

En: NO! Remember, 'Get the gun, it's okay. It's safe'? It must have meant the gun is not loaded. She's holding an empty gun.
Zh: 不！还记得吗，‘拿走那支枪，没事的。很安全’？那肯定就意味着那支枪没有上膛。她手中握着的，是一支空枪。

En: Fire off a shot over her head and use the moment of confusion to close the distance. Left hand grabs the barrel, right one breaks the wrist...
Zh: 朝她的头顶开一枪，利用她困惑的时间缩短距离。接着左手抓住枪管，右手拧断手腕…

En: A madwoman is pointing a gun at two police officers and you're thinking about warning shots?! Fuck the protocol! Take her out!
Zh: 一个疯女人正拿枪指着两个警官，而你还想着鸣枪示警？！让规定见鬼去吧！干掉她！

En: A madwoman is pointing a gun at a cop and you're thinking about warning shots?! Fuck the protocol! Shoot to kill.
Zh: 一个疯女人正拿枪指着一名警官，而你还想着鸣枪示警？！让规定见鬼去吧！当场射杀！

En: (Whisper.) "Kim, I'm almost certain there are no bullets in that gun."
Zh: （耳语。）“金，我几乎可以肯定枪里没有子弹。”

En: Go ahead, pull the trigger. I dare you.
Zh: “来吧，扣动扳机。我打赌你不敢。”

En: Ma'am, the weapon you're holding is mine and I *know* it has no bullets.
Zh: “女士，你握着的武器是我的，而且我*知道*里面没有子弹。”

En: My god, it's not loaded... The gun isn't loaded!
Zh: “天啦，它没有上膛…那支枪没有上膛！”

En: You're right, the lieutenant agrees. "Let's call her out on this."
Zh: “你说得对，”警督表示赞同。“我们直接对她喊话吧。”

En: I've been thinking that too. But it's impossible to tell for certain in these lighting conditions.
Zh: 警督点点头。“我也在在想这个。但是在这种光照条件下，我也不太肯定。”

En: Huh? The lieutenant studies the swaying firearm. "My god, I think you're right."
Zh: “哈？”警督研究着摇晃的武器。“天啦，我觉得你是对的。”

En: Yeah, with shit-eyes it must be, binoclard.
Zh: 没错，用你的狗眼看好，一定是这样，四眼仔。

En: Hold on, why does that make me nauseous?
Zh: 等等，为什么我会感觉到恶心？

En: That knowledge comes from a very dark place.
Zh: 那种知识来自于一个非常黑暗的地方。

En: How dark?
Zh: 有多黑暗？

En: 'Paint the wall with your brains' dark.
Zh: ‘脑浆涂地’的黑暗。

En: Gun-gun, I'm coming! (Go for it.)
Zh: “我的小枪枪，我来了！”（去拿枪。）

En: No, I know myself. I know I'm gonna fail.
Zh: 不，我了解自己。我知道我会失败的。

En: No gun is worth this risk... (Slowly back away.)
Zh: “没有什么枪值得冒这种风险…”（慢慢退后。）

En: She's frantic, confused, teetering under all that gear... There's an opening, if you just move fast enough... If not, the lieutenant's got her on his sights.
Zh: 她狂乱，迷惘，在那身盔甲下摇摇欲坠…如果你的动作足够快的话，应该能找到突破口…如果不行，警督也已经盯上她了。

En: Like when he had the hanged man in his sights? You're a dead man.
Zh: 就像上次他盯着那个吊着的男人？你已经是个死人了。

En: Host Almighty, don't count on *that*! You saw the binoclard can't shoot for shit!
Zh: 我的个老天爷啊，可千万别指望*那个*！你见识过四眼仔的枪法到底有多烂！

En: Sure, you're you. But how do we end this? She's not just gonna let you leave.
Zh: 当然，你是你。但是我们该如何结束这一切呢？她是不会让你离开的。

En: SUICIDE BY COP! SPLIT-SECOND DECISION. EAT LEAD, SCUMBAG!
Zh: “警察自杀！瞬间决定。尝尝子弹的味道吧，人渣！”

En: CONFINED QUARTERS, ASSAILANT COMING AT ME! SPLIT-SECOND DECISION, FIRE AT WILL!
Zh: “封锁现场！袭击者冲我来了！瞬间决定，无限制射击！”

En: Wh... what is this? The old woman looks at the treacherous weapon. "This isn't police issue. Police weapons have bullets. This isn't real!"
Zh: “什…这是什么？”老妇人看着背叛她的武器。“这不是警察的东西。警察的武器里面有子弹。它不是真的！”

En: OH MY GOD!!!!
Zh: 我的天啦！！！

En: In your peripheral vision the lieutenant releases the half-squeezed trigger.
Zh: 在你的外围视觉中，警督松开了半扣着的扳机。

En: I was 80% sure the gun is empty and *still* the instinct almost took over.
Zh: 我有80%的把握，那支枪是空的，*而且*这种直觉几乎控制了一切。

En: God... the lieutenant exhales loudly and uses his off-hand to wipe his forehead. "I almost blew her head off..."
Zh: “天啊…”警督大声地呼出一口气，用另一只手擦拭着前额的汗滴。“我差点就把她的脑袋给打爆了…”

En: Phew... The lieutenant exhales sharply. "For a moment there..."
Zh: “呼…”警督重重地吐了一口气。“有那么一会儿…”

En: Situation is under control! The lieutenant's voice rings over the police siren. "You're not shot, she's not a threat, everybody stay calm!"
Zh: “情况已经得到控制！”警督的声音盖过了警笛。“你没有中枪，她不是什么威胁，大家都冷静一点！”

En: I was 95% sure the gun is empty and *still* that instinctual muscle twitch could have ended in an unnecessary death.
Zh: 我有95%的把握，那支枪是空的，*不过*本能的肌肉抽搐很可能会导致不必要的伤亡。

En: CONDITION BLACK, ALL SYSTEMS CRITICAL!
Zh: 黑色警戒，所有系统处于临界状态！

En: Oh... I see. Okay, the gun was empty. All is good. You're fine.
Zh: 哦…我明白了。好吧，枪是空的。一切都好。你也没事。

En: No... no-no-no-no. THEY'RE REALLY NOT. The gun wasn't even loaded. No shots fired. You're fine.
Zh: 不…不不不不。这不是真的。枪甚至都没有上膛，也没有开火。你安然无恙。

En: The gun lands on the wooden planks and tears run down her scratched cheeks. "Police guns always have bullets... What is this? Why did you sell me this?"
Zh: 枪掉落在木板上，眼泪从她划破的面颊上流过。“警枪总是有子弹的…这是什么？你为什么要把它卖给我？”

En: You tried to kill me!!!
Zh: “你想杀了我！！！”

En: I'm very sorry this is how life turned out for you. (Gently touch her shoulder.)
Zh: “我很抱歉，但这就是你的生活。”（轻柔地碰触她的肩膀。）

En: What do you think is happening to her?
Zh: “你觉得她这是怎么了？”

En: Alright, what am I gonna do with you? (Proceed.)
Zh: 好吧，我该拿你怎么办？（继续。）

En: Should we just arrest her? (Proceed.)
Zh: “我们应该逮捕她吗？”（继续。）

En: Grab the gun! Right now! This might be your *ONLY* chance.
Zh: 抓住那支枪！就是现在！这也许是你*唯一*的机会了。

En: She looks devastated.
Zh: 她看起来很震惊。

En: (Pick up your gun.)
Zh: （捡起你的枪。）

En: I'm done with this gun. (Leave it behind forever.)
Zh: “我再也不会用这支枪了。”（永远抛弃它。）

En: Why would they cheat me like this?! I don't have *ANYONE*! She slams her heavy boot in the ground. The impact travelling through the planks, sends your gun through the crack, into the black nothingness below.
Zh: “他们为什么要这样骗我？！我*什么人*也没有了！”她的脚重重地跺在地面上。震动顺着木板扩散，让你的枪落入缝隙，掉入下方黑暗的虚无中。

En: That gun was the single greatest thing you had. This is an enormous loss.
Zh: 那支枪是你拥有的最好的东西。这是一个巨大的损失。

En: Weapon still trained on The Pigs, the lieutenant swiftly reaches down to snatch up the gun.
Zh: 警督一边用枪对准猪猪女侠，一边迅速地抓过那支枪。

En: That gun is a joke compared to the actual greatest thing he lost.
Zh: 相比他失去的那个最重要的东西，这支枪就是个笑话。

En: No one ever cares anymore, her voice is growing fainter as she rocks back and forth, slowly. "Why would they cheat me like this..."
Zh: “没人在乎了，”她慢慢地前后摇晃，声音越来越微弱。“为什么他们要这样骗我…”

En: Poor woman... The lieutenant holsters his pistol.  "We need to figure out what to do with her now."
Zh: “可怜的女人…”警督收起他的枪。“现在我们需要想想该怎么处理她。”

En: *This* limbed and headed machine of pain and undignified suffering is grinding to a halt. Tired of walking the desert, it doesn't want to feel or think anymore.
Zh: *这个*肢体和头部组成的机器——备受痛苦和毫无体面的折磨——正在逐渐停止。厌倦了在沙漠中行走的它，已经不想再去感觉，去思考。

En: Great gears are grinding to a halt. The machine is powering down. She's all out of jolt.
Zh: 巨大的齿轮慢慢停止转动。机器的电力逐渐降低。她的全身都失去了力气。

En: Her scratched skin is warm to the touch, but the person inside doesn't even know you're there.
Zh: 她划破的皮肤碰触起来依旧温暖，但是里面的人甚至没有感知到你的存在。

En: Okay, you need to figure out what to do with her now.
Zh: 好吧，现在你需要想想怎么处理她。

En: I don't think she actually... The lieutenant shakes his head. "You're right of course. This could've been very bad."
Zh: “我不觉得她真的…”警督摇摇头。“当然，你说的没错。这件事可能会往很糟糕的方向发展。”

En: The person who did that is no longer there. No one is right now. The empty shell of an old woman is slowly rocking back and forth.
Zh: 做下那件事的人已经不在这里。没有任何人在这里。那个老妇人只剩一具空壳，正慢慢地前后晃动。

En: You're not alone. We're all here.
Zh: “你不是一个人。我们都在这里。”

En: She's in a stupor, the lieutenant replies, inspecting the catatonic woman standing in the dark. "I've seen this before. God knows for how long... Could be days when they get like this."
Zh: “她陷入恍惚状态了，”警督观察着站在黑暗中的精神症女患者，回复到。“我之前见过这种情况。天知道还会持续多久…变成这样之后，可能会持续好几天。”

En: But why was she like this in the first place?
Zh: “但她一开始为什么会变成这样？”

En: Honestly, I don't know. Dementia, probably, he lowers his voice. "Dementia and Channel 8. And loneliness."
Zh: “老实说，我不知道。可能是痴呆症吧，”他压低了声音。“痴呆症和8频道。还有孤独。”

En: Nod. "Neurodegenerative disease."
Zh: 点点头。“神经变性疾病。”

En: Yes. This is what happens when you listen to too many cop shows on the radio.
Zh: “是的。在广播里听过太多警察节目就会出现这种状况。”

En: You're probably right. (Nod.) "Loneliness."
Zh: “也许你是的对的。”（点头。）“孤独。”

En: I guess we'll never know.
Zh: “我猜我们永远也不会知道了。”

En: Probably not, the lieutenant agrees. "But more pressing is the question of what to do with her right now?"
Zh: “也许不能，”警督表示赞同。“不过更紧急的是，现在我们该怎么处理她？”

En: Looks like a bad case. He gives the woman a compassionate look. "But the question is what to do with her right now."
Zh: “看起来挺糟糕的。”他对女人露出了一个怜悯的表情。“但问题是，现在该怎么处理她。”

En: There's a bit more to this than that, the lieutenant disagrees. "But right now we need to decide what to do with her."
Zh: “我认为这件事比那个更重要，”警督不太同意。“不过现在我们需要决定该怎么处理她。”

En: Could be, agrees the lieutenant. "Her hands were trembling and she did seem uncoordinated... But what are we gonna do with her?"
Zh: “也许，”警督表示同意。“她的手在颤抖，看起来确实有些不协调…不过我们该怎么处理她呢？”

En: She's in a stupor. There's no telling when she'll snap out of it. If ever.
Zh: 她陷入恍惚状态了。谁也不知道她什么时候会清醒过来，如果还有这个可能的话。

En: He's trying not to think about how bad it could have been had the gun been loaded.
Zh: 他努力不去想——如果枪里有子弹，现在的情况到底会有多糟糕。

En: But it feels like *you* are, because the old woman standing there is motionless, emotionless and unresponsive. Almost like an inanimate object.
Zh: 但是感觉*你*是，因为老妇人一动不动地站在那里，面无表情，毫无反应。几乎就像个没有生命的物体。

En: Nobody's ever around, the old woman mumbles, staring blank at the boardwalk, "nobody ever comes to visit me."
Zh: “我身边从来没有人，”老妇人嘟囔着，茫然地凝视着木板道，“从没有人来看过我。”

En: The woman stands in front of you, motionless, unresponsive. Almost like an inanimate object now. A mountain of police paraphernalia.
Zh: 老妇人一动不动地站在你眼前，面无表情，毫无反应。几乎就像个没有生命的物体。只是一座警用设备的小山。

En: Kim Kitsuragi is a nice guy. Wish he was here right now.
Zh: 金·曷城是个好人。希望他现在一切都还好。

En: What...? The woman looks at the weapon in disbelief, her eyes suddenly reddish with tears. She looks straight down the barrel... and squeezes the trigger.
Zh: “什么…？”老妇人疑惑地看着手中的空枪，眼里突然充满泪水。她直直地看着枪管…然后扣动扳机。

En: I don't think there's any need for that. In her current state -- and without the gun -- she isn't really a threat to anyone.
Zh: “我觉得没这个必要。以她现在的状态——而且没了枪——她真的不会对任何人造成威胁。”

En: The catatonic woman -- especially now that she's unarmed -- doesn't seem to be a threat to anyone, but herself.
Zh: 这个紧张症女患者——特别是现在已经没有武器的情况下——似乎不会对任何人造成威胁，除了她自己。

En: We could let Titus know. This is a perfect problem for the local 'peacekeepers' to handle. They might even know her family.
Zh: “我们可以告诉提图斯。这是个可以留给本地*维和人员*处理的完美问题。他们甚至可能认识她的家人。”

En: I guess I'll just notify my station. They'll contact the Sanitarium and handle the logistics.
Zh: “我想我还是通知局里吧。他们会联系疗养院，处理好后勤问题。”

En: Wait, I was really hoping I can give her one of those station call slips...
Zh: “等等，我真的希望可以给她一份警局传唤通知单…”

En: Yeah, Titus sounds like the man for the job.
Zh: “是的，听起来提图斯应该是最佳人选。”

En: I don't want to endorse the Hardie boys' inflated sense of self-worth. They're not cops -- we are.
Zh: “我不想支持哈迪兄弟那种膨胀的自我价值感。他们不是警察——我们是。”

En: That would just be a waste of paper. I mean look at her. His eyes travel back to the old woman. "She's not going to respond to a station slip."
Zh: “那不过是浪费纸张而已。我是说，你看看她。”他的目光回到了老妇人身上。“她是不会回应警局传唤单的。”

En: I think we can just leave her like this. She'll be fine.
Zh: “我想可以把她一个人留在这里。她会没事的。”

En: No, the lieutenant sharply interjects. "That is not an option. We'll either unload this to Titus and his guys or I'll ask for help from my station."
Zh: “不，”警督连忙打断你。“没有这种选项。要么把这事交给提图斯和他的人，要么就打电话给我的分局寻求帮助。”

En: Okay, he nods. "That settles it. I'll contact my station after we wrap it up for the day. They can contact the Sanitarium and handle the logistics."
Zh: “好吧，”他点点头。“那就说定了。等今天的工作结束，我会联系我们分局。他们会联系疗养院，安排好后勤工作。”

En: Then we can ask him once we get back to the Whirling. But we have to hurry, cause it's late and they might have already gone home.
Zh: “等我们一回飞旋旅社就可以直接去找他。但是我们得快点，现在已经不早了，他们很可能已经回家了。”

En: Oh, before we go... The lieutenant pulls out your service pistol. "I believe this belongs to you."
Zh: “哦，在我们走之前…”警督拿出你的警枪。“我想这应该是你的吧。”

En: Titus wants to be the sheriff, I'll tell him where to find you. Maybe he knows your family.
Zh: “提图斯想当警长，我会告诉他去哪里找你。也许他会认识你的家人。”

En: At ease, patrol officer Pigs, your heart is in the right place. (Bow.)
Zh: “放松点，巡警猪猪女侠，你是个好人。”（鞠躬。）

En: Quietly leave the old woman alone.
Zh: 静静地离开那个老妇人。

En: Thank you, Kim. I *do* actually want my gun back.
Zh: “谢谢，金。我*确实*很想把我的枪找回来。”

En: No, I'm *really* done with it. You keep it.
Zh: “不，我*真的*受够它了。你留着吧。”

En: Okay but... He bites his lip and mumbles under his breath: "... probably couldn't change your mind anyway... Let's go then."
Zh: “好吧，不过…”他咬紧嘴唇，低声嘟囔着：“…估计也不太可能改变你的想法…我们走吧。”

En: Of course. Understandably things got a bit hectic before. Don't worry about it. He smiles as he hands you the weapon. "Reunited at last. Let's go now."
Zh: “当然。可以理解，之前事情搞得有点紧张。别太担心。”他微笑着把武器递给你。“总算团圆了。我们走吧。”

En: But I think we're done here for now. Let's head out, this is done.
Zh: “但是我觉得现在已经差不多了。我们出发吧，一切都结束了。”

En: As you turn to leave, the faintest of voices comes from the woman. "Please, leave the radio on..." she mutters. It seems like a reflex, a half-remembered sentence.
Zh: 你转身打算离开，那个老妇人突然发出一阵微弱的声音。“请不要关掉收音机…”她嘟囔着。这似乎是一种本能反应，一句只记得一半的话。

En: She must have built a new -- sadly better -- reality from the only material available: radio waves and cop shows.
Zh: 她自己肯定是建起了一个新的——可悲但却更好的——通过仅有的素材能够构筑的现实：无线电波和警察秀。

En: Reflex to what? Being left alone?
Zh: 对什么有反应？被一个人留下吗？

En: Exactly. With only the voice of Gareth Morrand to accompany her on Channel 8.
Zh: 没错。只有8频道主持人加雷斯·莫兰德的声音陪伴着她。

En: Lieutenant Kitsuragi would probably know what to do, but right now you're just gonna have to leave her like this.
Zh: 曷城警督可能知道该怎么做，但是现在你只能这样离开她了。

En: The locals will take care of her. Someone's bound to know her family. Nothing you can do for her anymore.
Zh: 本地人会照顾好她的。肯定有人认识她的家人。你没什么可以为她做的了。

En: Cross me again and I will bury you, Pigs.
Zh: “再遇到我的话，我会亲手将你埋葬，猪猪女侠。”

En: The catatonic woman doesn't understand -- probably even hear -- your kind words. She just *is*.
Zh: 精神症女患者不理解——可能甚至没听懂——你善意的话语。她只能*这样*而已。

En: It's a shame. Recognition from an actual police officer would probably mean a lot to her. Too bad she'll never know...
Zh: 说来可惜。得到一名真正警官的赞誉对她而言也许是件很重要的事。遗憾的是她永远也不会知道了…

En: She will *know*. (Hang the Setting Sun medal on her chest.)
Zh: 她会*知道*的。（把落日奖章挂在她的胸前。）

En: Nah, that's not worth it.
Zh: 不，那可不值得。

En: Mr. Morrand... Gareth, thank you. Thank you! As the sharp end of the pin grazes the flesh, a blessed smile emerges. Then it expires, and like a shadow moving on the ground, all sentience fades again.
Zh: 别针锋利的尖端擦伤她的皮肤，她的脸上却浮现出幸福的笑容。“莫兰德先生…加雷斯，谢谢你！”接着那份微笑也过了期，就像阴影从地上掠过，她的所有知觉再次消失。

En: Mr. Morrand? Mr. Gareth Morrand, the former cop, turned Channel 8 crime show host? That Gareth Morrand from the radio?
Zh: 莫兰德先生？加雷斯·莫兰德先生，前任警察，后来去8频道主持犯罪节目的那个？就是收音机里的那个加雷斯·莫兰德？

En: That... was the most honourable thing anyone has ever done.
Zh: 那…是任何人可能做过的做光荣的事情。

En: That... was the most honourable thing *anyone* has ever done. You are virtue embodied.
Zh: 那…是*任何人*可能做过的做光荣的事情。你就是美德的具现。

En: You weirdo. Get outta here before anyone sees you.
Zh: 你这个怪胎。在别人看见你之前赶紧离开这里。

En: Doesn't look like she would know the difference anyway.
Zh: 看来她根本不知道其中的区别。

En: Soon someone might have to. Doesn't look like there's much life left in her.
Zh: 很快可能会有人要做了。看来她的寿命已经所剩无几。

En: Take her to the farm. Put her down.
Zh: 带她去农场。把她放下。

En: Morrand's from Traffic.
Zh: 交通系统的莫兰德。

En: It's all nancies up there in Traffic. I count zero balls.
Zh: 交警队里都是些基佬。一个够胆的都没有。

En: That... was the most honourable thing anyone has ever done, Harry.
Zh: 那…是任何人可能做过的做光荣的事情。

En: As you turn to leave, the faintest of voices comes from the old lady. It's but a reflex -- nothing else. "Please leave the radio on... Mr. Morrand is on Channel 8..."
Zh: 你转身打算离开，那个老妇人突然发出一阵微弱的声音。只是一种本能反应——别无其他。“请不要关上收音机…莫兰德先生在8频道…”

En: As in 'Traffic' police?
Zh: 就像是‘交通’警察吗？

En: Necessity-driven architectural design at its finest.
Zh: “以需求为导向的最佳建筑设计。”

En: Don't worry, Mikael, you'll learn all about it.
Zh: “别担心，米克尔，你都会学到的。”

En: Fifty years ago this place was the heart of innovation.
Zh: “50年前，这里是创新者的天堂。”

En: Feld versus Tricentennial -- I wish we could've witnessed that, Mikael.
Zh: “菲尔德对阵三生公司——要是我们能亲眼见证就好了，米克尔。”

En: On the outside – ruins. But what are ruins, Mikael? Detritus of lost epochs.
Zh: “在外面——废墟。但是废墟是什么呢，米克尔？那是失落时代的碎片。”

En: We are living in *very* interesting times, Mikael.
Zh: “我们生活在一个*非常*有趣的时代，米克尔。”

En: Remind me to give you that collection of essays on modern architecture, Mikael.
Zh: “提醒我把那本现代建筑的论文集拿给你，米克尔。”

En: The significance of all *this* is hard to overestimate.
Zh: “*这里*的重要性可不容小觑。”

En: I'm just spending time with my kid here, showing him around the lesser-known parts of our home town... It wouldn't be *wise* to carry huge amounts of cash on such expeditions!
Zh: “我只是在这里陪陪我的孩子，让他了解一下家乡鲜为人知的部分…外出探险的时候携带大量现金可不是个*明智*的决定！”

En: Of course, detective, I wouldn't have assumed anything else. Matter of fact, he looks up again, a playful hint shining in his eyes...
Zh: “当然了，警探。其实，我也不会做出任何其他假设。事实上，”他再次抬起头，眼睛里闪烁着调皮的光…”

En: If not, then you definitely should -- if not for tips and tricks, then just for theoretical curiosity. Anyway, that's just a little something that sprang to mind. He squeezes his son's shoulder lightly. "You were saying?"
Zh: “如果没有，那你绝对应该这么做——如果不是为了提示和技巧，那为了理论上的好奇心也可以。总之，这只是我脑子里突然蹦出来的一件小事。”他轻轻捏着儿子的肩膀。“所以你刚才在说什么？”

En: I don't know if you're familiar with this, but the Vespertine Department of Justice has published a rather interesting paper on the criminal profiling in former socialist states. Have you read it?
Zh: “我不知道你对这个熟不熟，不过维斯珀司法部发表过一篇很有意思的论文，内容是关于前工人主义国家的犯罪心理画像。你读过吗？”

En: A-ha, but it's not just *any* empty old building! He raises his hand to his eyes to shield himself from the freezing snow. All three of you turn to admire the mural before you.
Zh: “啊哈，但它并不*只是*一栋空无一人的旧建筑！”他把手抬到眼睛的位置，想遮挡冰雪的白光。你们三个人开始欣赏起眼前的这幅壁画。

En: What not a lot of people know is -- this used to be the R&D department of *Feld Electrical*. And Feld, which now sells ink cartridges, mostly, was once a top dog in the turn-of-the-century cybernetics boom.
Zh: “只有很少人知道——这里曾经是*菲尔德电气*的R&D部门。而且，现在菲尔德主要是卖墨盒的了，不过在世纪之交神经机械学盛行的时候，它可是其中的领军人物。”

En: Look at the building looming over you.
Zh: 看着你头顶的那座大厦。

En: Hold on, what's R&D?
Zh: 等一下，R&D是什么？

En: It looks old and weathered, with seagulls picking apart its stone-and-metal carcass. Bushy undergrowth has taken hold of the collapsed rooftop; some kind of bird has set up a nest on a broken windowsill.
Zh: 它看起来破旧而风化，还有海鸥在撕扯石头和金属的残骸。浓密的灌木丛托起了倒塌的屋顶；破碎的窗台上筑着某种鸟类的巢。

En: A-ha, but it's not just *any* empty old building! He raises his hand to his eyes to shield himself from the pattering rain. All three of you turn to admire the mural before you.
Zh: “啊哈，但它并不*只是*一栋空置的老楼！”他把手抬到眼睛的位置，想遮挡冰雪的白光。你们三个人开始欣赏起眼前的这幅壁画。

En: A-ha, but it's not just *any* empty old building! He raises his hand to his eyes to shield himself from the freezing snow. All four of you turn to admire the mural before you.
Zh: “啊哈，所以它*并不是*一栋空置的老楼！”他把手抬到眼睛的位置，想遮挡冰雪的白光。你们四个人开始欣赏起眼前的这幅壁画。

En: Wait, what's an R&D department?
Zh: “等一下，R&D部门是什么？”

En: I don't think I've ever heard of this Feld Electrical. (Proceed.)
Zh: “我好像没听过菲尔德电气。”（继续。）

En: Apologies, it's an acronym for research and development, they don't use it anymore. He smiles brightly, laugh lines around his eyes.
Zh: “抱歉，那是研究与开发部门的缩写，现在已经不这么用了。”他笑的很灿烂，眼圈周围都是细纹。

En: That's not surprising. Only a vestigial ink cartridge and ferrotape manufacturer remains. He adjusts his suit jacket.
Zh: “这并不奇怪。现在它只是个退化的墨盒和湿版照片制造商。”他整理了一下自己的西装外套。

En: It was here in Martinaise -- possibly in this very building -- that they developed prototypes for a... He pauses for effect. "...*tape computer*."
Zh: “它就在马丁内斯这里——可能就在这栋大厦里——技术原型诞生了……”他装模作样地停顿了一会儿。“……*磁带电脑*。”

En: Indeed, what?
Zh: “的确，发生了什么事呢？”

En: He assumes something like a combat stance, facing the wind.
Zh: 他想象着一种直面狂风的战斗姿态。

En: It was here in Martinaise -- possibly in this very building -- that they developed prototypes for a... He stops mid-sentence, realizing you're not listening.
Zh: “就在马丁内斯的这个位置——也许就在这栋大厦里——他们开发出了原型……”他意识到你没在听，中途停了下来。

En: All of this was built by Feld, even the boardwalk. Wild Pines built Martinaise proper as a resort for their middle management, Feld built this side of town for R&D.
Zh: 他停下来，指向另一栋建筑，然后继续说到：“那些都是菲尔德建造的，就连木板道也是。野松公司为他们的中层管理人员把马丁内斯打造成了一个度假村，菲尔德为了R&D建设了城市的这个部分。”

En: Yes -- tape computers. *And* Feld Electrical.
Zh: “是的——磁带电脑。*还有*菲尔德电气。”

En: *I* certainly haven't. He shakes his head. "Though I understand the socio-economic causes of the Revolution, it pains me to imagine the revolutionaries setting fire to this precious device. But so they did. The Feld Playback Experiment vanished into the fires of '07."
Zh: “*我*确实没见过。”他摇摇头。“虽然我理解大革命爆发的社会经济原因，但是一想到革命者烧毁这些珍贵设备的画面，还是让我感到痛心。但总之他们这么做了。菲尔德重现实验消失在了07年的那场大火中。”

En: Even *one* would be very useful, he agrees. "Though I understand the socio-economic causes of the Revolution, it pains me to imagine the revolutionaries setting fire to this precious device. But so they did. The Feld Playback Experiment vanished into the fires of '07."
Zh: “哪怕是留下*一台*都会非常有用，”他表示同意。“虽然我理解大革命爆发的社会经济原因，但是一想到革命者烧毁这些珍贵设备的画面，还是让我感到痛心。但总之他们这么做了。菲尔德重现实验消失在了07年的那场大火中。”

En: The Lo Manthang thing was enough for me. I'm inspired.
Zh: “罗马丹什么的已经听够了。我很受鼓舞。”

En: Great. Great to hear that.
Zh: “很好。很高兴能听到你这么说。”

En: Inspiration can go a long way in physical endeavours. You should try it again now.
Zh: 鼓舞对于身体的努力大有益处。现在你应该再试一次。

En: Do you know of an *alternative* way into the building?
Zh: “你知不知道有没有*其他*办法可以进入那栋大厦？”

En: Alternative? I don't even have a *mainstream* way in, I'm afraid, although... He points over the guardrail, to the sewage run-off there.
Zh: “其他办法？恐怕我连*主要*办法都不知道，不过…”他指向护栏那边的一个下水道溢放口。

En: There's a sewage pipe there, maybe that does something? Sorry I can't help more.
Zh: “那边有个污水管，也许有什么用场？很抱歉，我也帮不了你太多。”

En: Pipes *can* be broken. With force.
Zh: 管道*可以*被砸破，如果用蛮力的话。

En: The man speaks in the artificial cadence of a professor -- or someone who's been on too many radio shows.
Zh: 男人说话的方式带着一种刻意又抑扬顿挫的调调，像个教授——或者是某个听过太多电台节目的人。

En: A tape computer?
Zh: “磁带电脑？”

En: Mhm. An elegant folding mechanism of rollers and ferrotape ribbons, portable enough to be a take-it-home solution, revolutionizing business machines, possibly even bringing them to the average consumer.
Zh: “嗯。一种滚轴和湿版摄影色带的高雅折叠结构。它提供了便携式的解决方案，是一种革命性的商用机器，甚至还能把它们带给普通的消费者。”

En: Perhaps reminded of a childhood memory? It's clear he would prefer there were a big wheel lighting up the coast.
Zh: 也许想起了儿时的记忆？显然他更希望能有一个大摩天轮照亮海岸。

En: A short-lived legislative foundation for a short-lived utopia.
Zh: 一个短命的立法基础，一个短暂的乌托邦。

En: The kid takes a peek at the green and silver würm on the cover of the book. Already forgetting about this part of the discussion.
Zh: 小孩看了一眼封面上绿银相间的蠕虫。已经忘记了这部分的讨论。

En: Buckle up!
Zh: 坐稳了，刺激的要来了！

En: This is all ninsy-pinsy garbage for sissy-people!
Zh: 这些全是那帮娘娘腔说的废话！

En: Made of black film and folding tape structures...
Zh: 由黑膜和折叠磁带结构组成…

En: So you haven't seen anyone around?
Zh: ”所以你没在附近见到任何人？”

En: No, I'm sorry. As I said -- this is just a day off. We just arrived anyway.
Zh: “没有。我很抱歉。我之前也说过了——只是稍微休息一天。而且我们刚刚才到。”

En: There's something friendly and familiar in how he says that. A day off.
Zh: 他说话的方式有种友好和熟悉的感觉。休息日。

En: He's telling the truth. He hasn't seen anyone.
Zh: 他说的是实话。他没有见过任何人。

En: He's not gonna give you money, what are you doing? Clearly you were just profiling.
Zh: 他不会给你钱的，你在干什么？你显然只是在做剖绘。

En: No, I mean, c'mon -- you need the money. If it's not a thing, he can give you some.
Zh: 不，我是说，拜托——你需要钱。如果这东西不值一提的话，他可以给你一点。

En: Forget it. They don't sell those kinds of books at "Crime, Romance & Biographies of Famous People."
Zh: 忘了吧。“犯罪，浪漫&名人传记”是不卖那种书的。

En: Pick *your* brain? If anything, this was rather one-sided. He did the talking. Whatever...
Zh: 向*你*请教？如果说还有什么的话，这明明是一边倒吧。都是他在说。无所谓了…

En: Suddenly little Mikael opens up his mouth: "Dad's fighting with sticks every night after dinner for four hours. He has a special room for that -- *and* a special costume!"
Zh: 突然间，小米克尔开了口：“每天晚上吃完晚餐之后，爸爸都会跟棍子打架，要打四个小时呢！他还有个专门的房间——*而且*还有套专门的衣服！”

En: Dad, can I go climb that ladder? Mikael asks. "I want to go climb that ladder!"
Zh: “爸爸，我可以爬那个梯子吗？”米克尔问到。“我想爬那个梯子！”

En: A wheel? The kid looks wistfully at the horizon, as if picturing gondolas rising to the sky.
Zh: “一个轮子？”孩子看着地平线，望眼欲穿，像是正在脑中描绘贡多拉飞向天空的画面。

En: Suddenly little Mikael opens up his mouth: "Dad's fighting with sticks every night after dinner for four hours. He has a special room for that -- *and* a special costume!"
Zh: 突然间，小米克尔开了口：“每天晚上吃完晚餐之后，爸爸都会跟棍子打架，要打四个小时呢！他还有个专门的房间——*而且*还有套专门的衣服！”

En: This is a long-shot, but could you help me contact the Coalition?
Zh: “这事说来话长，不过你能帮我联系联盟吗？”

En: Well, that certainly depends. You know, the popular image of the Coalition is of a monolithic politico-military entity, but in reality, it's comprised of many interconnected, yet semi-autonomous components...
Zh: “这个嘛，这当然要看情况。你知道，大众印象中的联盟是一个单一的政治军事实体，但实际上，它是由许多相互联系，但又半独立的部分组成的……”

En: For instance, the offices of INSURCOM are responsible for military affairs. These offices are located in the Bank of the World building, just a few kilometres from here... He points west toward the skyscrapers across the bay.
Zh: “比如，伊苏林迪指挥中心的办公室就负责军事事务。这些办公室位于世界银行大厦，离这里只有几公里……”他向西指着海湾对面的摩天大楼。

En: But then there's also the Provisional Commission, as well as the Institute for Revacholian Culture, with its architecturally significant headquarters in Le Jardin.
Zh: “但还有临时委员会，以及瑞瓦肖文化研究所，两者建筑学意义上壮观的总部就在勒雅尔丹。”

En: What's INSURCOM?
Zh: “什么是伊苏林迪指挥中心？”

En: What's this Insitute for Revacholian Culture?
Zh: “这个雷瓦肖文化研究所是什么？”

En: Who handles the money? There's got to be an economic component.
Zh: “谁来处理钱的事情？肯定有负责经济的部门。”

En: That's all fine, but what if I want to assign *responsibility*? (Proceed.)
Zh: “这都可以，但如果我想分配*职责*呢？” (继续)

En: INSURCOM is responsible for all aspects of the military occupation of Revachol. During the Revolution, they coordinated the efforts of the Occidental and Graadian invasion, but these days their function is primarily logistical.
Zh: “伊苏林迪指挥中心负责瑞瓦肖军事占领的一切工作。在大革命期间，他们调度了奥西登人和格拉德人的入侵工作，但如今他们的职能主要是后勤工作。”

En: Oh, the Institute is a marvellous organisation, my *favourite* part of the Coalition by far. Their mandate is two-fold: To export notable works of Revacholian culture throughout the Moralintern, while also importing the most important cultural products of the wider world to citizens of Revachol...
Zh: “噢，研究所是一个了不起的组织，是联盟中迄今为止我*最喜欢*的部门。他们的任务有两个方面：向整个国际道德伦理委员会输出著名的瑞瓦肖文化作品，同时也将全球范围内最重要的文化产品输入给瑞瓦肖的公民……”

En: Of course there is! The Coalition's economic affairs are handled by the Bank of the World -- Revachol Mission. They operate in concert with the Institute of Price Stabilité in Sur-la-Clef.
Zh: “当然有啊！联盟的经济事务由世界银行——也就是瑞瓦肖使团负责。他们与苏拉菲的价格稳定研究所协同运作。”

En: Ah, that's simple! You're looking for the Comité de Responsabilité de Revachol. It's a sort of clearinghouse for Coalition affairs in Revachol...
Zh: “啊，简单！你要找的是瑞瓦肖责任委员会。它算是瑞瓦肖的联盟相关事务信息交流中心……”

En: If you will, picture the Coalition as a kind of wheel. The hub of this wheel is the Comité de Responsabilité, and out from that hub radiate a great many spokes, which connect to INSURCOM, the Institute for Revacholian Culture, the Provisional Commission, and so on...
Zh: “如果你愿意，可以把联盟想象成某种轮子。这个轮子的中心是责任委员会，从这个中心辐射出许多辐带，这些辐带连接着伊苏林迪指挥中心、瑞瓦肖文化研究所、临时委员会等等……”

En: There's an exhibition on Vespertine proto-expressionism that I can't wait to take Mikael to. We're working our way through the major artistic movements of the last cenutry.
Zh: “有一个关于维斯珀原表现主义的展览，我等不及带米克尔去看。我们正在努力学习上个世纪的主要艺术运动。”

En: Yes, this is just the sort of reasonable authority you're looking for!
Zh: 是的，这正是你要找的那种合理的权威！

En: If you're not part of the Coalition, how do you know so much about its bureaucracy?
Zh: “如果你不是联盟的一员，你怎么会对联盟的官僚体系了解这么多？”

En: Alright, fine. How do I get in touch with the Committee?
Zh: “行，好吧。我怎么才能和委员会联系上呢？”

En: What kind of political system do you think represents *my* innermost character?
Zh: “你认为什么样的政治制度才能代表*我*的内在气质呢？”

En: What is the innermost character of the Coalition, then?
Zh: “那么，联盟的内在气质是什么样的呢？”

En: I have a longstanding interest in political systems. You see, it's my personal theory that political systems are a lot like personalities. In other words, how a state organises itself is in some way the public expression of its innermost character...
Zh: “我对政治制度的兴趣由来已久。你看，我个人的理论是，政治制度很像人格。换句话说，一个国家如何组织自身，在某种程度上是其内在气质的公开表达……”

En: The man's smile turns into a frown. "Well, now, *that* is rather complicated. You can write to the Committee directly, but I understand that there's quite the backlog. A month or more, according to friends of mine who just so happen to know about this sort of thing..."
Zh: 男人的笑容变成了皱眉。“好吧，现如今要做到*这件事*是相当复杂的。你可以直接给委员会写信，但据我所知积压了不少信件。据我那些略知一二的朋友说，起码要一个多月去处理……”

En: The man smiles at you for a long moment...
Zh: 那人对着你笑了很久……

En: Of course, if you could somehow contact Coalition Warship Archer, you could reach the Committee directly. But, well, that obviously presents any number of logistical and technical challenges.
Zh: “当然，如果你能以某种方式联系到联盟战舰弓箭手号的话，你就可以直接联系到委员会了。但是，怎么说呢，这显然会带来相当数量的后勤和技术难题。”

En: He is doing his best not to show it, but the smile is ever-so-slightly more forced than before.
Zh: 虽然他想尽力掩饰，但他的笑容比之前更加勉强了。

En: Of course, officer, we've only recently met. It would require a more thorough conversation than we have time for to answer a question like that. It's fascinating to think about, though, isn't it?
Zh: “当然，警官，我们最近才认识。我们没有时间回答这样的问题，这需要更深入的交流。不过，只是思考它就已经很奇妙了，不是吗?”

En: Wait, what's Coalition Warship Archer?
Zh: “等等，联盟战舰弓箭手号是什么？”

En: Why do I need to contact a military aerostatic to talk to a civilian bureaucrat?
Zh: “为什么我需要联系一架军用静风舰来和一个文职官僚交谈？”

En: Why do I need to contact a military warship to talk to a civilian bureaucrat?
Zh: “为什么我需要联系一艘军队战舰来和一个文职官僚交谈？”

En: So how does one go about contacting a Coalition warship? (Proceed.)
Zh: “那么如何去联系联盟军舰呢？” (继续)

En: Why, officer, it's that gigantic aerostatic right over there. Have you really never noticed it before? A look of profound concern comes across the man's face.
Zh: “为什么这么问呢，警官。就是那边那个巨大的航空器啊。你真的从来没有注意过它吗？”那人的脸上露出了深深的忧虑之色。

En: You follow the man's gaze out toward the west. There, hanging some distance above the horison, you can just make out a strange silhouette...
Zh: 你顺着那人的目光向西边看去。在那里，悬在地平线上方一段距离外，你可以看到一个奇怪的轮廓……

En: You follow the man's gaze out toward the west. There, amidst the low-hanging stars, you can just make out signs of motion...
Zh: 你跟着那人的目光向西边看去。在那里，在低垂的星空中，你勉强可以看到运动的迹象……

En: It looks, to your eye, like some kind of long, metallic fish, suspended beneath a number of ceiling fans...
Zh: 在你眼里，它看起来像某种长长的金属鱼， 悬挂在一堆吊扇下面……

En: You look around, but you don't see anything unusual. Then again, your eyes aren't the sharpest...
Zh: 你环顾四周，但你没有看到任何不寻常的东西。不过话说回来，你的眼神也不是最锐利的……

En: It's a set of position lights moving in unison, like a restless constellation...
Zh: 这是一组夜航示廓灯整齐划一地移动，就像一个不安分的星座……

En: You can't see anything. But then, it's dark, and your eyes aren't the sharpest...
Zh: 你什么也看不见。但是，天黑了，你的眼神也不是最锐利的……

En: No, it's more like a creature from the abyssopelagic zone, a great dark bioluminescent fish...
Zh: 不，它更像是来自深海的生物，一条巨大的黑色发光鱼……

En: Not just any fish, it moves like some ancient bottom-feeder gliding over the ocean floor in search of prey...
Zh: 不是普通的鱼，它的动作就像某种古老的食底泥动物在海底滑行寻找猎物……

En: Don't let the paintjob fool you, it's a goddamn killing machine, and there's nothing you can do to stop it!
Zh: 别被它的外表骗了，那是个该死的杀人机器，你根本无法阻止它！

En: Don't let the shiny lights fool you, it's a goddamn killing machine, and there's nothing you can do to stop it!
Zh: 别被那些闪亮的灯光骗了，那是个该死的杀人机器，你根本无法阻止它！

En: It's about four-and-a-half kilometres from your position, you estimate.
Zh: 你估计，它离你的位置约4.5公里。

En: Oh, right. The *Archer*. I thought you said... something else.
Zh: “哦，对了。*弓箭手号*。我以为你说的是……别的东西。”

En: I don't think I've ever noticed it before.
Zh: “我想我以前从来没有注意过它。”

En: Ah, well, that's a relief to here. It would really be *quite concerning* if you hadn't been aware of it until just now...
Zh: “啊，好吧，这就放心了。如果你直到刚才才注意到的话，那就真的是*相当令人担忧*了……”

En: Fascinating! You know, in one sense, that's exactly what the Coalition intends. You see, they would prefer that most people in Revachol *don't* think of themselves as living under a *military* occupation. That's why they generally prefer to keep their warships at a discreet distance from major population centres...
Zh: “漂亮！你知道，从某种意义上说，这正是联盟的意图。你看，他们更希望瑞瓦肖的大多数人*不*自觉地生活在*军事*占领之下。这就是为什么他们通常倾向于让自己的战舰与主要人口中心谨慎地保持距离……”

En: It's amazing. An inspiring symbol.
Zh: “这真是太神奇了。它是一个鼓舞人心的象征。”

En: It looks like a fish.
Zh: “它看起来像一条鱼。”

En: It looks like a killing machine.
Zh: “它看起来像一台杀人机器。”

En: It looks like it's about four-and-a-half kilometres away.
Zh: “看起来它好像在4.5公里之外的地方。”

En: Huh. Can't believe I never noticed it before.
Zh: “咦，我竟然从来没有注意到它。”

En: That is certainly how the Coalition intends for it to be seen! You know, I once read a very interesting paper from Messina on the psychology of air-based artillery...
Zh: “这当然是联盟打算对人展示的样子！你知道吗，我曾经读过梅西纳的一篇非常有趣的论文，关于空基火炮的心理学……”

En: Apparently its presence stimulates the *exact* same part of the neural cortex that more primitive cultures associate with divine judgment. Isn't that fascinating?
Zh: “很显然，它的外观会刺激神经皮层的*特定*部分，而这部分在某些更原始的文化中会和神罚联系起来。这不是很美妙吗？”

En: That's very interesting. Most people I've met say it reminds them of a giant metallic dragonfly, but of course there's no right way to respond to a massive airborne artillery platform...
Zh: “非常有趣。我见过的大多数人都说它让他们想起了巨大的金属蜻蜓，但当然，面对一个巨大的空中火炮平台，怎么回应都不准确……”

En: Ah, well, it is one of the most heavily armed aerostatics to ever fly. I've heard it said that the Archer is capable of engaging whole fleets of lesser vessels *from multiple sides* simultaneously. How about that?
Zh: “啊，好吧，它是有史以来火力最强的武装静风舰之一。我听人说过，弓箭手号能够从同时从*多个方向*攻击一整支吨位较小的舰队。怎么样？”

En: I believe that is intentional. You see, the Coalition are very sensitive to how they are perceived by the people of Revachol, which is why they generally prefer for their warships to remain at a discreet distance from major population centres...
Zh: “我相信这是有意为之。你看，联盟对瑞瓦肖人怎么看待他们非常敏感，这就是为什么他们通常倾向于让自己的战舰与主要人口中心谨慎地保持距离……”

En: A very astute question! You see, in addition to its role as a military warship, the Archer is also responsible for coordinating all Coalition communications and surveillance operations in Revachol.
Zh: “一个非常精辟的问题！你看，弓箭手号除了担任战舰的角色外，还负责调度联盟在瑞瓦肖的所有通讯和监视行动。”

En: Hold on, it's *watching* us, too?
Zh: “等等，它在*监视*我们？”

En: I feel like there's *got* to be another way.
Zh: “我觉得*一定*还有别的办法。”

En: Not just watching, it's *listening* as well. I don't know the particulars, of course, but I would expect a warship like the Archer to possess the most advanced surveillance equipment available outside of Seol.
Zh: “不只是监视，还有*监听*。当然，我不知道具体的情况，但我觉得像弓箭手号这样的战舰会拥有索尔之外最先进的监视设备。”

En: Well, I suppose you could rent a private aerostatic and attempt to rendezvous with Archer directly, but I must caution against it. Warships like the Archer are usually authorised to fire on unidentified aerostatics on sight.
Zh: “好吧，我想你可以租一艘私人静风舰，去直接与弓箭手号接触，但我必须警告你不要这么做。像弓箭手号这样的战舰，通常都会被授权在看到不明身份的航空器接近时射击。”

En: That might be the trickiest question of all. From a technical standpoint, it shouldn't be all that difficult. You simply require a radio transmitter capable of broadcasting on Coalition frequencies...
Zh: “这可能是最棘手的问题。从技术角度来说，应该不是什么难事。你只需要一个能够在联盟频率上进行广播的无线电发射器……”

En: The problem is that those sorts of radio transmitters are *tightly* controlled by the Coalition. Even the RCM doesn't typically have access to them outside of special joint operations...
Zh: “问题是，联盟*严密*控制那种无线电发射器流通。即使是RCM，特殊的联合行动除外，一般也不会接触到它们……”

En: All that said, someone with a powerful radio and a certain level of technical skill might be able to circuit-bend their way onto the Coalition frequencies...
Zh: “话虽如此，拥有大功率无线电和一定技术水平的人，也许能迂回接入到联军的频率上……”

En: It's only too bad the old Feld engineers are no longer available. This sort of thing would have been *right* in their wheelhouse.
Zh: “只可惜老菲尔德的工程师已经不在了。这种事情本来*正对*他们的专业。”

En: He's correct. It makes coordinating operations with the Coalition exceedingly difficult, from our perspective.
Zh: “他说的没错。从我们的角度来说，这使得与联盟方面协调行动变得异常困难。”

En: What am I supposed to do then?
Zh: “那我该怎么办？”

En: Hmmm, she's not a 'Feld engineer,' exactly, but perhaps that programmer from the church might be of assistance?
Zh: “嗯，她并不是‘菲尔德工程师’，确切地说，但也许那个在教堂的程序员可能派的上用场？”

En: Hmmm, no one would mistake them for Feld engineers, but perhaps your young friends in the *tent* might be of assistance?
Zh: “嗯，没有人会把他们看错成菲尔德工程师，但也许你那些住在*帐篷*的年轻朋友们可能派的上用场？”

En: Oh, good: Now we're circuit-bending into radio towers. Just what this *side task* was missing...
Zh: “哦，真不错：现在我们要迂回接入无线电塔。正是这个*支线任务*急需的……”

En: This sounds like something that programmer you met in the church might help with...
Zh: 这听起来像是你在教堂里遇到的那个程序员能帮得上忙的事情……

En: Perhaps your new friends in the tent might be able to help you?
Zh: 也许你那些住在帐篷里的新朋友能帮你？

En: You *will* figure it out. Something tells you the person you're looking for is already on this coast...
Zh: 你*会*想出办法的。直觉告诉你要找的人已经在这里了……

En: In any event, it's a fascinating technical challenge. I'll be *very* interested to see how you tackle it.
Zh: “无论如何，这都是一项迷人的技术难题。我很有兴趣看看你是怎么解决的。”

En: But of course! What else? He smiles and ruffles his kid's head.
Zh: “当然可以！你还想知道什么？”他笑了笑，揉乱了孩子的头发。

En: How did those tape computers work? Did they work like radiocomputers?
Zh: “这些磁带电脑是怎么工作的？跟无线电脑很像吗？”

En: How did those tape computers work?
Zh: “这些磁带电脑是怎么工作的？”

En: Ten years ago, I did a little... freelancing, I guess you could say. I was a special consultant for an exhibition at the Wompty-Dompty-Dom Centre in Vredefort, Oranje. It raised the same questions, and we had lengthy discussions with Paul Ockermann, who was head curator at the time -- this was before the twins Keith and Guy Joost joined the team -- trying to...
Zh: “10年前，我做了一段时间的……自由职业者，我猜应该可以这么说吧。我曾经在三教九流中心的一次展会中被聘为特别顾问，地点在奥兰治的弗里德堡。那里有人提出了同样的问题，我们跟当时的主馆长——保罗·奥卡曼进行了漫长的讨论（这是基斯双胞胎和盖伊·约斯特加入团队之前的事了），想要…”

En: Wait. Did he just say *Wompty-Dompty-Dom Centre*?
Zh: 等等。他刚才好像说了*三教九流中心*？

En: He did it! He said *Wompty-Dompty-Dom Centre* like it's the most natural thing in the world.
Zh: 他说了！他说出*三教九流中心*的时候，就好像是这个世界上最自然的事情。

En: What the hell *is* a Wompty-Dompty-Dom Centre? And who the hell are Keith and Guy Joost?!
Zh: 三教九流中心*到底*是什么鬼？还有基斯和盖伊·约斯特又是什么鬼？

En: Okay. The Wompty-Dompty-Dom Centre? Paul Ockermann? Keith and Guy Joost? What are you *talking* about?
Zh: “好吧。三教九流中心？保罗·奥卡曼？基斯和盖伊·约斯特？你到底在*说*什么啊？”

En: The Wompty-Dompty-Dom Centre for Contemporary Arts. There it is again -- those words. "The exhibition itself drew on Lagerman's notion of memory and so there were some parallels, that's why the head curator Paul Ockermann chose to..."
Zh: “三教九流中心专门展出当代艺术。”又来了——这些词。“展览本身借鉴了拉格曼的记忆理念，所以也存在一些相似的地方，这也就是为什么主馆长保罗·奥卡曼选择…”

En: You're making this up. (Turn to the lieutenant.) "Kim, is he making this up?"
Zh: “你瞎编的吧。”（转向警督。）“金，这都是他瞎编的吗？”

En: You're making this up. (Turn to his son.) "Hey, is your dad making this all up?"
Zh: “你瞎编的吧。”（转向他的儿子。）“喂，这都是你爸爸瞎编的吗？”

En: Come on, there is no place called the Wompty-Dompty-Dom Centre of Arts.
Zh: “拜托，根本就没有一个叫三教九流艺术中心的地方。”

En: In fact, I'm not. The Wompty-Dompty-Dom Centre, he says casually, "is a place you can visit if you're ever in Vredefort, and are ever in the market for an exhibition space slash contemporary art research centre." He clears his throat.
Zh: “事实是，我没有。三教九流中心，“他随意说到，“如果有机会去弗里德堡，你可以过去看看，他们一直在积极物色一个展览空间/当代艺术研究中心。”他清了清嗓子。

En: Actually, I think there is... He pauses. "No, it's too much, he's joking."
Zh: “说实话，我觉得是有的…”他停了一会儿。“不，有点太过了，他在开玩笑。”

En: Very, very cool, he agrees. "Though I understand the socio-economic causes of the Revolution, it pains me to imagine the revolutionaries setting fire to this precious device. But so they did. The Feld Playback Experiment vanished into the fires of '07."
Zh: “非常、非常酷，”他表示赞同。“虽然我理解大革命爆发的社会经济原因，但是一想到革命者烧毁这些珍贵设备的画面，还是让我感到痛心。但总之他们这么做了。菲尔德重现实验消失在了07年的那场大火中。”

En: All of this sounds like something Ruby would be interested in...
Zh: 这些听起来像是露比会感兴趣的东西…

En: He means that 'the boys' got shot by the communists. 'The boys' were bourgeois.
Zh: 他的意思是被康米主义者枪杀的‘那些男孩’。‘那些男孩’都是资产阶级分子。

En: Wait, is he saying that we should just *bypass* the excesses of the Revolution?
Zh: 等等，他是说我们应该*忽视*大革命的暴行吗？

En: (Turn to Mikael.) "He means they all got shot in the head because they were bourgeois. Now, do you know what the bourgeoisie is?"
Zh: （转向米克尔。）“他的意思是说，他们被爆头是因为他们是资产阶级。所以，你知道资产阶级是什么吗？”

En: (Turn to Mikael.) "He means they all died at the hands of the communists."
Zh: （转向米克尔。）“他的意思是说，他们都死在了康米主义者的手里。”

En: Tape computers, right.
Zh: “磁带电脑，没错。”

En: Officer! He sounds disappointed. "I told you this chapter of history wasn't on Mikael's curriculum yet. Let's keep this kid-friendly."
Zh: “警官！”他听起来有些失望。“我告诉过你，历史的这一章节还不适合纳入米克尔的课程。我们还是继续儿童友好型的话题吧。”

En: The boy looks first at you, then back at his father, confused.
Zh: 男孩先是看了看你，然后看看自己的父亲，一头雾水。

En: A pleasure wheel? The lieutenant looks wistfully at the horizon, as if picturing gondolas rising to the sky.
Zh: “一个摩天轮？”警督看着地平线，望眼欲穿，像是正在脑中描绘贡多拉飞向天空的画面。

En: A-ha, but it's not just *any* empty old building! He raises his hand to his eyes to shield himself from the pattering rain. All four of you turn to admire the mural before you.
Zh: “啊哈，所以它*并不是*一栋空置的老楼！”他把手抬到眼睛的位置来遮挡细雨。你们四个人开始欣赏起眼前的这幅壁画。

En: A-ha, but it's not just *any* empty old building! He raises his hand to his eyes, springtime sun warming his handsome face. All four of you turn to admire the mural before you.
Zh: “啊哈，所以它*并不是*一栋空置的老楼！”他抬起手遮住眼睛，初春的太阳温暖着他英俊的面庞。你们四个人开始欣赏起眼前的这幅壁画。

En: A-ha, but it's not just *any* empty old building! He raises his hand to his eyes, springtime sun warming his handsome face. All three of you turn to admire the mural before you.
Zh: “啊哈，所以它*并不是*一栋空置的老楼！”他抬起手遮住眼睛，初春的太阳温暖着他英俊的面庞。你们三个人开始欣赏起眼前的这幅壁画。

En: Wait. What does this man know about pyrholidon addiction?
Zh: 等等。关于吡嚯浣酮上瘾，这个男人都知道些什么？

En: Wait. What does this man know about pyrholidon addiction?
Zh: 等等。关于吡嚯浣酮上瘾，这个男人都知道些什么？

En: I can assure you -- *nothing* in this world comes even close to being addicted to this devil.
Zh: “我可以向你保证——这个世界上根本*没有什么*能比得上对这个恶魔上瘾。”

En: Hold on. Pyrholidon addiction? What an interesting metaphor... (Study his face.) "Or perhaps not a metaphor at all?"
Zh: “等等。吡嚯浣酮上瘾？真是个有趣的比喻…”（仔细观察他的脸。）“又或者，这根本不是比喻？”

En: You got me, detective. He chuckles, before a shadow takes hold of his face. "But my history should hardly come as a surprise."
Zh: “被你发现了，警探。”他轻笑出声，随后整张脸都被阴影笼罩。“不过，我的过去应该没有什么好惊讶的吧。”

En: Oh, I know, detective. He chuckles, before a shadow takes hold of his pallid face -- he misses the devil.
Zh: “哦，我明白的，警探。”他轻笑出声，随后整张苍白的脸都被阴影笼罩——他想念那个恶魔。

En: Oh, I know, detective. He chuckles, but a shadow takes hold of his pallid face -- what does he mean that he *knows*?
Zh: “哦，我明白的，警探。”他轻笑出声，随后整张苍白的脸都被阴影笼罩——他说他*明白*是什么意思？

En: Here's a former junkie. I can recognize one when I see it.
Zh: 这个人以前是个瘾君子。我一眼就能认出来。

En: That's right, Mikael. Trant grabs his kid into a single-handed embrace, happy to change the subject. "It also has a meditative quality, helps to clear my head... But anyway," he says, swaying with his hand.
Zh: “没错，米克尔。”特兰特一把抱起他的孩子，很乐意换个话题。“它还有一种禅意，能帮我清空大脑…不过无所谓了……”他挥挥手说到。

En: But anyway, he says after a while, "I am boring you with details again. You were saying?"
Zh: “但不管怎么说，”过了一会儿，他开了口，“跟你讲这些细节，你一定觉得挺无聊的吧。不好意思，你刚才说什么了？”

En: I don't really remember what I was saying...
Zh: “我真不记得自己说过什么了…”

En: Then I guess everything got said, right? He looks up to the sky. "The drizzle... it never seems to stop, does it?"
Zh: “那我猜该说的都说过了，对吧？”他抬头望向天空。“这雨…好像永远不会停一样，是吧？”

En: Then I guess everything got said, right? He squints his eyes in the snow.
Zh: “那我猜该说的都说过了，对吧？”白茫茫的雪让他眯起双眼。

En: Then I guess everything got said, right? He squints his eyes in the sun.
Zh: “那我猜该说的都说过了，对吧？”耀眼的阳光让他眯起双眼。

En: Wait, what was it that you wanted to ask...?
Zh: 等等，你想问什么来着…”

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Still, Tricentennial was beating them in business machines. He gestures toward the building. "But Feld had an ace up their sleeve. Or, should I say, they were *developing* an ace up their sleeve?..." He grins. "I'm mixing my metaphors here."
Zh: “不过，三生公司在商用机器上胜过了他们。”他朝着大厦比了个手势。“不过菲尔德自有看家本领。或者我应该这样说，他们在*开发*自己的看家本领？…”他咧嘴一笑。“我好像把比喻都混在一起了。”

En: What was that ace?
Zh: “那个看家本领是什么？”

En: Very interesting. Let's get back to this later.
Zh: “挺有趣的。我们之后再继续说。”

En: Which is a feat of engineering even today's giants Rehm, ICN, and ZAMM haven't achieved yet. He grins, admiring the sentence he just produced.
Zh: “一项工程学的创举，就连今天的那些巨头：雷姆，ICN和ZAMM都没能达成。”他咧嘴一笑，回味着自己刚刚说出的那句话。

En: What happened?
Zh: “发生什么事了？”

En: The Revolution? The boy wipes his nose on his sleeve.
Zh: “大革命？”男孩用袖子擦擦鼻子。

En: Unfortunately their moonshot project never made it to the market. He nods. "Feld's move to Revachol backfired. The Revolutionary government liquefied their assets and expropriated those very advanced prototypes. Possibly from this very building... or one of the adjacent ruins." He pauses, pointing to the other building, then continues:
Zh: 他点点头。“可惜，他们的登月项目没能投放市场。菲尔德搬进驻瑞瓦肖的计划也搁浅了。革命政府变现了他们的资产，征用了那些极为先进的技术原型。也许就是从这栋大厦里夺走的…或者是从邻近的某个废墟吧。”

En: Okay. Back to... where were we? Tape computers, I think.
Zh: “好吧。回到…我们刚才说到哪了？磁带电脑，好像是。”

En: But perhaps I should return to the tape computers. As I was saying, the device itself was very elegant, fragile even. One could write directly on the tape using a special chemical solution. The machine would then analyse the handwriting, perform operations and project output onto a white screen. It was a beautiful, delicate thing.
Zh: “不过也许我应该回到磁带电脑的话题上。刚才也说过，设备本身非常精细，甚至有些脆弱。人们可以用一种特殊的化学溶液直接在磁带上书写。机器会分析笔迹，操作处理，然后把成果投射到白屏上。那是一种美丽又精致的东西。”

En: (Nod.) "Cool."
Zh: （点头。）“听起来很酷嘛。”

En: Pfft. I've seen cooler things than that.
Zh: “切。我见过比它更酷的东西。”

En: The RCM should get some of those.
Zh: “RCM应该弄点那玩意。”

En: Wait, the Feld Playback Experiment?
Zh: “等等，菲尔德重现实验？”

En: Why did the revolutionaries destroy it?
Zh: “为什么革命分子要毁掉它？”

En: Yes, the official name of the prototype. Some sources report it as the Feld Playback *Experience*, but those are incorrect.
Zh: “没错，那是原型机的官方名称。有些报道把它叫做菲尔德回放*体验*，其实是不对的。”

En: Who knows? Maybe it was an accident, or maybe they didn't want the technology to end up in the wrong hands. Either way -- they're all gone now, all three versions of the prototype. Nothing but debris and ashes remains inside that building. He takes a step back; the boardwalk creaks mournfully in the wind.
Zh: “谁知道呢？也许是个意外，或者也许他们不想技术最终落入坏人之手。不管怎么说——现在都没了——三台原型机都没了。这栋大厦里只剩下废墟和灰尘。”他退后一步；脚下的木板道在风中发出凄凉的嘎吱声。

En: Two seagulls circle in the sky. You look up and think: really? Or was there a fourth prototype that remains hidden in the mausoleums below Coal City?...
Zh: 两只海鸥在空中盘旋。你抬起头想到：真的只有三个吗？或者还有第四个原型藏在煤城下面的坟墓里没被人找到呢？…

En: Okay, I'll look into that then.
Zh: “好吧，我会调查一下的。”

En: Okay. I have some materials at home I could share with you...
Zh: “好吧。我家里有些材料，可以跟你分享…”

En: I'm looking for a suspect. Have you seen anyone suspicious around?
Zh: “我正在找一个嫌疑人。你有没有在附近看到可疑的人？”

En: No, I'm afraid I can't help you with this one, officer. It's just a regular day off for me and Mikael here. He pats his son's head.
Zh: “不，恐怕这个我帮不了你，警官。今天对于我和米克尔来说，只是个惯例的休息日。”他摸了摸儿子的脑袋。

En: They started out as a midway electronics outfit in Königstein two centuries ago. After an aggressive move to Revachol, Feld became a global player in the emerging personal electronics market of the pre-Revolutionary era.
Zh: “两个世纪前，他们以中途电子设备制造商的身份在科宁斯坦起家。在采用激进策略进驻瑞瓦肖之后，菲尔德成为了前大革命时代全球新兴个人电子用品市场的有力竞争者。”

En: Something about the way this man carries himself makes you wonder whether he might be able to help you with your *committee business*.
Zh: 这个人的行事风格让你怀疑他能否帮忙处理你的*委员会事务*。

En: It's his *sartorial style*. Call it 'well-travelled bean-counter'...
Zh: 是他的*时尚格调*。就是“见多识广的精明人”……

En: It's his manner of speaking. He reminds you of a lecturer...
Zh: 是他说话的方式。他让你想起了一位讲师……

En: Detective, a word. He turns to the blonde man. "Excuse us a moment, please."
Zh: “警探，借一步说话。”他转身对金发男子说“抱歉，请让我们单独谈谈。”

En: Of course, lieutenant. He nods. "Take your time. Mikael and I have plenty to keep us occupied!"
Zh: “当然，警督。”他点点头。“慢慢来吧。米克尔和我有很多事要忙！

En: In a low voice, the lieutenant whispers: "I haven't the faintest idea who this gentleman is, but I've got a *hunch* he might have some affiliation with the Coalition, in case you're still serious about that *committee business*..."
Zh: 警督耳语道：“我不知道这位先生是谁，但我有*预感*，他可能与联盟有一些联系，如果你还在认真考虑那个*委员会的事情*……”

En: In any case, I'll let you take it from here.
Zh: “不管怎样，接下来的事就交给你了。”

En: With that, you both turn back to the blonde man.
Zh: 说完，你们俩回到了金发男人的身边。

En: Whatever it is, he gives you an encouraging smile.
Zh: 虽然不知其中深意，但他给了你一个鼓励的微笑。

En: You, officers! Come to investigate the historic subtext of West Martinaise? I'm Trant Heidelstam, he turns to the lieutenant. "You must be Kim Kitsuragi, right? I was just telling my son about this building. Not a lot of people realize the historic significance here. Very rich in *hypertext*."
Zh: “喂，警官们！来调查西马丁内斯的历史潜文本吗？我是特兰特·海德斯塔姆，”他转向警督。“这位一定就是金·曷城了，对吗？我正准备跟我儿子讲讲这栋建筑呢。很少有人能意识到这里的历史意义。这里的*超文本*真的非常丰富。”

En: This man is your half brother. You feel it. But *why*?
Zh: 你能感觉到，这个男人与你存在某种近乎手足同僚的联系。但是*为什么*呢？

En: Not that he would have to worry about being robbed -- he looks surprisingly buff. Does he work out?
Zh: 倒不是说担心他会被抢——他看起来出奇的强壮。他有在锻炼吗？

En: You, officer! Come to investigate the historic subtext of West Martinaise? I was just telling my son about this building. Not many people appreciate the historic significance here. Very rich in *hypertext*.
Zh: “喂，警官！来调查西马丁内斯的历史潜台词吗？我正准备跟我儿子讲讲这栋建筑呢。很少能有人意识到这里的历史意义。这里的*超文本*真的非常丰富。”

En: Nice to meet you. The lieutenant nods.
Zh: “很高兴见到你。”警督点点头。

En: You look like someone who has money. Do you have any money?
Zh: “你看起来好像很富的样子。你有钱吗？”

En: I do have some money, yes, but that's not what's really important here. He brushes it off like it's not a thing at all.
Zh: “我确实有点钱，没错，不过这个问题不重要。”他对这件事避而不谈，好像它根本不值一提。

En: You're probably more familiar with *RTD*, research and *technological* development.
Zh: “你可能对*RTD*更熟悉一些，研究与*技术*开发。”

En: Could I have some of that 'unimportant' money then?
Zh: “那能给我一点你那些*不值一提*的钱吗？”

En: I don't want your money, I just wanted to see whether my profiling skills were working.
Zh: “我不想要你的钱，我只想看看自己是否还能正常使用我的剖绘技巧。”

En: Oh, no, I don't have it *on me*, officer, I was talking in more... general terms. He looks uncomfortable, his left hand squeezing his son's shoulder.
Zh: “哦，不，我现在身上没带*钱*，警官，我其实是用了更…笼统一些的说法。”他看起来不太舒服，左手捏紧了儿子的肩头。

En: By the way, do you work out?
Zh: “顺便问一句，你有在健身吗？”

En: I do some Lo Manthang stick fighting now and then.
Zh: “我偶尔会练习罗马丹斗棍。”

En: Lo Manthang stick fighting is a form of martial arts originating from the isle of Lo Manthang. It uses slender wooden sticks for confronting the opponent.
Zh: 罗马丹斗棍是一种武术，起源于罗马丹岛。主要使用细长的木棍对抗敌人。

En: Wait, what's Lo Manthang stick fighting?
Zh: “等等，罗马丹斗棍是什么？”

En: Mea culpa! You were not familiar with that one either. This man is a book head.
Zh: 我的错！你对那个也不是很熟悉。这男人是个书呆子。

En: A form of martial arts from the isle of Lo Manthang, right?
Zh: “那是起源于罗马丹岛的一种武术，对吗？”

En: Actually, a great many cultures have their own version of stick fighting, such as the sacred Mabolo tradition of the Hali people, the name deriving from the butterfruit tree traditionally used for crafting the long, slender sticks -- whereas the sticks used in other cultures...
Zh: “事实上，很多文明都有自己版本的斗棍术，比如哈利族神圣的码波勒传统，这个名字来源于鳄梨树，传统上是用于制造细长木棍的——然而在其他文明中，这种棍子的使用…”

En: You doze off, as he paints you a comprehensive picture of the history of stick fighting among mankind, seasoning it with unexpected pop-culture references.
Zh: 他为你描绘了一幅人类斗棍历史的全景，还加入了一些意想不到的流行文化元素来调剂，而你却感觉昏昏欲睡。

En: It's an anthropological heritage of the Lomanthang people, a martial art of sorts, but what not a lot of books mention is that it also carries a *cultural* significance among the Lomanthangs, as it used to be the best means of showing off to look for a bride, which...
Zh: “它是罗马丹族群的人类学遗产，是一种武术，但是只有很少书中有提到：它同时承载着罗马丹人的一种*文化*意义——在过去寻找新娘时，它是一种最佳的炫耀方式…”

En: ... interestingly enough, brings us to the socio-economic structure of the traditional rural tribes of the Lo Manthang isles, which... He goes on to give you a detailed overview of their way of life, the amiable, slightly nervous smile not once leaving his face.
Zh: “……这一点本身已经很有意思了，能让我们看出罗马丹岛传统乡村部落的社会经济结构…”他继续为你详细概述了他们生活方式，脸上一直挂着亲切又略带些紧张的微笑。

En: I couldn't have said it better. This man is a fountain of knowledge!
Zh: 他已经说的很好了。这个男人就是知识的源泉！

En: I'm not really interested in the practice, I just want to know how often you work out. Now and then -- that's what, like once a week?
Zh: “其实我对实践真的没多大兴趣，我只想知道你锻炼的频率。你说偶尔——那大概是，一周一次吗？”

En: Lo Manthang stick fighting is a little like a pyrholidon addiction, he explains with a little smile. "I've been practising it for nearly 20 years now, so you could say that my doses have grown a little... peculiar."
Zh: “罗马丹斗棍和吡嚯浣酮一样，会上瘾的。”他微笑着解释到，“我现在大概已经练了快20年，所以可以说我的剂量有点…特别。”

En: So what is it, twice a week then? Every other day? *Man* is it difficult to stay on the right topic with you.
Zh: “那是多少，一周两次吗？每隔一天？*伙计*，跟你说话保持一个步调有那么难吗？”

En: That's right, Mikael. Trant grabs his kid into a single-handed embrace, looking slightly embarrassed. "It also has a meditative quality, helps to clear my head... But anyway," he says, swaying with his hand.
Zh: “没错，米克尔。”特兰特一手抱起自己的孩子，看起来略微有些尴尬。“它还有一种禅意，能帮我清空大脑…但不管怎么说，”他一边说，一边挥着手。

En: And, as you know, they have this very distinctive dress uniform with scarlet breeches and little cylindrical fur caps, I think these are called... You daze off, as Trant tells you about the peculiarities of mounted police forces in central Occident.
Zh: “而且，你也知道，他们的军礼服非常与众不同，深红色马裤，还有圆柱形的小皮帽，我想名字好像是…”你有些困倦，而特兰特还在喋喋不休地讲述着奥西登中央骑警部队的独特性。

En: But anyway, he says after a while with a charming, apologetic smile, "I am boring you with details again. You were saying?"
Zh: “但不管怎么说，”过了一会儿，他露出一个迷人又歉意的微笑，“跟你讲这些细节，你一定觉得挺无聊的吧。不好意思，你刚才说什么了？”

En: Can you teach me? Do you have the sticks with you?
Zh: “你能教我吗？你身上带着棍子吗？”

En: Something tells me I'm not going to climb the ladder with Lo Manthang sticks.
Zh: “有什么东西告诉我，不要用罗马丹棍去爬那个梯子。”

En: No-no, he says, immediately backing off, "I'm just speculating *theoretically* here... Of course, there's also the method ancient Iilmaraans used to get into the huts of their enemies."
Zh: “不，不，”他立即后退一步，说到，“我只是做些*理论性*的推测…当然了，古代伊尔玛人有一种办法闯入敌人小屋的方式。”

En: Then there's also the method of climbing that the ancient Iilmaraans used to get into the huts of their enemies.
Zh: “还有一种攀登的方法，古代伊尔玛人曾经用这种方法爬进敌人的小屋。”

En: Wicked cool, can you tell me about this method?
Zh: “听起来莫名带感，你能教我这种方法吗？”

En: Yes, but we're not really dealing with *huts* here, are we? (Look at the building.)
Zh: “没错，不过我们这里对付的可不是*小屋*，不是吗？”（看着那栋建筑。）

En: If I'm not mistaken then it involved months of following a very rigid exercise regimen. No, *years*...
Zh: “如果我没弄错的话，那可能需要好几个月的严格训练。不，是*好多年*…”

En: But I don't have years to spare.
Zh: “但我抽不出几年时间啊。”

En: From an evolutionary point of view you could see this building as a logical conclusion to the more traditional *hut*.
Zh: “从进化的角度来看，你可以把这栋建筑看作是基于传统*小屋*合理演化的结果。”

En: No, not really.
Zh: “不，不见得。”

En: In this case I suggest looking into the Seolite *invisibles* class. The word refers to their remarkable ability to traverse any environment in the most efficient way possible. There's this excellent investigative piece of journalism by...
Zh: “如果是这样的话，我建议研究一下西奥的*隐形*课程。这是说他们有一种非凡的能力，可以用最有效的方式隐匿到任何环境中去。有一个非常棒的调查新闻，作者是…”

En: You know what, forget about it.
Zh: “你知道吗，还是算了吧。”

En: I don't have time to read any papers. Don't you have any *practical* advice?
Zh: “我没时间看什么报纸。你就不能提供些*实际点的*建议吗？”

En: Actually no, I should not forget it, I should look into it myself, there are some really interesting techniques... He stops, as Trant Heidelstam junior jerks his coat hem with an excited, almost silly look on his face.
Zh: “实际上呢，不行，我不应该忘记的，我要自己研究一下，有那么多有意思的技术……”小特兰特·海德斯塔姆猛地拉了一下他的衣角，脸上露出兴奋、甚至有些傻傻的表情。
