En: Yes, I have! His face lights up with excitement, bringing out the subtle laugh lines in the corner of his eyes. "There was this one time where I..."
Zh: “是的，我有！”他的脸因为兴奋整个亮了起来，眼角也带出了一丝笑纹。“有一次，我在…”

En: No, that doesn't sound like a very reasonable idea, replies Trant, narrowing his eyes, "physics, remember? But anyway..."
Zh: “不，听起来不太可行，”特兰特眯着眼睛回答到，“身体状况，还记得吗？但不管怎么说…”

En: Oh no. You've made *el bigo mistako*. He's going to go on forever!
Zh: 哦不。你犯*史前最大的错误*。他会永远这样絮叨个没完了！

En: Mark my words, all of this somehow ties to the history of Occidential financial markets.
Zh: 记住我的话，所有这些都与西方经济市场的历史有着某种关联。

En: Tape computers. He nods, wind tousling his suit jacket.
Zh: “磁带电脑。”他点点头，风吹乱了他的西装外套。

En: Yes, they even built a pleasure wheel, but that got destroyed in the war.
Zh: “没错，他们甚至建了个摩天轮，不过在战争中毁坏了。”

En: Yes. To lure in their star engineers. This part of Martinaise was nothing but reeds before Feld arrived. They had to make the prospect of living here *attractive*. It was supposed to become a global centre for innovation in cybernetics -- but history had other plans.
Zh: “是的。为了吸引他们的明星工程师。在菲尔德公司到来之前，马丁内斯这片区域除了芦苇丛什么也没有。他们必须让这里的前景变得*有吸引力*。本来这里应该变成神经机械学的全球创新中心——不过历史另有其他计划。”

En: Man, he's good at speaking. People must love him.
Zh: 天啦，他可真能说的。大家肯定都很爱他。

En: I want to hear about the Feld building again...
Zh: “我想再听听菲尔德大厦的事…”

En: Sure, what's on your mind?
Zh: “当然可以，你想问什么？”

En: They used them for military communications, he says, while his son looks up, idly chewing on the corner of his würm-themed colouring book. "But also to write and send out press releases. The most notorious example being Le Décret de Mars."
Zh: “他们把这些设备用在军事通信上，”他回答道。他的儿子抬起头，无聊地啃着以蠕虫为主题的填色簿的一角。“同时也用来写作和发送新闻稿。最臭名昭著的例子就是*三月法令*。”

En: What's the March decree? I mean the radio transmission sent out to news agencies and world governments by the newly-created Commune of Revachol on the 7th of March in the year '02.
Zh: “三月法令是什么？我是指02年3月7日，新创建的瑞瓦肖公社发送给通讯社和世界政府的无线电广播。”

En: What was that?
Zh: “那是什么？”

En: I tried to get Mikael to memorise it. He looks at his son, who starts giggling, his face hidden behind the book. "*Tried to*. Someone was much too interested in würms to be paying any attention."
Zh: “我试过让米克尔记住它。”他看着自己的儿子，米克尔把脸藏在书的后面，咯咯地笑了起来。“*试过*。看来有人对*蠕虫*太感兴趣，根本没有注意听。”

En: It's a beautiful piece of text, actually. A singer-songwriter I know -- Charette -- called it a love poem to Revachol on her political concept album 'Bons baisers d'Insulinde'. You should read it. Every local library in Revachol stocks a copy of the decree.
Zh: “其实，那是一篇很美的文本。我认识的一个创作歌手——夏洛特——在自己的政治概念专辑*群岛蜜吻*中，称之为献给瑞瓦肖的情诗。你真的应该读一下。瑞瓦肖的每座地方图书馆都保存着这份法令。”

En: *Very* important. Mikael, say hi to the officer. He rests his hand on the boy's shoulder. The child stays hidden behind the hem of his father's coat, clutching to his würm-themed colouring book.
Zh: “*非常*重要。米克尔，跟警官问个好。”他把手放在男孩的肩膀上。男孩一直躲在父亲外套的背后，紧紧握着那本蠕虫主题的填色书。

En: Mikael's a little tired today. We spent all night trying to run Orbis on his radiocomputer. Have you heard of it? It's a programming language used in Graad. Quite tricky, but he wanted to play this Graad-made adventure programme. We've been getting *really* into würms lately...
Zh: “米克尔今天有点累了。我们整晚都尝试在他的无线电脑上运行奥比斯。你听说过吗？那是在格拉德使用的一种编程语言。有些不好上手，不过他想玩格拉德出品的这个冒险程序。我们最近*真的*特别沉迷蠕虫…”

En: But I assume you're not here for giant würms when there are so many real things to see. Just as I was telling Mikael before -- this is where the Coalition landed in '08. We could be standing on what is the most interesting landmark in Revachol West. He points to the building again.
Zh: “但是我猜你不是为大蠕虫来的吧，特别是这里有那么多真实的东西可以看的时候。就像我之前告诉米克尔的——08年联盟就是在这里登陆的。我们此刻可能就站在西瑞瓦肖最有趣的一个地标上。”他再次指向那栋建筑。

En: Wow, get a load of this guy. He *really* enjoys his trivia. The Orbis programming language was named after its inventor, Viktor Orbis, a cybernetician from Graad. They run Vox in the Occidental countries...
Zh: 哇哦，看看这家伙。他*真的*很享受自己那点冷知识。编程语言奥比斯是以它的发明者维克托·奥比斯命名的，他是一位来自格拉德的神经机械学专家。奥西登国家运行的是沃克斯…

En: You know this is not what I meant.
Zh: “你知道这不是我的意思。”

En: Wait, what was that about the windows before?
Zh: “等等，你刚才说那些窗户是怎么回事？”

En: You and Kim know each other? (Proceed.)
Zh: “你和金认识吗？”（继续。）

En: Oh yes. So, Mikael, they had to deal with monitor glare, especially in the summer. They still had vector monitors back then. That was 49 years ago. So they didn't have windows on the south wall.
Zh: “哦，是的。所以，米克尔，他们必须处理显示器反光的问题，特别是夏天。那时候用的还是矢量显示器呢，都是49年前的事了。这就是南边没有窗户的原因。”

En: No, I can't say that we've met before. But I've *heard* of Kim, of course. Mikael, say hi to the officers. He rests his hand on the boy's shoulder. The child stays hidden behind the hem of his father's coat, clutching to his würm-themed colouring book.
Zh: “不，不能说我们之前见过。不过我*听说*过金，没错。米克尔，跟警官们打个招呼。”他把手放在男孩的肩膀上。男孩一直躲在父亲外套的背后，紧紧握着那本蠕虫主题的填色书。

En: Hold on. Hyper...text?
Zh: “等一下。超…文本？”

En: Yes, hypertext, he says, as if it's self-evident. "Jan Kaarp and the collection of cultural hyperlinks."
Zh: “没错，超文本，”他的口气好像这根本不言自明一样。“扬·卡普和文化超链接集合。”

En: He's just making up fancy words. This doesn't mean anything!
Zh: 他只是在编造一些华丽的词藻。其实根本没有任何含义！

En: Hold on. Hyper...text?
Zh: “等一下。超…文本？”

En: Yes, hypertext, he says, as if it's self-evident. "Jan Kaarp and the collection of cultural hyperlinks."
Zh: “没错，超文本，”他的口气好像这根本不言自明一样。“简·卡普和文化超链接系列。”

En: He's just making up fancy words. This doesn't mean anything!
Zh: 他只是在编造一些华丽的词藻。其实根本没有任何含义！

En: Wait, what was that about the windows before?
Zh: “等等，你刚才说那些窗户是怎么回事？”

En: Oh yes. So, Mikael, they had to deal with monitor glare, especially in the summer. They still had vector monitors back then. That was 49 years ago. So they didn't have windows on the south wall.
Zh: “哦，没错。所以，米克尔，他们必须处理显示器反光的问题，特别是夏天。那时候还用的是矢量显示器呢，都是49年前的事了。这就是南边没有窗户的原因。”

En: Alright, you were talking about the building, go on. (Continue.)
Zh: “好吧，你在聊那栋建筑，继续吧。”（继续。）

En: Let's talk about this empty building again.
Zh: “我们再聊聊那栋空建筑吧。”

En: What's so fascinating about an empty old building?
Zh: “那栋空无一人的旧建筑到底有什么吸引人的地方？”

En: Have you been talking to other police officers?
Zh: “你最近跟其他警官说过话吗？”

En: Do you have any advice on how to climb that ladder? (Point to the one next to the mural.)
Zh: “如果我们想爬上那个梯子，你有什么建议吗？”（指着壁画旁边的梯子。）

En: Great, thank you for all the interesting information. [Leave.]
Zh: “太棒了，谢谢你提供的这些有趣信息。”[离开。]

En: Hello again! He nods to you, smiling.
Zh: “又见面了！”他微笑着朝你点点头。

En: ... and, Mikael, notice the windows? Especially with how there are no windows on the south side? This was to deal with... A blond man stands next to his son, pointing to the weather-worn ruins. He sees you approaching and smiles...
Zh: “…还有，米克尔，注意到窗户了吗？特别是南边完全没有窗户？这是为了…”一个金发男人站在他的儿子身边，指着被风雨侵蚀的废墟。他看着慢慢靠近的你，笑了笑…

En: You're saying that Feld Electrical built this boardwalk? (Look under your feet.)
Zh: “你说是菲尔德电气造了这个木板道？”（看着自己的脚下。）

En: What happened to the engineers, the company people?
Zh: “工程师还有公司员工怎么样了？”

En: What did the revolutionaries do with those advanced tape computers?
Zh: “那些革命者对那些先进的磁带电脑做了什么？”

En: I wanted to ask something else...
Zh: “我想问点别的…”

En: Oh, I'm afraid it didn't end well for the boys. He smiles again, as if he's somehow personally responsible for this bleak turn of events. "But this story is a bit too *dark* for little Mikael here. Now if you were to ask about *tape computers*..."
Zh: “哦，恐怕那些人的结果不怎么样。”他又笑了笑，就好像他需要为那些惨无人道的事情负责一样。“不过这个故事对于我的小米克尔来说，有些太*黑暗*了。不如你问问我*磁带电脑*的事……”

En: Actually, no one knows. No one even knows what a computer made entirely of tape would look like! But word has it they were *very elegant* -- exquisite, alien-looking turn-of-the-century hardware... He raises his finger, remembering something.
Zh: “事实上，没人知道。根本没人知道完全由磁带组成的电脑长什么样！但是传闻说它们*非常优雅*——精致，有着怪异的外观和超越世代的硬件…”他竖起手指，记起了什么。

En: Me? Yes, at some point, I'm sure I have. There was this one rather, uh, he stops, smiling at the reminiscence, "*memorable instance* when I had a chance to meet the head of Dominion Police of Königstein."
Zh: “我？是的，在某一时刻，我肯定是有的。有一个特别，呃，“他停了下来，微笑回忆着，“*难忘的例子*，当时我有机会见到柯尼希斯自治领的警察局长。”

En: How to climb that ladder, you say? He turns around, eyeing the bleak metal bars next to the giant mural. "I do know that the Lomanthangs used *sticks* to climb battlements, there's even a special technique for that."
Zh: “你说怎么爬上那个梯子？”他转过身，看着巨型壁画旁那些黯淡的金属杆。“我确实知道罗马丹人会用*棍子*爬上城垛，甚至还有门特殊技术呢。”

En: No, thanks to *you* for having me and little Mikael here to pick your brain... A very interesting conversation indeed.
Zh: “不，要谢谢*你*，能让我和小米克尔在这里向你请教…这的确是一次非常有趣的对话。”

En: Hello! Isn't this a fine morning? He nods to you, smiling.
Zh: “你好啊！真是个愉快的清晨，不是吗？”他笑着对你点点头。

En: Good evening! He nods to you, smiling.
Zh: “晚上好！”他笑着对你点点头。

En: A tragedy... The lieutenant looks in the can, eyes watering from the smell.
Zh: “一场悲剧…”警督看着罐子，眼睛被味道熏得有些湿润。

En: Now, this makes partying look *bad*. Still -- don't let it sway you. *You're* responsible.
Zh: 这就让派对看起来就有点*糟糕*了。不过——不要让它影响到你。*你*是很负责的。

En: He shakes his head with genuine sadness.
Zh: 他摇摇头，流露出真实的悲伤。

En: It's no older than a day, or two. No mould yet.
Zh: 不超过一天，或者两天。还没有长霉。

En: It's hard to concentrate in the smell. The sea air brings some relief.
Zh: 在这种味道下，你很难集中精神。海风带来了一些安慰。

En: There's some tare, an empty cigarette package, and a crumpled kebab wrapper in the trash bin.
Zh: 垃圾箱里有一些回收品，一个空香烟盒，还有一张皱巴巴的烤肉包装纸。

En: Examine the tare.
Zh: 检查回收品。

En: Examine the cigarette package.
Zh: 检查香烟盒。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Examine the kebab wrapper.
Zh: 检查烤肉包装纸。

En: You see traces of mayonnaise and ketchup on it, as well as a tomato wedge. The wrapper reads: 'SHISH KEBAB REVACHOL'.
Zh: 上面还残留着蛋黄酱和番茄酱的痕迹，还有一点发霉的番茄角。包装纸上写着：“瑞瓦肖烤肉。”

En: Whoever tossed it here was a heavy smoker: the brand name reads 'Red Astra'.
Zh: 不管是谁把它丢在这里的，一定是个老烟枪：这个牌子的名字是“红阿斯特拉”。

En: Two empty bottles of 'Touloula' vodka and a can of black 'Potent Porter' is all you find.
Zh: 你只找到两支空的‘陶尔洛拉’伏特加酒瓶，还有一罐‘高度波特’啤酒。

En: No. There's more in there. 'Livis' strawberry liquor, plus some pilsner bottles too. Better not pick them up, they seem unhygienic.
Zh: 不。还不止这些。“利维斯”草莓酒，还有些比尔森啤酒。最好不要捡起来，它们似乎不太卫生。

En: 'Red Astra' is the black market version of 'Astra' cigarettes, known for their high tar content.
Zh: “红阿斯特拉”是黑市版的“阿斯特拉”香烟，因为高焦油含量而闻名。

En: Looks like he's been dead for a while. I'm surprised no one found him before you came along. He stands up and shivers as a gust of wind blows through his jacket.
Looks like he's been dead for a while. I'm surprised no one found him before you came along. He stands up and shivers as a gust of wind blows through his bomber jacket.
The smell is... not as bad as a two-week-old corpse, but it's definitely heading there.
Zh: 这种味道…没有一具放了两周的尸体那么糟糕，不过肯定也是朝着那个方向去的。

En: Half of his body has slipped between the cracked boardwalk, starting with the right leg. The fall has left him broken, contorted like a sad puppet.
Zh: 他的半边身体从裂开的木板道里滑了下去，从左腿开始的。这一摔把他给摔坏了，像个悲伤的玩偶一样扭曲着。

En: A bad fall. It might have been dark outside? This place is a mine field in the dark.
Zh: 摔得很严重。可能是因为外面很黑？这地方在黑暗中就是个雷区。

En: Be very, very careful where you step here.
Zh: 你走在这里的时候，一定要非常、非常小心。

En: He was *just* about to head home. The first step back home proved to be his last.
Zh: 他*正打算*回家。回家的第一步反而变成了他的最后一步。

En: True. It feels... disrespectful.
Zh: 这是真的。感觉…有些无礼。

En: Examine the chewing gum wrapper.
Zh: 检查口香糖包装纸。

En: 'Rubowski' spearmint chewing gum. Green leaves on the cover. The man's mouth is half-agape from the terror of the fall...
Zh: ‘洛博维斯’留兰香口香糖。包装纸上有绿色的叶子。男人的嘴因为坠落的恐惧半张着…

En: He ate the whole pack, right? It's to cover the smell of alcohol before going home. The worst thing is...
Zh: “他吃了一整包，对吗？这是为了在回家之前盖住嘴里的酒味。最糟糕的事情是…”

En: The lieutenant shudders from the cold. "I've seen it before: almost the same scenario. Even the chewing gum. It's always the same..."
Zh: 男人因为寒冷而颤抖。“我之前也见过：几乎是一模一样的场面。就连口香糖都是。总是这么回事…”

En: Look in.
Zh: 往里面看。

En: The blackness of death. Stench. You think you see white chewing gum too.
Zh: 死亡的黑色。恶臭。你觉得自己见过这种白色口香糖。

En: Confirmed. Nearly the whole pack is there, solidified on his lower rear teeth.
Zh: 确认完毕。几乎一整包都塞进去了，就凝固在下排牙齿后面。

En: Back off.
Zh: 退后。

En: Careful. The wind blows hard, from the east.
Zh: 小心。东边吹来的风很大。

En: In a ditch off a road below the 8/81, he thinks. A young father. Then he shakes his head, to make the memory stop.
Zh: 在8/81下方公路的一条沟渠里，他想到。一个年轻的父亲。然后他摇摇头，让记忆中止。

En: The library card! He was supposed to return a book.
Zh: “借书证！他是打算去还书的。”

En: I can't see a book... The lieutenant pats his pockets. "So he took the book to the library, then came back and thought -- I'll have one."
Zh: “我没看见书…”他拍了拍他的口袋。“所以他把书送到了图书馆，然后走回来，想到——我要去喝一杯。”

En: I'll have *one* drink before I get home.
Zh: 在我回家之前，我要去*喝一杯*。

En: Just one drink. I have my cool jacket on. I took the book to the library. I've earned it.
Zh: 只要一杯。我穿着自己的酷夹克。我把书送去了图书馆。这是我应得的。

En: Damn it... He's definitely *someone's* husband. The lieutenant points at the golden ring on the man's left hand, the flesh around it swollen and pale.
Zh: “该死…他肯定是*某人*的丈夫。”警督指着男人左手上的戒指，那周围的肉有些浮肿和苍白。

En: The view from the lookout is beautiful...
Zh: 从瞭望台看去，风景十分优美…

En: Agreed. If this somehow converges later, why not. But keep it simple for now.
Zh: 同意。如果之后能收敛一点的话，为什么不呢。不过现在还是简单一点。

En: Sometimes a kebab is just a kebab.
Zh: 有时候，烤肉就是烤肉而已。

En: I'd say we should just write down 'head trauma' and leave it at that. Let's keep this detour as short as possible -- the sooner we get back to finding Ruby the better.
Zh: “要我说，我们应该在验尸报告里写下‘头部创伤’，然后就到此为止。尽量不要绕太大的弯子——我们越快回去找露比就越好。”

En: Calm now. Carefully. Just another day, just another dead body. Breathe.
Zh: 现在，冷静下来。又是寻常的一天，又是一具尸体而已。深呼吸。

En: I don't see any other major wounds. Do you?
Zh: “我没有看见其他大伤口。你呢？”

En: It's hard to say.
Zh: “很难说。”

En: No, just this one at the back of his head.
Zh: “不，只有后脑勺有个伤口。”

En: Seems like the head wound was fatal. It's exactly the shape of the bench...
Zh: “看来头部那个伤口就是造成死亡的原因。它跟长凳的形状是完全吻合的……”

En: Tare, all around us... He looks at two other bottles forgotten near the coin-operated viewer.
Zh: “我们周围都是回收品…”他看着投币望远镜附近遗留的另外两只瓶子。

En: Tare, all around us... He looks at two other bottles near the coin-operated viewer, then at your yellow plastic bag.
Zh: “我们周围都是回收品…”他看着投币望远镜附近遗留的另外两只瓶子，然后看向你的黄色塑料袋。

En: I'd prefer if you didn't collect them this time. It's not... proper.
Zh: “我宁愿这次你别把它们捡走。这样不太…合适。”

En: The next thing to do is call the station from my Kineema and hand over the case. Then we can return to the murder investigation and be done with it.
Zh: “接下来，就是回我的锐影打给警局，把案子转交出去。然后我们就能继续调查凶杀案，了结这件事。”

En: Alright. The station is going to want details, however. We already examined the library card we found on the body -- good, that should be enough for the first lead.
Zh: “好吧。不过警局可能需要我们提供细节。我们已经检查过尸体上发现的借书证——这样挺好的，应该足够作为第一条线索了。”

En: Alright. We've already examined the library card -- let's call the Jamrock Library from my Kineema, see if we can learn anything about this Billie Méjean.
Zh: “好吧。我们已经检查过借书证——接着，返回锐影那边，打电话给加姆洛克图书馆，看看能不能获得比莉·梅让的信息。”

En: Alright. We should first examine that library card you found. Then we can call the station from my Kineema, let them know we're taking the case.
Zh: “好吧。首先，我们应该检查一下你发现的那张借书证。然后回到我的锐影，打电话给警局，通知他们这个案子归我们了。”

En: And while we're there we should also call the station, let them know that we're taking the case.
Zh: “我们过去之后应该打给警局，通知他们这个案子归我们了。”

En: Hold on... The lieutenant leans over the corpse and examines his face. Two glassy eyes stare back at him, void of any signs of life.
Zh: “等等…”警督在尸体旁边蹲下，检查他的面部。两只玻璃般的眼睛回望着他，完全没有任何生命迹象。

En: Looks like he's been dead for a while. I'm surprised no one found him before you came along. He stands up and shivers as a gust of wind blows through his bomber jacket.
Zh: “看来他已经死了有一会儿了。很奇怪，居然没人发现他。”他站起身，一阵狂风吹过他的短夹克，他打了个寒颤。

En: The woman you met at the bookstand? He looks at the bundle of flesh and rags lying on the ground. "Why do you think it's her husband?"
Zh: “你在书摊边遇到的那个女人？”他看着地上那具尸体。“你为什么会觉得是她的丈夫？”

En: The leather jacket. (Point at the man's clothes.) "It matches her description perfectly."
Zh: “那件皮夹克。”（指向男人的衣服。）“完全符合她的描述。”

En: This is the most working class way to go -- completely alienated and forgotten.
Zh: “这是最适合工人阶级的死亡方式——完全被隔绝和遗忘。”

En: Because it's the most working class death you've ever seen.
Zh: 因为这是你见过的最工人阶级的死亡方式。

En: Bottles, all around him. (Point at the tare.) "She said he was drinking somewhere in Martinaise."
Zh: “瓶子，他周围到处都是。”（指着回收品。）“她说他在马丁内斯的某个地方喝酒。”

En: Yes, yes, the lieutenant seems impatient, "but what *actual* facts do you have that connect him with this woman you met at the bookstand?"
Zh: “是啊，是啊，”警督有些不耐烦，“但你是基于什么*实际*因素把他和书摊前遇到的那个女人联系在一起的呢？”

En: The bright blue lining? He nods, then points at the ring on the man's left hand: "Well, he's definitely *someone's* husband."
Zh: “亮蓝色的内衬？”他点点头，然后指着男人左手上的戒指：“呃，他确实是*某人*的丈夫。”

En: To hide the stench of alcohol no doubt.
Zh: 毫无疑问，是为了隐藏酒精的臭味。

En: When damp these boards are really slippery, even a sober man could loose their balance here.
Zh: 这些木板潮湿的时候会变得非常滑，就连清醒的人都可能会失去平衡。

En: That's what the chewing gum seems to point to as well.
Zh: 口香糖好像也指向了相同的结论。

En: We should take another look at the library card you found on him, the lieutenant says, straightening his back.
Zh: “我们应该再看看你在他身上发现的借书证，”警督挺直了后背，说到。

En: Only some sunflower seeds remain in his soaked pockets. You already took the library card.
Zh: 他湿透的口袋里只有一些葵瓜子。你已经把借书证拿走了。

En: A man lies on the boardwalk, his limbs bent and neck turned at an unnatural angle. Right next to him is an empty bottle of spirits. In his cramped hand: a chewing gum wrapper.
Zh: 一个男人躺在木板道上，四肢弯曲，颈部扭转到一个不自然的角度。他旁边是一个烈酒空瓶。在他攥紧的拳头里：有一张口香糖包装纸。

En: Hold on... The lieutenant squats next to the corpse and examines his face. Two bulging eyes stare back at him, void of any signs of life.
Zh: “等等…”警督在尸体旁边蹲下，查看他的脸。两只突出的眼睛回望着他，完全没有任何生命迹象。

En: Lividity is faintly pronounced. Whoever this is, he's been dead for two days, no longer. He stands up and shivers as a gust of wind blows through his bomber jacket. "We need to investigate."
Zh: “铁青色隐约可见。不管是怎么回事，他大概死了不超过两天。”他站起身，一阵狂风吹过他的短夹克，他打了个寒颤。“我们需要调查一下。”

En: Study the man's clothes.
Zh: 查看男人的衣服。

En: Study the man himself.
Zh: 查看死去的男人。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: What do you think happened here?
Zh: “你觉得这里发生什么事了？”

En: He's wearing mud-caked boots, beige trousers and an old brown leather jacket with a bright blue lining. There are traces of kebab sauce on his chest.
Zh: 他穿着泥泞的靴子，米黄色裤子，还有一件带亮蓝色内衬的旧皮夹克。他的胸口还残留着烤肉酱的痕迹。

En: The man has fallen through a crack in the boardwalk and hit his head against the metal bench. Coagulated blood covers his black hair. One of his feet is still dangling through the hole.
Zh: 男人踩中了木板道的缝隙，脑袋撞到金属长凳。他脑后的头发上覆盖着血液凝块。其中一只脚还挂在洞里。

En: What should we do with him?
Zh: “我们拿他怎么办？”

En: The leather jacket suits him well. It must be custom-made.
Zh: 他的皮夹克很合身。肯定是专门定制的。

En: Search his pockets.
Zh: 翻找他的口袋。

En: You find some sunflower seeds and a rain-soaked library card, folded into two. His jacket feels sodden and heavy under your hand.
Zh: 你找到些葵瓜子，一张被雨水浸透的借书证，是对折起来的。他的夹克湿透了，在你手下显得格外沉重。

En: Death by misadventure. He slipped and fell through the boardwalk. A truly unfortunate accident... If it wouldn't have been for that bench, he'd be alive.
Zh: “意外身亡。他滑了一下，踩空了一脚。的确是一个不幸的事故…如果没有那个长凳的话，他说不定能活下来。”

En: Height: 170-175 cm, curly hair, stout build. Age: approximately 50-60 years.
Zh: 身高170-175厘米，卷发，身材肥胖。年龄大约50-60岁。

En: You have to be quite inebriated to fall that bad. Well over a litre of pure ethanol. Three bottles of wine or one and a half of spirits?
Zh: 一定是喝得伶仃大醉才会摔得那么厉害。一升纯酒精，三瓶葡萄酒，或者一瓶半烈酒？

En: Examine his face.
Zh: 检查他的脸。

En: Or maybe it was just dark.
Zh: 或者只是因为太黑了。

En: His expression is dull like the sea behind him, drops of water shining on his moustache. His eyes, empty and wide, look frightening in their frozen gaze.
Zh: 他的脸部就像背后的大海一样僵硬，水滴在他的胡子上闪闪发亮。他的眼睛，空洞地圆睁着，那种冷冰冰的眼神有些吓人。

En: From where I stand I can see two options. We either take the case and follow the leads to identify the body on our own -- or we report back to the station and leave this for our colleagues to handle.
Zh: “在我看来有两种选择。我们可以接下案子，顺着线索确认尸体身份——或者我们汇报给警局，把案子交给同事去处理。”

En: What should I do with the body?
Zh: 我应该怎么处理这具尸体？

En: First you need to talk with the lieutenant, then report back to the station. This is too complicated to handle on your own.
Zh: 首先，你需要跟警督聊聊，然后汇报给警局。这个情况太复杂，你一个人处理不了。

En: Do you think he was drunk? (Point at the bottle.)
Zh: “你觉得他喝醉了吗？”（指着酒瓶。）

En: Oh yes, the lieutenant nods.
Zh: “是的，”警督点点头。

En: I still need some time to decide how I want to solve this case. (Return to this later.)
Zh: “我还需要点时间来决定怎么处理这个案子。”（稍后再回来。）

En: Let's leave this case for the station, I don't want to spend time on it. (Don't take the case.)
Zh: “我们把案子留给警局吧，我不想在这件事上面浪费时间。”（不接案子。）

En: No need to rush.
Zh: “不用着急。”

En: Alright. The station is going to want details, however. Let's examine that library card you found on the man's body, see if it has any clues. Then we can call the station from my Kineema and hand over the case.
Zh: “好吧。不过警局可能会需要我们提供细节。先检查一下你在他身上找到的借书证，看看有没有什么线索。然后我们回到我的锐影那里联系警局，把案子移交出去。”

En: Who is this man?
Zh: “这人是谁？”

En: Looks like one of the locals -- he'd have to know this spot to come here. You don't *just* walk over here. He looks south, the way you came.
Zh: “看起来像是本地人——他肯定知道这个地方才会过来。你不会*就这么*走过来的。”他看向南边，你来的方向。

En: Could it be related to the lynching? Ruby?
Zh: “这可能跟私刑有所关联吗？露比？”

En: Could it be related to the lynching?
Zh: “这可能跟私刑有所关联吗？”

En: We know who this is. It's the working class woman's missing husband -- dead on the boardwalk.
Zh: “我知道这是谁。是那个女工人走失的丈夫——死在了木板道上。”

En: Looks like just another old drunkard to me.
Zh: “在我看来，又是一个老酒鬼。”

En: He looks like me. I could have ended up just like him -- dead on some empty boardwalk with a bottle next to my corpse.
Zh: “他看起来很像我。我最后也可能会落得他这种下场——死在一条空旷的木板道上，尸体旁边还摆着一只空酒瓶。”

En: But that's just a lazy assumption. What do you think?
Zh: “但这不过是个偷懒的假设。你怎么看？”

En: Well, he's definitely *someone's* husband. The lieutenant points at the golden ring on the man's left hand, the flesh around it swollen and pale.
Zh: “呃，他肯定是*某人*的丈夫。”警督指着男人左手上的戒指，那周围的肉有些浮肿和苍白。

En: No, I don't see anything that points in that direction. For now let's treat this case as a simple -- albeit sad -- accident, unrelated to the murder case.
Zh: “不，我没发现任何线索指往那个方向。我们暂时把这起案子当做一次简单的——尽管有些悲伤的——事故，跟凶杀案没有任何关联。”

En: But let's try to not run ahead -- for now all we know is that he's an unidentified middle-aged man found dead on the Martinaise boardwalk.
Zh: “不过，话还是别说的太早——以目前的情况来看，我们只知道他是个身份不明的中年男人，死在了马丁内斯的木板道上。”

En: I think labelling him as an alcoholic is a bit premature. We don't know anything yet.
Zh: “我觉得给他贴上酒鬼的标签还有些为时过早。我们什么都还不知道呢。”

En: We do know that he was married. The lieutenant points at the ring on the man's left hand, the flesh around it swollen and grey.
Zh: “我们知道他已经结婚了。”警督指着男人左手上的戒指，那周围的肉有些浮肿、发灰。

En: A dead working class man with a bottle in his hand? Don't deceive yourself, you know who this is.
Zh: 一个死去的男工人，手里还握着一只空瓶？别骗自己了，你知道他是谁。

En: But you're right, let's not run ahead. For now all we know is that he's an unidentified middle-aged man found dead on the Martinaise boardwalk.
Zh: “不过你说对，我们的想法还是别太超前。现在我们只知道他是一个身份不明的中年男子，死在了马丁内斯的木板道上。”

En: And he was married. The lieutenant points at the ring on the man's left hand, the flesh around it swollen and grey.
Zh: “而且他已婚。”警督指着男人左手上的戒指，那周围的肉有些浮肿、发灰。

En: Well, at least you're not married. The lieutenant points at the ring on the man's left hand, the flesh around it swollen and grey.
Zh: “呃。至少你没结婚。”警督指着男人左手上的戒指，那周围的肉有些浮肿、发灰。

En: Or what if you are...?
Zh: 如果你结了呢…？

En: Married to your drink, that's for sure.
Zh: 跟酒精结婚了，这是肯定的。

En: A cool leather jacket with a bolt of blue? Oh no, this sounds terribly familiar...
Zh: 蓝色内衬的酷皮夹克？哦不，这个听起来熟悉地可怕……

En: This is an omen, a sign from above -- that you need to stop drinking.
Zh: 这是一个预兆，是上天传达的一个信号——你不能再喝酒了。

En: What we're witnessing here is a demise of a great party beast. That leather jacket, an empty vodka bottle... (Kiss your fingers.)
Zh: “我们在这里见证了一个伟大派对动物的终结。那件皮夹克，一个空伏特加酒瓶…”（亲吻你的手指。）

En: At least this man knew how to party! Imagine the same scene without the bottle -- now that would be just sad.
Zh: 至少这个男人知道怎么狂欢！想象一下同样的场景，但是没有那个酒瓶——那就真的只剩下悲哀了。

En: This is an omen, a sign from above -- don't start drinking again.
Zh: 这是一个预兆，是上天传达的一个信号——别再喝酒了。

En: The wedding ring. The lieutenant points at the ring on the man's left hand, the flesh around it swollen and grey.
Zh: “结婚戒指。”警督指着男人左手上的戒指，那周围的肉有些浮肿、发灰。

En: Study the surroundings.
Zh: 查看周围的环境。

En: Hold on -- what about field autopsy?
Zh: “等等——那现场验尸呢？”

En: What about the kebab?
Zh: “那烤肉酱呢？”

En: What about it? The deceased ate some kebab. He shrugs. "It's probably from a nearby place, maybe in the Pox..."
Zh: “怎么了？死者吃过烤肉。”他耸耸肩。“可能就在附近的某个地方，也许在帕克斯…”

En: Someone should be held responsible for this broken boardwalk. It's dangerous.
Zh: “应该有人为这条破木板道负责。实在是太危险了。”

En: They'll seal this place off after the news reaches the Coalition officials. I doubt that they have enough resources to actually repair the boardwalk.
Zh: “消息传到联盟官员那里之后，他们会把这里封闭起来。我怀疑他们的资源够不够，能不能真的修好这条木板道。”

En: Not that sealing it off would keep anyone away. All it does is keep the city council's hands clean. He smiles sourly.
Zh: “封起来并不代表别人不会靠近。这样唯一能做的，就是不会把市政府的手弄脏。”他苦涩地笑了。

En: Right. (Conclude.)
Zh: “好吧。”（总结）

En: It does seem to be a pretty straightforward misadventure, although there's still the question of identifying the body...
Zh: “这好像是个特别明确的意外事故，不过还有确认尸体身份的问题…”

En: Yes, but what if there's a killer on the loose? Two suspicious deaths in such a short time frame!
Zh: 是的，但是如果还有个逍遥法外的杀人犯呢？在很短的时间内就接连发生了两起可疑的死亡案件！

En: We should consider the possibility that we're dealing with a sequence killer here.
Zh: “我们需要考虑连环杀人犯的可能性。”

En: You're right, connecting it with the lynching is a stretch.
Zh: “你说得对，把这案子跟私刑联系在一起有点夸张了。”

En: Someone could have pushed him from behind with an intention to kill.
Zh: “可能是带着杀人的意图某个人故意从背后推了他一把。”

En: It's possible, but let's take it step-by-step. We still haven't identified the man.
Zh: “有这个可能，不过还是一步步来吧。我们还没有确定这个人的身份。”

En: What if he *witnessed* something? All of this could be staged. We don't know the exact cause of death.
Zh: “如果他*目击*到什么了呢？一切都可能是有预谋的。我们还不知道准确的死因呢。”

En: A *sequence killer*? The lieutenant narrows his eyes in the wind. "There's nothing that connects those two bodies. This is a completely different case -- an accident."
Zh: “*连环杀人犯*？”警督站在风中，眯起双眼。“那两具尸体之间没有任何联系。这是个完全不同的案子——是一场意外。”

En: Without any further evidence -- yes.
Zh: “如果没有进一步证据的话——是这样没错。”

En: What if all of this is staged? He could have seen something that night in the yard...
Zh: 如果这一切都是有预谋的呢？那晚，他可能在院子里看到了什么…

En: I'm just saying that we should be open to all possibilities.
Zh: “我只是想说，我们应该对一切可能性抱持开放态度。”

En: Yes, but... I just wanted to make it clear it's not a sequence killer, okay?
Zh: “是的，但是…我只想说清楚，这不是什么连环杀人犯，好吗？”

En: There's some dried blood on the metal bench, right where the corpse's head rests. The floorboards are rotten and slippery wet around the hole. An empty bottle lies nearby. A chewing gum wrapper is clutched in his fist.
Zh: 金属长凳上有一些干涸的血迹，就在尸体头部停留的位置。地板有些腐烂，洞的周围又湿又滑。一只空酒瓶就躺在附近不远的地方。他的拳头里还紧紧攥着一张口香糖包装纸。

En: Examine the man's head.
Zh: 检查男人的头部。

En: A dried chunk of blood covers the hair at the back of his head -- an open wound. It's sticky and cold to your touch.
Zh: 他后脑的头发上有一个凝固的血块——是开放性创伤，触感黏腻又冰冷。

En: Step on the floorboards.
Zh: 踏上地板。

En: This is what killed him.
Zh: 就是这个东西杀了他。

En: This is where he came out of himself. Drop by drop when he was unconscious. It took three, maybe four minutes.
Zh: 这里就是他走向终结的地方。在他昏迷的时候，一点一滴。大概花了3分钟，也许是4分钟。

En: You see waves churning below... Something cracks beneath your feet.
Zh: 你看见海浪在下面翻腾…有什么东西在你脚下嘎吱直响。

En: The boards screech under the weight of the T-500 ceramic sabatons. It's a good thing they're so light, cause it's hard to determine whether it was the dead man's weight that caused the boardwalk to break... It definitely looks fragile.
Zh: 在T-500陶瓷护脚甲的重量下，木板发出尖锐刺耳的声音。还好它们很轻，很难说到底是不是死者的重量导致木板破碎的…确实看起来挺脆弱的。

En: They screech under your feet, ominously. It's hard to say whether the dead man's weight was what caused the boardwalk to break... It definitely looks fragile.
Zh: 它们在你脚下发出尖锐刺耳的声音，充满了不祥的感觉。很难说到底是不是死者的重量导致木板破碎的…它们确实看起来挺脆弱的。

En: He could have easily disappeared into the sea through that hole. And you would have never found him.
Zh: 通过那个洞，他很容易就能消失在大海里。这样你就永远也找不到他了。

En: Examine the bottle.
Zh: 检查瓶子。

En: A 0.75 litre 'Touloula' vodka with its cap missing. There's hardly anything left inside.
Zh: 一瓶0.75公升的‘陶尔洛拉’伏特加，瓶盖已经不见了。里面几乎没剩下什么东西。

En: Step back.
Zh: 后退一步。

En: The entire boardwalk creaks in the wind as you take a step back.
Zh: 你退后一步，整个木板道在风中嘎吱作响。

En: Another dead body. This is your job. Steel yourself.
Zh: 又是一具尸体。这是你的工作。做好准备。

En: It's mid-market spirits. With a slight touch of menthol. The man meant to enjoy himself, have a good time.
Zh: 这是中端市场的烈酒。带有一丝薄荷醇的味道。这个男人本想好好享受一下，度过一段美好时光。

En: He was confused when he died. Confused and alone, most likely. Overcome with the awful surprise of it all.
Zh: 他死的时候一定很困惑。很有可能是困惑而孤独的。他被这次恐怖的突袭压倒了。

En: What about alcohol poisoning and liver failure?
Zh: “那酒精中毒和肝脏衰竭呢？”

En: Some symptoms of acute alcohol poisoning could have definitely played a role here: severe confusion, respiratory depression, unpredictable behaviour...
Zh: “急性酒精中毒的病症确实可能跟他的死因有关：极度混乱，呼吸抑制，无法预测的行为…”

En: I'd say we should just write down 'head trauma' in the autopsy report and leave it at that. It would save us at least two hours of unnecessary work.
Zh: “要我说，我们应该在验尸报告里写下‘头部创伤’，然后就到此为止。这样至少可以节省两个小时不必要的工作。”

En: Yes, but... isn't that kind of sloppy?
Zh: “是啊，但是…这样是不是有点太草率了？”

En: You're right, I don't see anything criminal here. Let's leave it at that.
Zh: “你说得对，我没发现什么犯罪行为。这个话题就到此为止吧。”

En: Maybe, but we don't really have much time or resources to spare. The guys at Processing will take care of the rest.
Zh: “也许，不过我们真的没有时间，也没有那么多资源可以浪费。分析部门的人会处理好接下来的事情。”

En: A field autopsy isn't necessary if the cause of death doesn't appear to be criminal -- and this looks like a simple accident to me.
Zh: “如果死因不像是犯罪行为导致的话，那就没必要现场验尸——而且在我看来，这就是一起简单的事故。”

En: But I think that death arrived through head trauma, not liver failure.
Zh: “不过我觉得死因是头部创伤，不是肝脏衰竭。”

En: Good call. The guys at Processing can take care of the rest.
Zh: “说得对。负责分析的人会处理好接下来的事情。”

En: A man lies on the boardwalk, his limbs bent and neck turned at an unnatural angle. Right next to him is an empty bottle of spirits. In his cramped hand: a chewing gum wrapper.
Zh: 一个男人躺在木板道上，四肢弯曲，颈部扭转到一个不自然的角度。他旁边是一个烈酒空瓶。在他攥紧的拳头里：有一张口香糖包装纸。

En: Good, we should take a look at that library card after this is done.
Zh: “很好，等这边结束之后，我们应该看看那张借书证。”

En: We found him. We should finish this. (Take the case.)
Zh: “我们找到他的。就该由我们了结。”（接下案子。）
